  estimated_grp decimal(8,2) [note: 'The estimated gross rating point (GRP) delivery for this inventory, representing the total audience reach multiplied by frequency. GRP is a standard broadcast audience measurement metric.']
  estimated_impressions varchar [note: 'The projected number of advertising impressions (individual ad views) this inventory will deliver, based on historical audience data.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'The projected unique audience count (reach) that will be exposed to advertising in this inventory slot at least once.']
  estimated_trp decimal(8,2) [note: 'The estimated target rating point (TRP) delivery for this inventory, representing the rating within a specific demographic target audience. TRP is used for targeted advertising campaigns.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this inventory record is no longer valid or available for sale.']
  held_units varchar [note: 'The number of advertising units currently held or reserved pending final commitment, typically during proposal negotiation.']
  hut_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of homes using television during this inventory time period. HUT is a standard broadcast audience availability metric.']
  inventory_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this inventory slot, used in sales proposals and trafficking systems.']
  inventory_date date [note: 'The specific broadcast date for which this inventory is available.']
  inventory_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise end time of the inventory window, including time zone information.']
  inventory_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise start time of the inventory window, including time zone information.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the advertising inventory: available for sale, held pending commitment, sold and committed, preempted for higher-priority content, or blocked due to restrictions.']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the advertising inventory format: linear broadcast spot, video-on-demand (VOD) ad position, dynamic ad insertion (DAI) slot, free ad-supported streaming television (FAST) pod, or over-the-top (OTT) impression.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory record was last updated or modified.']
  makegood_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory is eligible to be used for makegoods (compensatory ad spots provided when original spots did not air as scheduled).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this inventory record.']
  preemptible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory can be preempted (bumped) for higher-priority programming or advertising. Preemptible inventory is typically sold at lower rates.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'The pricing tier classification for this inventory based on audience quality and demand: premium (highest value), standard, value, or remnant (lowest value, unsold inventory).']
  put_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of persons using television during this inventory time period. PUT is a standard broadcast audience availability metric.']
  rate_card_cpm decimal(12,2) [note: 'The published rate card cost per thousand impressions (CPM) for this inventory. CPM is the standard pricing metric in advertising sales.']
  rate_card_cprp decimal(12,2) [note: 'The published rate card cost per rating point (CPRP) for this inventory. CPRP is used in broadcast television pricing based on audience ratings.']
  remaining_avails varchar [note: 'The number of advertising units still available for sale, calculated as total available minus sold and held units.']
  sales_category varchar [note: 'The sales market category for this inventory: upfront (advance sales event), scatter (last-minute sales), local, national, digital, or programmatic.']
  sold_units varchar [note: 'The number of advertising units that have been sold and committed to advertisers.']
  total_available_units varchar [note: 'The total number of advertising units (spots or impressions) available for sale in this inventory record.']
  unit_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The standard duration in seconds for each advertising unit in this inventory (e.g., 15, 30, 60 seconds).']

  Note: 'Available advertising inventory record representing sellable ad time or impression capacity for a specific channel, daypart, program, and date window. Tracks total available units (spots or impressions), sold units, remaining avails, rate card CPM/CPRP, audience delivery estimate (GRP/TRP), HUT/PUT index, blackout restrictions, and inventory status (available, held, sold, preempted). Feeds the avails system for sales proposals and scatter market pricing.'
}

Table advertising.rate_card {
  rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising rate card. Primary key for the rate card entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Rate card pricing requires formal approval by revenue management or pricing strategy employee for governance and audit compliance. Replaces text \'approved_by\' field with proper FK for workforce integr']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broadcast channel or streaming platform to which this rate card applies.']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Rate cards are priced by Nielsen demographic cell (A18-49, W25-54, etc.) - fundamental to broadcast advertising economics. CPM/CPRP rates vary by demographic segment. Essential for proposal generation']
  ad_pod_position varchar [note: 'Position within the ad pod (group of ads in a commercial break) for which the rate applies. First and last positions typically command premium rates due to higher viewer attention.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rate card was formally approved for use in ad sales and order entry.']
  cpm_basis decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions or viewers that the rate is based on. CPM is a standard metric in advertising pricing used to normalize rates across different audience sizes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rate card record was first created in the system.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time segment of the broadcast day for which the rate applies. Dayparts are standard time blocks used in media planning to segment audience availability and pricing (e.g., prime time 8-11pm, daytime 10am-4pm). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime_time|late_fringe|late_night|overnight|weekend|sports|special_event — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  discount_eligibility varchar [note: 'Description of volume discounts, frequency discounts, or package deals that can be applied to this rate card for qualifying advertisers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rate card expires and is no longer valid for new ad orders. Nullable for open-ended rate cards.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rate card becomes active and available for use in ad order pricing and proposals.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or Designated Market Area (DMA) to which the rate card applies. Can be national, regional, or local market identifier.']
  gross_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Published rate for the ad spot before any discounts, commissions, or agency fees are applied. This is the list price used as the baseline for negotiations.']
  grp_value decimal(8,2) [note: 'Gross Rating Point value associated with the rate, representing the percentage of the target audience reached multiplied by frequency. Used for planning and buying broadcast advertising.']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Type of advertising inventory covered by the rate card. Linear refers to traditional scheduled broadcast, VOD to video on demand, SVOD to subscription video on demand, AVOD to ad-supported video on demand, TVOD to transactional video on demand, OTT to over-the-top streaming, FAST to free ad-supported streaming television, and simulcast to simultaneous multi-platform broadcast. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|vod|svod|avod|tvod|ott|fast|simulcast — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rate card record was last updated or modified.']
  makegood_policy varchar [note: 'Policy describing conditions under which makegoods (compensatory ad spots) will be provided if audience guarantees are not met or technical issues occur.']
  minimum_audience_guarantee varchar [note: 'Minimum guaranteed audience size (in thousands) for the daypart and demographic. If actual audience falls below this threshold, makegoods may be required.']
  net_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Rate after standard agency commission (typically 15%) and other standard deductions are applied. This is the effective rate paid by the advertiser.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, terms, conditions, or special instructions related to the rate card, such as blackout dates, special event pricing, or advertiser category restrictions.']
  preemption_priority varchar [note: 'Indicates whether spots purchased at this rate can be preempted (bumped) for higher-paying advertisers. Non-preemptible rates are premium, while preemptible rates offer discounts but risk being moved or cancelled.']
  program_genre varchar [note: 'Genre or category of programming for which the rate applies (e.g., News, Sports, Drama, Reality, Comedy). Certain genres command premium rates due to audience engagement and demographics.']
  rate_card_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the rate card for easy identification, typically includes year and market or channel reference (e.g., \'2024 Prime Time National Rate Card\').']
  rate_card_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the rate card, used for external reference and communication with sales teams and advertisers. Typically follows format RC-YYYYNNNN.']
  rate_card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate card indicating its operational state and usability for ad order entry.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate card by sales market type. Upfront rates are negotiated in advance for the broadcast year, scatter rates are for last-minute inventory, package rates bundle multiple spots, digital rates apply to OTT/streaming, sponsorship rates for integrated content, and remnant rates for unsold inventory.']
  seasonality_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base rates to account for seasonal demand variations (e.g., 1.25 for high-demand periods like holidays, 0.85 for low-demand summer months).']
  spot_length_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the advertising spot in seconds for which the rate applies. Common lengths are 15, 30, 60, and 120 seconds.']
  trp_value decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target Rating Point value for the specific demographic audience segment. TRP is similar to GRP but focuses on a specific target demographic rather than total audience.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rate card to track revisions and updates. Follows semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0).']

  Note: 'Advertising rate card defining the standard pricing for ad inventory by channel, daypart, spot length, and audience demographic. Captures rate card version, effective date range, channel, daypart, spot length, gross rate, net rate, CPM basis, GRP value, audience demographic target, rate type (upfront, scatter, package, digital), and approval status. Used as the pricing baseline for ad order entry and proposal generation. Distinct from negotiated deal rates on individual orders.'
}

Table subscriber.subscriber {
  subscriber_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscriber. Primary key for the subscriber master record across all service tiers (SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, bundled). System-generated surrogate key independent of household or device.']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Business justification: Links subscribers to Nielsen panel profiles and ACR measurement identities. Required for cross-platform audience measurement, ratings attribution, and MRC-accredited viewership reporting. Standard pra']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscriber account. Determines access entitlements and billing state. Active subscribers have full access; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; cancelled accounts are terminated; trial accounts are in promotional period; churned accounts have completed cancellation.']
  adobe_profile_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this subscriber to their unified profile in Adobe Experience Platform. Used for personalization, segmentation, and journey orchestration.']
  arpu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monthly revenue generated by this subscriber across all revenue streams (subscription, transactional, advertising). Key metric for subscriber value assessment and segmentation.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of subscription billing. Monthly, quarterly, and annual are recurring; lifetime is one-time payment; pay-per-view is transactional.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether California subscriber has opted out of personal data sale. Required for CCPA compliance.']
  ccpa_opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when CCPA opt-out was recorded. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predictive score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating likelihood of subscriber cancellation within next billing cycle. Generated by machine learning model based on engagement, payment history, and behavioral signals.']
  coppa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscriber account is subject to COPPA restrictions (under 13 years old). When true, data collection and advertising are restricted.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing subscriber\'s primary geographic location. Used for content rights windowing, regional pricing, and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscriber record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Subscriber\'s date of birth. Used for age verification, parental controls, COPPA compliance, and demographic segmentation.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for subscriber communication, authentication, and personalization. Used as primary contact channel and login credential.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the subscriber, typically used for integration with Zuora billing and Adobe Experience Platform. May be customer-facing account number or external system key.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s given name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriber has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR. Required for EU subscribers.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when GDPR consent was obtained. Required for audit trail and consent management.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s self-identified gender. Used for demographic analytics and content personalization. Optional field respecting subscriber privacy preferences.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking subscriber to their household group. Multiple subscribers may share a household for family plans and concurrent stream management.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of subscriber\'s most recent authentication. Used for engagement tracking and churn prediction.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s family name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.']
  last_viewing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of subscriber\'s most recent content viewing session. Used for engagement analysis and retention modeling.']
  ltv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Predicted total revenue this subscriber will generate over their entire relationship with the service. Used for acquisition cost justification and retention prioritization.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriber has consented to receive marketing communications. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed for this subscriber based on their service tier. Enforced by playout and CDN systems.']
  max_registered_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to this subscriber account. Used for device management and anti-piracy enforcement.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are active on this subscriber account. When true, content access is restricted based on rating thresholds and PIN protection.']
  parental_control_pin varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN code required to access age-restricted content when parental controls are enabled. Stored in hashed format for security.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for subscriber support and account recovery. May be used for two-factor authentication.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s postal or ZIP code. Used for geographic segmentation, local content recommendations, and blackout enforcement.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing subscriber\'s preferred interface and content language. Used for UI localization and content recommendations.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the subscriber originally registered. Used for acquisition analytics and channel effectiveness measurement.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber account was first created. Used for cohort analysis, LTV calculation, and subscriber lifecycle tracking.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Current subscription service tier. SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand) includes basic and premium tiers; AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand) is free with ads; TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand) is pay-per-view; bundle includes multiple services.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription is scheduled to end or renew. Null for active recurring subscriptions. Used for churn prediction and retention campaigns.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription tier became effective. Used for billing cycle calculation and tenure analysis.']
  total_viewing_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours of content viewed by this subscriber since registration. Key engagement metric for LTV calculation and content recommendation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscriber record was last modified. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  zuora_account_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this subscriber to their billing account in Zuora subscription management system. Used for payment processing and revenue recognition integration.']

  Note: 'Master identity record for every subscriber across SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, and bundled service tiers. Serves as the SSOT for subscriber identity, capturing registration source, registration timestamp, account status, service tier, demographic attributes, ARPU, LTV, churn risk score, GDPR/CCPA/COPPA consent flags, and integration keys to Zuora (billing) and Adobe Experience Platform (personalization). Each subscriber has a unique identity independent of household or device.'
}

Table subscriber.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the household grouping. Primary key for the household entity.']
  primary_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary account holder or subscriber who owns this household account. This subscriber has administrative privileges for the household.']
  account_created_date date [note: 'Date when the household account was first created in the system. Used for tenure analysis and customer lifetime value (LTV) calculations.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the household\'s service location. Used for geographic market assignment and service delivery.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional field for additional address detail.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household subscription automatically renews at the end of each billing period. When false, subscription expires unless manually renewed.']
  average_revenue_per_user decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monthly revenue generated by this household, calculated as total revenue divided by number of active months. Key metric for household value assessment.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month when the household\'s recurring subscription charge is processed. Typically set at initial subscription and remains consistent.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Predictive score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the likelihood of this household cancelling their subscription in the next billing period. Generated by machine learning models based on engagement and payment patterns.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the household is located. Used for geographic segmentation and local content availability.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the household location. Determines applicable content rights, pricing, and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this household record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to sharing their viewing data with third parties for analytics and advertising purposes. Required for privacy compliance.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'Nielsen Designated Market Area code identifying the geographic television market for this household. Used for local content availability, blackout enforcement, and advertising targeting.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the household, typically derived from the primary account holder\'s name or a custom household designation.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household account. Determines access rights and billing state.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household based on its composition and purpose. Determines applicable service tier rules and entitlement models.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the household\'s preferred content and interface language. Used for content recommendations and UI localization.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent authentication event for any member of this household. Used for engagement tracking and inactive account identification.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful payment received from this household. Used for delinquency tracking and churn prediction.']
  lifetime_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by this household since account creation, including subscription fees, transactional purchases (TVOD), and advertising revenue attribution. Used for customer segmentation and retention investment decisions.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional offers. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed for this household based on their service plan. Enforced by the streaming platform to prevent account sharing abuse.']
  max_content_rating varchar [note: 'Highest content rating level allowed for viewing in this household when parental controls are active. Based on Motion Picture Association (MPA) and TV Parental Guidelines ratings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_registered_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to this household account. Used to enforce device limits per service tier.']
  mvpd_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the MVPD or vMVPD provider if this household accesses content through a cable/satellite/virtual provider authentication. Used for TV Everywhere entitlement validation.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control restrictions are active for this household. When enabled, content access is filtered based on rating thresholds and PIN protection.']
  parental_control_pin varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN code required to access age-restricted content when parental controls are enabled. Stored in hashed format for security.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument on file for this household\'s subscription billing. Determines payment processing flow and retry logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|gift_card|carrier_billing|app_store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the household address. Used for geographic market (DMA) assignment and service availability determination.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Current subscription service tier for the household. Determines content library access, stream quality (SD/HD/4K), and feature availability.']
  size varchar [note: 'Number of registered members or subscribers within this household. Used for family plan eligibility and simultaneous stream limit enforcement.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code for the household address. Used for regulatory compliance and regional content licensing.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the household\'s current subscription is scheduled to end or renew. Null for active auto-renewing subscriptions.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the household\'s current paid subscription began. May differ from account creation date if there was a trial period or reactivation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the household location. Used for scheduling, program guide display, and live event timing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this household record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the household\'s identity and location. Multiple methods may be used in combination for higher confidence.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of household identity and address verification process. Used to enforce geographic entitlements and prevent fraudulent account creation.']

  Note: 'Represents a logical household grouping of one or more subscribers sharing a service plan. Tracks household-level attributes including address, geographic market (DMA), household size, primary account holder reference, parental control settings, simultaneous stream limits, and household verification status. Supports MVPD household-based entitlement models, family plan billing, and account sharing enforcement.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription_plan {
  subscription_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription plan. Primary key for the subscription plan catalog.']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Subscription plans are platform-specific products (Disney+ Basic vs Hulu Live TV). Billing, entitlement provisioning, and subscriber reporting all require knowing which OTT platform a plan belongs to.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Each subscription plan maps to specific revenue streams for financial reporting, revenue recognition policies, and segment analysis. Finance requires this mapping for ASC 606 compliance and management']
  target_demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Subscription tiers are designed targeting specific Nielsen demographics (A18-49, A25-54 key demos). Required for pricing strategy, tier optimization, and validating that plan design attracts target au']
  ad_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan includes advertising (AVOD/FAST model) or is ad-free (SVOD model).']
  addon_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan can be purchased as an add-on to another base subscription plan (e.g., premium sports channel add-on).']
  audio_quality_max varchar [note: 'Maximum audio quality format available under this subscription plan (e.g., stereo, surround 5.1, Dolby Atmos, DTS:X).']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriptions under this plan automatically renew at the end of each billing period by default.']
  base_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard recurring price for the subscription plan before any discounts, promotions, or taxes. Expressed in the default currency.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which subscribers are billed for this plan (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual, biannual, one-time, usage-based).']
  blackout_restrictions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan is subject to geographic blackout restrictions for certain live sports or regional content.']
  bundled_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a bundled subscription plan that combines multiple services or content packages (e.g., streaming + live TV + music).']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing subscriber cancellation: anytime (immediate), end of billing period, no refund, or prorated refund.']
  churn_risk_category varchar [note: 'Assessed churn risk category for subscribers on this plan based on historical churn rate analysis (low, medium, high).']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed under this subscription plan. Null indicates unlimited concurrent streams.']
  content_library_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of content library access granted by this plan (e.g., full catalog, limited selection, premium content only, sports only, news only, kids only).']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract commitment period in months for this subscription plan. Null indicates month-to-month with no minimum commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  device_registration_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to a subscriber account under this plan. Null indicates unlimited device registrations.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether offline download of content is permitted under this subscription plan.']
  download_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing at any given time. Null if download_enabled is false or unlimited.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged to subscribers who cancel before the end of the contract term. Null if no early termination fee applies.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this subscription plan is available for purchase (e.g., USA,CAN,GBR,AUS). Empty indicates global availability.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when this subscription plan was first made available to subscribers.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this subscription plan record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription plan record was last modified in the system.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control features are available under this subscription plan to restrict content based on ratings.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the subscription plan used in billing systems and customer communications. Managed in Zuora product catalog.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the subscription plan features, benefits, and included content for subscriber-facing materials and internal reference.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the subscription plan displayed to subscribers (e.g., Premium Plus, Family Bundle, Sports Add-On).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription plan: active (available for new subscriptions), inactive (temporarily unavailable), deprecated (no longer offered but existing subscriptions continue), sunset (being phased out), coming soon (announced but not yet available).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Business model classification: SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand), FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television), hybrid, or add-on plan.']
  promotional_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a limited-time promotional subscription plan offered at a special price or with enhanced features.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the subscription plan tier indicating the level of service and content access (e.g., basic, standard, premium, ultimate, enterprise, family).']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date when this subscription plan will no longer be available for new subscriptions. Null if the plan is not scheduled for sunset.']
  target_arpu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target average revenue per user (ARPU) metric for financial planning and performance tracking of this subscription plan.']
  target_ltv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target lifetime value (LTV) metric representing the projected total revenue from a subscriber over the duration of their subscription to this plan.']
  trial_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for the free trial period if trial_eligible is true. Null if no trial is offered.']
  trial_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan is eligible for a free trial period for new subscribers.']
  video_quality_max varchar [note: 'Maximum video resolution quality available under this subscription plan (e.g., SD, HD, Full HD, 4K, 8K).']

  Note: 'Catalog of all available subscription plans and service tiers offered to subscribers — SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, FAST, bundled, and add-on plans. Captures plan name, tier classification, billing frequency, base price, trial eligibility, concurrent stream limit, download allowance, ad-supported flag, and plan lifecycle status. Managed in Zuora as the product catalog.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription {
  subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription record. Primary key.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method used for recurring billing of this subscription.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Subscriptions generate revenue tracked in specific revenue streams for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance, segment reporting, and P&L analysis. Finance teams need to classify subscription revenue by stream fo']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this subscription.']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that defines the service tier, features, and pricing structure.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel or source through which the subscriber acquired this subscription (e.g., web, mobile app, partner referral, promotional campaign).']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was activated and service delivery began. May differ from enrollment date if there was a pending period.']
  arpu decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average Revenue Per User calculated for this subscription over its lifetime, used for subscriber value analysis and forecasting.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription will automatically renew at the end of the current term. True = auto-renew enabled, False = manual renewal required.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The standard subscription rate amount per billing cycle, in the billing currency, before any discounts or promotions.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing for this subscription: monthly, quarterly, annual, semi-annual, weekly, or one-time (for TVOD transactions).']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign identifier associated with the subscription acquisition, used for campaign performance tracking and attribution.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was cancelled by the subscriber or system. Null for active subscriptions.']
  cancellation_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the cancellation: subscriber (voluntary), system (automated), admin (manual intervention), payment_failure (billing issue), fraud_detection (security). Null for active subscriptions.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for subscription cancellation, used for churn analysis and retention strategy. Null for active subscriptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|payment_failure|fraud|service_quality|price|content_selection|technical_issues — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the subscription contract term in months. Null for month-to-month or open-ended subscriptions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for subscription billing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  current_term_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current billing term or subscription period. Represents the next renewal or expiration date.']
  current_term_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current billing term or subscription period.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the subscriber first enrolled in this subscription plan. Represents the start of the contractual relationship.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period ends after a payment failure or cancellation, after which access is fully revoked. Null if no grace period applies.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated or modified.']
  ltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lifetime Value representing the total revenue generated or projected from this subscription over its entire lifecycle.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed under this subscription plan, enforced by the streaming platform.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are enabled for this subscription to restrict access to age-inappropriate content. True = enabled, False = disabled.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional rate expires and standard pricing resumes. Null if no promotion is active.']
  promotional_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The discounted subscription rate amount during the promotional period, in the billing currency. Null if no promotion is active.']
  promotional_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is currently on a promotional or discounted rate. True = promotional pricing active, False = standard pricing.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription is scheduled to renew for the next billing cycle.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the subscription, displayed to subscribers and customer service representatives.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription: active (in service), suspended (temporarily paused), cancelled (terminated by subscriber or system), expired (end date reached), trialing (in trial period), pending (awaiting activation).']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Classification of subscription model: SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand), bundle, premium add-on, or trial.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the subscription was suspended (e.g., payment failure, subscriber request, fraud investigation). Null for non-suspended subscriptions.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription service was fully terminated and access was revoked. May differ from cancellation date if there is a grace period.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period ends and billing begins, if applicable. Null for non-trial subscriptions.']
  trial_start_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period began, if applicable. Null for non-trial subscriptions.']
  zuora_subscription_reference varchar [note: 'External subscription identifier from Zuora billing system for revenue recognition and invoicing integration.']

  Note: 'Active or historical enrollment of a subscriber to a specific subscription plan. Represents the contractual service relationship — capturing plan enrollment date, renewal date, billing cycle, current status (active, suspended, cancelled, trialing), auto-renew flag, promotional rate, and Zuora subscription ID. One subscriber may have multiple subscriptions (e.g., base SVOD + premium add-on).'
}

Table subscriber.device_registration {
  device_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the device registration record. Primary key.']
  app_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.app_version. Business justification: Feature flag evaluation, force-upgrade enforcement, and A/B test cohort assignment require linking registered devices to specific app_version records. Critical for rollout management and compatibility']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Business justification: ACR (Automatic Content Recognition) and cross-platform measurement systems link registered streaming devices to audience profiles for viewership attribution. Enables deduplicated reach/frequency repor']
  device_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.device_type. Business justification: Device certification and DRM enforcement require mapping subscriber devices to certified device_type records. QoS tier assignment, ABR profile selection, and DRM security level validation all depend o']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household to which this device belongs. Enables household-level device limit enforcement and shared viewing analytics.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who registered this device. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  ad_tracking_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has consented to advertising tracking on this device. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  advertising_device_identifier varchar [note: 'Device advertising identifier (IDFA for iOS, AAID for Android). Used for targeted advertising and attribution tracking. Subject to user consent per GDPR and CCPA.']
  audio_codec_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audio codecs supported by the device (e.g., AAC, Dolby Digital, Dolby Atmos, DTS). Determines audio track selection.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent streams allowed for this device under the subscribers plan. Enforced at playback initiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this device registration record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  deregistration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the device was deregistered. Used for churn analysis and fraud investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: user_initiated|account_closure|fraud_detected|device_limit_exceeded|inactivity|admin_action|device_lost_stolen — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deregistration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was deregistered or removed from the subscriber account. Null if device is still registered.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique cryptographic hash or identifier that uniquely identifies the physical device. Used for device authentication and fraud prevention.']
  device_limit_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this device counts toward the subscribers per-account device limit. Used to exempt customer service test devices or promotional devices.']
  device_nickname varchar [note: 'User-assigned friendly name for the device (e.g., Living Room TV, Johns iPhone). Improves device management user experience.']
  drm_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the DRM device certificate used for secure content decryption. Required for protected content playback.']
  drm_security_level varchar [note: 'Security level of the DRM implementation on the device. Determines maximum content quality and protection level (e.g., Widevine L1 for HD, L3 for SD).']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated fraud risk score for this device registration (0.00 to 100.00). Higher scores indicate higher likelihood of fraudulent activity.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location derived from IP address or device GPS (country, region, city). Used for content windowing and blackout restrictions.']
  hdr_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports HDR video playback (HDR10, Dolby Vision, HLG). Enables premium content delivery.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the device was registered. Used for geolocation, fraud detection, and blackout enforcement.']
  last_active_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent authenticated activity from this device. Used to identify inactive devices and enforce inactivity policies.']
  max_download_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing on this device. Enforced per subscription plan terms.']
  max_resolution_supported varchar [note: 'Highest video resolution the device can decode and display. Used for adaptive bitrate streaming (ABR) profile selection.']
  offline_download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this device is authorized for offline content downloads. Subject to plan entitlements and DRM restrictions.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are active on this device. Restricts access to age-inappropriate content per COPPA and content rating standards.']
  parental_control_pin_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the parental control PIN. Used to authenticate access to restricted content without storing the PIN in plaintext.']
  push_notification_token varchar [note: 'Device-specific token for sending push notifications (APNS for iOS, FCM for Android). Used for engagement campaigns and service alerts.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the device was registered. Used for registration workflow analytics and fraud detection.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the device registration. Determines whether the device is authorized for playback.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first registered by the subscriber. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this device registration record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'HTTP user agent string reported by the device. Contains detailed device, OS, and browser information for analytics and troubleshooting.']

  Note: 'Records every device registered by a subscriber for authenticated playback. Captures device type (smart TV, mobile, tablet, set-top box, web browser, gaming console), device fingerprint, manufacturer, model, OS platform and version, registration timestamp, last active timestamp, device nickname, device status (active, deregistered, blocked), and DRM device certificate reference. Enforces per-account device limits and supports device management workflows including remote deregistration.'
}

Table subscriber.entitlement {
  entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the entitlement record. Primary key.']
  channel_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel package or bundle that this entitlement provides access to.']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Entitlement authorization must validate content rating against subscriber parental controls and age restrictions. Real business process: streaming platform checks entitlement.compliance_content_rating']
  content_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_episode. Business justification: Episodic entitlements for serialized content. Essential for SVOD/TVOD episode-level access control, pay-per-episode models, and early-access windows. Complements existing title_id for granular episode']
  drm_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DRM policy that governs content protection and playback authorization for this entitlement.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.media_asset. Business justification: Entitlements grant subscribers access rights to specific media assets in VOD/streaming platforms. Essential for content delivery authorization, DRM policy enforcement, playback validation, and subscri']
  release_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content windowing strategy that defines the availability period and distribution channel for this entitlement.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.season. Business justification: Season-level entitlements support seasonal release windows, partial series access, and season passes. Common in TVOD and premium tier models where seasons are monetized separately.']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Series-level entitlements ("access all episodes") common in subscription models. Supports binge-watching, series passes, and franchise-based access. Enables efficient authorization without per-episode']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who holds this entitlement.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription. Business justification: Entitlements are granted based on active subscription enrollment. Currently entitlement links to subscriber and subscription_plan, but not to the actual subscription instance. Adding subscription_id F']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that grants this entitlement.']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific content asset or title to which access is granted.']
  vod_library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VOD library collection that this entitlement grants access to.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Degree of access granted by this entitlement: full (complete access), preview (sample or trailer only), limited (restricted features or duration), or premium (enhanced quality or early access).']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement transitioned from pending to active status and became usable by the subscriber.']
  ad_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement provides ad-free viewing experience. False indicates AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand) model applies.']
  authorization_count varchar [note: 'Total number of playback authorization requests processed for this entitlement since activation.']
  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement will automatically renew upon expiration based on the underlying subscription plan.']
  blackout_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement is exempt from geographic blackout restrictions typically applied to live sports or regional content.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance requirements applicable to this entitlement (e.g., \'GDPR\', \'CCPA\', \'COPPA\'). Used to enforce data privacy and regulatory obligations.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed under this entitlement. Null indicates no stream limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was first created in the system.']
  device_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent devices that can use this entitlement. Null indicates no device limit.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether offline download is permitted for content under this entitlement.']
  early_access_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement grants early access to content before general release.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement becomes active and access rights begin.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entitlement: active (currently valid), suspended (temporarily disabled), expired (past end date), revoked (administratively cancelled), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entitlement based on how it was granted: subscription (standard plan), promotional (marketing grant), bundle (package deal), addon (premium add-on), mvpd_carriage (Multichannel Video Programming Distributor carriage agreement), or trial (free trial period).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement expires and access rights end. Null for perpetual entitlements.']
  external_entitlement_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this entitlement in the originating external system (e.g., Zuora subscription ID, MVPD authorization token).']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this entitlement is valid. Empty indicates no geographic restriction.']
  grant_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason code for why this entitlement was granted (e.g., \'Annual Subscription\', \'Promotional Campaign Q4-2024\', \'Customer Retention Offer\', \'MVPD Carriage Agreement\').']
  last_authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent playback authorization request that validated this entitlement at the CDN/DRM layer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional administrative notes or special instructions related to this entitlement.']
  parental_control_level varchar [note: 'Maximum content rating level accessible under this entitlement based on parental control settings. Follows Motion Picture Association (MPA) and TV Parental Guidelines ratings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|g|pg|pg13|r|nc17|tv_y|tv_y7|tv_g|tv_pg|tv_14|tv_ma — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quality_tier varchar [note: 'Maximum video quality level allowed by this entitlement: sd (standard definition), hd (high definition 1080p), 4k (ultra high definition), or 8k (8K resolution).']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the entitlement was revoked or suspended, if applicable (e.g., \'Payment Failure\', \'Terms of Service Violation\', \'Subscriber Request\', \'Rights Expiration\').']
  revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement was permanently revoked, if applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated this entitlement record: zuora (subscription billing), adobe_experience (personalization platform), mvpd_integration (multichannel video programming distributor), promotional_engine (marketing campaigns), or manual (administrative grant).']
  suspended_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement was suspended, if applicable.']
  trial_entitlement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a trial entitlement granted as part of a free trial period.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific content access rights granted to a subscriber based on their active subscription plan(s). Maps subscriber to entitled content windows, channel packages, VOD libraries, and premium add-ons. Captures entitlement source (plan, promotional grant, MVPD carriage), effective date, expiry date, and entitlement status. Drives playback authorization decisions at the CDN/DRM layer.'
}

Table subscriber.parental_control {
  parental_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parental control configuration record. Primary key.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.household. Business justification: Parental controls can be configured at household level (applying to all profiles in the household) or at individual profile level. Currently parental_control has subscriber_id and profile_id FKs. Addi']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber account that owns this parental control configuration. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  viewer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific viewing profile within the subscriber account to which this parental control applies. Enables per-profile restrictions within a household.']
  allowed_viewing_end_time timestamp [note: 'End time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is blocked after this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.']
  allowed_viewing_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is permitted starting at this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.']
  autoplay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic playback of next episode or recommended content is enabled for this profile. True if autoplay is on, False if disabled (often disabled for child profiles to limit binge-watching).']
  blocked_content_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content genre or category codes that are explicitly blocked for this profile (e.g., \'horror\', \'violence\', \'adult\'). Provides granular control beyond rating thresholds.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted out of data sharing under CCPA. True if opted out, False otherwise. Affects how parental control data may be used for analytics or personalization.']
  configuration_name varchar [note: 'User-defined label for this parental control configuration (e.g., \'Kids Profile\', \'Teen Settings\'). Helps account holders manage multiple configurations.']
  content_language_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\') to which content access is restricted. Null if no language restrictions apply. Used to limit content to specific languages for child profiles.']
  content_rating_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum content rating allowed for viewing under this configuration. Based on Motion Picture Association (MPA) film ratings (G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17) and TV Parental Guidelines (TV-Y, TV-Y7, TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA). Content rated above this threshold is blocked. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  coppa_child_profile_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profile is designated as belonging to a child under 13 years of age, triggering COPPA compliance requirements. True if the profile is for a child under 13, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parental control configuration record was first created in the system.']
  device_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parental control configuration restricts viewing to specific registered devices. True if device restrictions are enforced, False if profile can be accessed from any device.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this parental control configuration became or will become active. Supports scheduled activation of restrictions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this parental control configuration expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null if no expiration is set.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has provided explicit consent for processing parental control data under GDPR. True if consent is granted, False otherwise. Required for EU subscribers.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this parental control configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parental control configuration was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  maturity_level_override varchar [note: 'Custom maturity level designation that overrides standard rating thresholds for specific content. Used for granular exceptions (e.g., allowing a specific R-rated film for educational purposes). Null if no override is set.']
  max_daily_viewing_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of minutes of content viewing allowed per day for this profile. Null if no daily limit is set. Used to enforce screen time limits for child profiles.']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'Email address to which parental control alerts and notifications are sent (e.g., when a child attempts to access restricted content). Null if notifications are disabled.']
  parental_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this parental control configuration. Active indicates the configuration is enforced; Inactive indicates it is saved but not enforced; Suspended indicates temporary deactivation by system or admin.']
  pin_code_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the parental control PIN. Stored as a one-way hash for security. Never stored in plaintext. Used to authenticate access to restricted content or settings changes.']
  pin_protection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN is required to access content above the rating threshold or to modify parental control settings. True if PIN protection is active, False otherwise.']
  purchase_lock_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactional purchases (TVOD - Transactional Video On Demand) are blocked for this profile. True if purchase lock is active (preventing rentals/purchases), False if purchases are allowed.']
  search_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether search functionality is restricted to only show age-appropriate content. True if search results are filtered by parental controls, False if unrestricted search is allowed.']
  viewing_time_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time-of-day viewing restrictions are enforced for this profile. True if time restrictions are active, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Stores parental control configurations set by the account holder for a household or individual profile. Captures content rating thresholds (MPA ratings: G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17), PIN protection settings, COPPA child profile flag, blocked content categories, purchase lock, and time-of-day viewing restrictions. Enforces COPPA compliance for profiles identified as under-13.'
}

Table subscriber.viewer_profile {
  viewer_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the viewer profile within a subscriber account or household. Primary key.']
  device_registration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for device tracking, security monitoring, and cross-device analytics. May reference device registration records.']
  favorite_genre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.genre. Business justification: Personalization and recommendation engines require explicit genre preferences. Drives content discovery, homepage curation, and email marketing. Complements behavioral signals with declared preference']
  personalization_engine_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.personalization_engine. Business justification: A/B testing and personalization require tracking which recommendation engine serves each profile. Critical for measuring algorithm performance, cold-start strategy effectiveness, and experimentation i']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent subscriber account that owns this viewer profile. One subscriber may have multiple viewer profiles (e.g., up to 5 profiles per account).']
  aep_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Adobe Experience Platform segment IDs that this viewer profile belongs to. Used for personalized content recommendations, targeted advertising, and dynamic user experience customization.']
  audio_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred audio track language. Used for multi-language content with multiple audio tracks.']
  autoplay_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether autoplay of next episode or content is enabled for this profile. True enables automatic playback; false requires manual selection.']
  autoplay_previews_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic preview playback is enabled when browsing content. True enables preview autoplay; false disables previews.']
  avatar_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the profile avatar image stored in the Media Asset Management (MAM) system or Content Delivery Network (CDN). Used for visual identification in profile selection interfaces.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer has opted out of personal data sale under CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act). True indicates opt-out; false indicates consent. Required for California viewer data privacy compliance.']
  coppa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is subject to COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance requirements. True for profiles of children under 13; false for adult profiles. Enforces enhanced privacy protections and parental consent requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, lifecycle tracking, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_saver_mode boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether data saver mode is enabled to reduce bandwidth consumption. True limits video quality and disables high-bandwidth features; false allows full quality streaming.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is entitled to download content for offline viewing. True enables download feature; false restricts to streaming only. Controlled by subscription tier and DRM (Digital Rights Management) policies.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent was obtained for this viewer profile. Required for EU audience data privacy compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  genre_preferences varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred content genre identifiers for personalization and recommendation algorithms. Derived from viewing history and explicit user selections. Used by Adobe Experience Platform for segment targeting.']
  is_default_profile boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default profile that is automatically selected when the subscriber logs in. True for default profile; false for secondary profiles. Only one profile per subscriber should be marked as default.']
  is_kids_profile boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a dedicated kids profile with enhanced parental controls, age-appropriate content filtering, and COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance enforcement. True for kids profiles; false for adult/general profiles.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code with optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., \'en\', \'en-US\', \'es-MX\'). Determines the language for user interface, content metadata, and audio/subtitle defaults.']
  last_access_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was last accessed or used for content viewing. Used for engagement analytics, churn prediction, and inactive profile identification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_platform varchar [note: 'Platform type of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for platform-specific analytics and Over-The-Top (OTT) distribution tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_ios|mobile_android|smart_tv|roku|fire_tv|apple_tv|chromecast|gaming_console — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer profile has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional content. True allows marketing; false opts out. Required for GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.']
  maturity_rating_level varchar [note: 'Maximum content maturity rating level allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces parental controls and content filtering based on Motion Picture Association (MPA) film ratings and TV Parental Guidelines. Content exceeding this rating is blocked from playback. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent video streams allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces Digital Rights Management (DRM) and subscription tier entitlements. Typical values range from 1 to 4 streams.']
  max_downloads varchar [note: 'Maximum number of content titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing on this profile. Enforces subscription tier limits and storage management policies.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether personalized content recommendations and user experience customization are enabled for this profile. True enables Adobe Experience Platform personalization; false provides generic content discovery. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) consent requirements.']
  pin_code_hash varchar [note: 'Hashed PIN code for profile access control and parental lock enforcement. Used to restrict access to mature content or prevent unauthorized profile switching. Stored as cryptographic hash for security.']
  pin_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether PIN protection is enabled for this profile. True requires PIN entry for profile access; false allows unrestricted access.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the viewer profile (e.g., \'Dad\', \'Kids\', \'Guest\'). Used for profile selection and personalization.']
  profile_sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer value determining the display order of this profile in the profile selection interface. Lower values appear first. Used for user experience optimization.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the viewer profile. Active profiles can access content; inactive profiles are temporarily disabled; suspended profiles are blocked due to policy violations; deleted profiles are soft-deleted and retained for compliance.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the viewer profile within the household. Primary profiles have full account privileges; secondary profiles may have restricted access; kids profiles enforce parental controls; guest profiles are temporary.']
  subtitle_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred subtitle language. Used when subtitle_preference is enabled.']
  subtitle_preference varchar [note: 'Default subtitle display preference for this viewer profile. Off disables subtitles; on always displays subtitles; auto displays subtitles based on content language and user language mismatch.']
  total_viewing_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total viewing hours for this viewer profile across all content and sessions. Used for engagement metrics, Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) analysis, and Lifetime Value (LTV) calculations.']
  video_quality_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred video streaming quality for this profile. Auto uses Adaptive Bitrate Streaming (ABR) based on bandwidth; low/medium/high/ultra_hd enforce specific quality tiers. Impacts Content Delivery Network (CDN) delivery and bandwidth consumption.']
  viewing_history_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether viewing history is tracked and stored for this profile. True enables history tracking for recommendations and resume playback; false disables tracking for privacy. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual viewing profile within a subscriber account or household — supporting multi-profile accounts (e.g., up to 5 profiles per account). Captures profile name, avatar, language preference, content genre preferences, maturity rating level, is_kids_profile flag, and Adobe Experience Platform segment IDs for personalization. Distinct from the subscriber master record — one subscriber may have multiple viewer profiles.'
}

Table subscriber.churn_event {
  churn_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscriber churn event record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Churn events are analyzed by cost center for retention program budgeting and departmental accountability. Marketing and customer success teams track churn metrics against their cost center budgets for']
  distribution_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MVPD or vMVPD partner through which the subscription was provisioned. Null for direct-to-consumer subscriptions. Used for partner churn analysis.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household to which the churned subscriber belonged. Used for household-level churn analysis and multi-subscriber household retention strategies.']
  last_viewed_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Churn analysis tracks last content engagement to identify content gaps and inform win-back campaigns. Critical for retention analytics, content investment decisions, and personalized re-engagement off']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.outage_record. Business justification: Churn analysis must correlate service cancellations with technical outages to quantify revenue impact of infrastructure failures and prioritize reliability investments. Retention analytics require lin']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Churn analysis by profit center is critical for segment profitability reporting and EBITDA calculation. Media companies track subscriber retention by business unit for strategic resource allocation an']
  retention_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Retention specialists (workforce) are assigned to at-risk subscribers for win-back campaigns. Critical for churn prevention workflow tracking, agent performance metrics, and retention campaign ROI ana']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who churned. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific subscription that was cancelled. Links to the subscription master record.']
  win_back_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retention or win-back offer that was presented to the subscriber. Null if no offer was presented.']
  auto_renew_disabled_date date [note: 'The date when the subscriber disabled auto-renewal for their subscription. Null if auto-renewal was never disabled. Used to identify planned churn versus reactive churn.']
  cancellation_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or channel through which the cancellation was initiated. Supports analysis of churn patterns by customer touchpoint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_self_service|mobile_app|customer_care|email|chat|ivr|mvpd_drop|system_automated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the stated reason for cancellation. Used for churn analytics and product improvement initiatives. Empty value_regex indicates reference to external reason code lookup table with more than 6 values.']
  cancellation_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or additional details provided by the subscriber explaining the reason for cancellation. Supports qualitative churn analysis.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber exercised CCPA opt-out rights regarding data sale or sharing. Used for privacy compliance and marketing eligibility.']
  churn_prediction_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'The machine learning model-generated churn risk score that was assigned to the subscriber prior to the churn event. Range 0.0000 to 1.0000, where higher values indicate higher churn risk. Used for model validation and predictive accuracy assessment.']
  churn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber cancellation or lapse event occurred. This is the business event timestamp representing when the subscriber relationship ended.']
  churn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the churn event based on the nature of cancellation. Voluntary indicates subscriber-initiated cancellation, involuntary indicates business-initiated termination, payment failure indicates cancellation due to billing issues.']
  competitor_service_mentioned_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber mentioned switching to a competitor service during the cancellation process. Used for competitive intelligence and market positioning analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this churn event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the last payment amount. Supports multi-currency subscription operations.']
  customer_care_contact_count varchar [note: 'The number of customer care interactions the subscriber had in the 30 days prior to churn. Used to identify service quality issues and early warning signals.']
  days_since_last_viewing varchar [note: 'The number of days between the last viewing activity and the churn event. Used for engagement-based churn prediction models.']
