  device_count_at_churn varchar [note: 'The number of registered devices associated with the subscriber at the time of churn. Used for multi-device engagement analysis.']
  final_access_date date [note: 'The last date the subscriber had active access to content and services. May differ from churn_timestamp due to grace periods or paid-through dates.']
  gdpr_data_deletion_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber requested deletion of their personal data under GDPR right to erasure at the time of churn. Used for privacy compliance tracking.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the last successful payment received from the subscriber before churn. Used for ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) and revenue impact analysis.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'The date of the last successful payment received from the subscriber before churn. Used for revenue recognition and payment failure analysis.']
  last_viewing_date date [note: 'The date of the subscribers last content viewing activity before churn. Used to identify engagement drop-off patterns and at-risk subscriber signals.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the churn event captured by customer care agents or system processes.']
  parental_control_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether parental controls were enabled on the subscription at the time of churn. Used for family-oriented subscriber segmentation and churn analysis.']
  payment_failure_count varchar [note: 'The number of consecutive payment failures that occurred before the churn event. Used to identify involuntary churn patterns and dunning effectiveness.']
  promotional_discount_active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber had an active promotional discount at the time of churn. Used to analyze price sensitivity and promotional effectiveness.']
  reactivation_eligibility_date date [note: 'The date when the churned subscriber becomes eligible for reactivation campaigns or promotional offers. Used for win-back campaign targeting and timing.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'The subscription service tier that was cancelled. SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this churn event. Typically Zuora for subscription billing events or Adobe Experience Platform for digital engagement tracking.']
  subscription_tenure_months varchar [note: 'The total number of months the subscriber maintained an active subscription before churning. Used for cohort analysis and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculations.']
  total_lifetime_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative revenue generated from this subscriber across their entire subscription tenure. Used for LTV (Lifetime Value) analysis and churn impact assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this churn event record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  win_back_offer_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber accepted the win-back offer. True indicates acceptance and retention, False indicates rejection and churn completion. Null if no offer was presented.']
  win_back_offer_presented_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a retention or win-back offer was presented to the subscriber during the cancellation process. True indicates an offer was made, False indicates no offer was presented.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a subscriber cancellation or lapse event. Captures cancellation timestamp, cancellation channel (self-service, customer care, payment failure, MVPD drop), stated cancellation reason, win-back offer presented flag, win-back offer accepted flag, and reactivation eligibility date. Supports churn rate reporting and retention campaign targeting. Distinct from analytics — this is the operational event record.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription_change {
  subscription_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription change event. Primary key.']
  add_on_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the add-on product attached or detached during this change. Populated only for add-on attach and add-on detach change types.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Subscription changes originating from partner-driven campaigns (MVPD upgrade offers, bundling promotions, affiliate retention programs) require tracking the partner for revenue attribution, partner pe']
  primary_subscription_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user account (subscriber self-service or internal agent) that initiated the subscription change. Used for audit trails and agent performance tracking.']
  primary_subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that was active before this change. Null for new subscriptions or add-on attach events.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Plan upgrades/downgrades affect revenue stream classification for financial reporting. Finance tracks MRR changes by revenue stream for forecasting, variance analysis, and segment performance reportin']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this subscription. Enables subscriber-level change analysis and churn tracking.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription that was modified. Links to the parent subscription record in the subscriber domain.']
  zuora_invoice_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Zuora invoice generated for this subscription change. Links the change event to billing and revenue recognition processes.']
  applied_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied to the new plan as part of this change. Includes promotional offers, retention discounts, and loyalty rewards.']
  applied_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the new plan subscription fee. Used for promotion effectiveness analysis and revenue impact modeling.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic renewal is enabled for the subscription after this change. Critical for churn prediction and renewal revenue forecasting.']
  change_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the subscription change was initiated. Enables channel effectiveness analysis and self-service adoption tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile app|call center|chat|email|api|admin portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_effective_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription change became effective and billing adjustments were applied. Critical for revenue recognition and proration calculations.']
  change_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the agent or system during the change process. Captures context, customer feedback, and special handling instructions.']
  change_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the system completed processing the change and updated the subscription state. Used for operational efficiency metrics.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason provided or inferred for the subscription change. Critical for churn analysis, retention strategy, and product improvement.']
  change_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber or agent initiated the change request. Used for time-to-fulfillment analysis and customer experience tracking.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'The type of change applied to the subscription. Categorizes the nature of the modification for lifecycle analysis and revenue impact assessment.']
  content_entitlement_change varchar [note: 'Describes changes to content library access, premium channel entitlements, or windowing rights resulting from this subscription change. Critical for content licensing and rights management.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The length of the new subscription contract term in months resulting from this change. Used for commitment analysis and churn risk modeling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription change record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  device_limit_new varchar [note: 'The maximum number of concurrent devices allowed under the new subscription plan. Enables analysis of device limit impact on upgrade and downgrade decisions.']
  device_limit_previous varchar [note: 'The maximum number of concurrent devices allowed under the previous subscription plan. Used to track device entitlement changes and household sharing patterns.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription change record was last updated in the data platform. Supports change data capture and incremental processing workflows.']
  mrr_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net change in monthly recurring revenue resulting from this subscription change. Positive for upgrades, negative for downgrades. Calculated as new_monthly_recurring_revenue minus previous_monthly_recurring_revenue.']
  new_billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing frequency of the new subscription plan. Enables analysis of billing period changes and their impact on cash flow and retention.']
  new_monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly recurring revenue amount from the new plan after this change. Used to calculate MRR expansion, contraction, and net revenue retention metrics.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'The promotional code or coupon code entered by the subscriber during the change process. Used for campaign tracking and fraud detection.']
  offer_redemption_status varchar [note: 'The status of promotional offer redemption at the time of subscription change. Tracks successful and failed offer applications for campaign effectiveness measurement.']
  previous_billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing frequency of the previous subscription plan. Used to analyze billing period migration patterns and payment preference trends.']
  previous_monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly recurring revenue amount from the previous plan before this change. Critical for MRR movement analysis and revenue waterfall reporting.']
  promotional_offer_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer or retention campaign that was redeemed during this subscription change. Null if no offer was applied.']
  proration_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The prorated credit or charge amount applied to the subscriber account due to mid-cycle plan changes. Positive values represent charges, negative values represent credits.']
  proration_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the proration amount. Supports multi-currency subscription operations.']
  service_tier_change varchar [note: 'Describes the service tier transition for this subscription change. Specific to media streaming business models including AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), and tiered SVOD offerings.']
  zuora_amendment_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Zuora subscription amendment record that represents this change. Enables reconciliation with the billing system of record.']

  Note: 'Audit trail of all plan changes, upgrades, downgrades, pauses, reinstatements, and promotional offer redemptions on a subscriber\'s subscription. Captures previous plan, new plan, change type (upgrade, downgrade, pause, reactivate, add-on attach, add-on detach, offer redemption), effective date, change channel, proration amount, applied discount, offer reference, redemption status, and Zuora amendment reference. Enables subscription lifecycle analysis, promotion effectiveness tracking, and revenue reconciliation.'
}

Table subscriber.payment_instrument {
  payment_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment instrument record. Primary key.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this payment instrument. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: High-value or fraud-flagged payment methods require manual verification by billing/fraud teams. Critical for payment fraud prevention workflows, chargeback dispute resolution, and fraud analyst perfor']
  zuora_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for this payment method in the Zuora subscription billing system. Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  bank_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account for direct debit or ACH payment methods. Null for card-based payment methods.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number (ABA number in USA) for direct debit transactions. Masked or tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment instrument. Used for address verification and fraud prevention.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for additional address details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address associated with the payment instrument.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for address verification service (AVS) fraud checks.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address. Format varies by country (e.g., two-letter state code for USA).']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The brand of the credit or debit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, JCB, Diners Club). Null for non-card payment methods.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment instrument. Required for card-based payment methods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment instrument (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Determines the currency for all transactions using this instrument.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument was deactivated or closed. Null for active instruments. Used for lifecycle tracking and compliance reporting.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the payment instrument was deactivated. Used for churn analysis and payment method lifecycle management.']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'The month (1-12) when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit year when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.']
  failed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of failed transaction attempts using this payment instrument. Used for fraud detection and payment method health monitoring.']
  gateway_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this payment instrument. Used for gateway-specific operations and reconciliation.']
  gift_card_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining balance on the gift card or prepaid card. Updated after each transaction. Null for non-gift-card payment methods.']
  gift_card_code varchar [note: 'Masked gift card or prepaid card code. Full code is tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the default payment instrument for the subscriber. Used for automatic billing and subscription renewals.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful transaction using this payment instrument. Used for activity tracking and dormancy detection.']
  mandate_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the subscriber accepted the direct debit mandate. Required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.']
  mandate_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the direct debit mandate or authorization. Required for SEPA and other direct debit payment methods to comply with banking regulations.']
  masked_account_number varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the card or account number showing only the last 4 digits (e.g., ****1234). Full number is tokenized and stored securely per PCI DSS requirements.']
  payment_gateway varchar [note: 'Name of the payment gateway or processor used for this payment instrument (e.g., Stripe, Braintree, Authorize.Net, Zuora Gateway).']
  payment_instrument_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment instrument. Active instruments can be charged; expired or suspended instruments require update or replacement.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument used for subscription billing. Indicates the payment mechanism (credit card, debit card, PayPal, direct debit, bank transfer, gift card, or MVPD billing).']
  payment_token varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliant tokenization reference for the actual payment instrument details. Used to process transactions without exposing sensitive cardholder data.']
  paypal_email varchar [note: 'Email address associated with the PayPal account. Used for PayPal payment method identification and processing.']
  paypal_payer_reference varchar [note: 'Unique PayPal payer identifier for the linked PayPal account. Used for PayPal billing agreement reference.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the payment instrument was successfully verified. Used for compliance tracking and fraud prevention.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment instrument verification process. Indicates whether the payment method has been successfully validated through AVS, CVV check, or micro-deposit verification.']

  Note: 'Subscriber\'s stored payment instruments used for subscription billing. Captures payment method type (credit card, debit card, PayPal, direct debit, gift card, MVPD billing), masked card/account number, card brand, expiry date, billing address, is_default flag, and PCI DSS tokenization reference. Managed in Zuora as the payment method entity. Distinct from actual payment transactions owned by the billing domain.'
}

Table subscriber.offer_redemption {
  offer_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offer redemption transaction. Primary key for the offer redemption record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign under which this offer was promoted. Enables campaign-level performance tracking and Return on Investment (ROI) analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotional offers are tracked against marketing cost centers for campaign ROI analysis, budget accountability, and variance reporting. Finance allocates promotional discounts to responsible cost cent']
  offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer or discount campaign that was redeemed. Links to the offer master record.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution or retail partner through which the offer was distributed, if applicable. Null for direct-to-consumer offers.']
  qualifying_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Promotional offers often require viewing or purchasing specific titles (e.g., "watch premiere to unlock discount"). Supports campaign attribution, content-driven acquisition, and partnership promotion']
  resulting_subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that the subscriber enrolled in or upgraded to as a result of this offer redemption. Links to the subscription plan master record.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Promotional offers affect revenue stream classification and promotional revenue tracking for financial reporting. Finance separates promotional revenue from standard pricing for margin analysis and re']
  sales_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales and retention agents process promotional offer redemptions via phone/chat channels. Required for agent commission calculations, sales performance tracking, and offer redemption audit trails in s']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who redeemed the promotional offer. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific subscription instance that was created or modified as a result of this offer redemption. Links to the subscription record.']
  applied_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied to the subscription as a result of this redemption. Expressed in the subscription currency.']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The marketing source or touchpoint that led to this offer redemption. Examples include email campaign, social media ad, affiliate link, or organic search. Used for marketing attribution analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  discount_duration_months varchar [note: 'The number of months for which the discount will be applied to the subscription. Null for one-time discounts or permanent pricing changes.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied when discount_type is percentage. Null for non-percentage discount types. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 25.00 for 25% off).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The type of discount mechanism applied through this offer redemption. Determines how the discount value is calculated and applied to the subscription.']
  eligibility_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the redemption was rejected or marked ineligible. Null if redemption was successful. Used for customer service and offer optimization.']
  eligibility_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscribers eligibility for the offer was verified at the time of redemption. True if eligibility checks passed, False otherwise.']
  first_time_subscriber_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption resulted in a new subscriber acquisition (True) or was redeemed by an existing subscriber (False). Critical for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) and Lifetime Value (LTV) analysis.']
  offer_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the promotional offer expires and can no longer be redeemed. Used to validate redemption eligibility and track expired redemption attempts.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The platform or channel through which the subscriber redeemed the offer. Enables channel effectiveness analysis for promotional campaigns.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'The unique promotional code or voucher code entered by the subscriber at the time of redemption. Used for tracking and validation.']
  redemption_device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the subscriber to redeem the offer. Supports device-specific campaign optimization and user experience analysis.']
  redemption_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the redemption request originated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered Personally Identifiable Information (PII) under General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer redemption. Tracks whether the redemption was successfully applied, expired before use, reversed due to policy violation, or rejected due to eligibility failure.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber successfully redeemed the promotional offer. Represents the business event time of redemption.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated impact on revenue resulting from this offer redemption. Negative values represent revenue reduction due to discounts; positive values may represent incremental revenue from new subscriptions or upgrades.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the offer redemption was reversed. Common reasons include fraud detection, policy violation, subscriber request, or billing dispute. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the offer redemption was reversed or cancelled. Null if the redemption remains active. Used for chargeback and refund reconciliation.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber explicitly accepted the terms and conditions associated with the promotional offer at the time of redemption. Required for compliance and dispute resolution.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the offer terms and conditions that the subscriber agreed to at redemption. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc. Used for legal audit trails.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  zuora_subscription_number varchar [note: 'The subscription identifier in the Zuora billing system that was created or modified as a result of this offer redemption. Used for billing reconciliation and revenue recognition.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a subscriber redeeming a promotional offer or discount code. Captures subscriber reference, offer reference, redemption timestamp, redemption channel, applied discount amount, resulting subscription plan, and redemption status (applied, expired, reversed). Enables promotion effectiveness tracking and revenue impact reconciliation.'
}

Table subscriber.subscriber_consent_record {
  subscriber_consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscriber consent record. Primary key for the immutable audit trail of consent events.']
  device_registration_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device from which the consent was provided. Supports device-level consent tracking and audit requirements.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA consent changes requiring manual review are processed by compliance staff. Essential for regulatory audit trails, consent dispute resolution, and compliance team workload tracking in privacy']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the subscriber to whom this consent record applies. Links to the subscriber master entity.']
  collection_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the consent was collected. Identifies the touchpoint where the subscriber provided or withdrew consent. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|email|in_person|api|third_party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  communication_channel_preference varchar [note: 'The subscribers preferred communication channel for receiving messages related to this consent type. Governs operational routing of communications.']
  consent_audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'External reference or identifier linking this consent record to detailed audit logs or supporting documentation. Supports regulatory audit and compliance verification.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this consent expires and must be re-confirmed. Nullable for consents without expiration. Supports time-bound consent management.']
  consent_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope and purpose of the data processing covered by this consent. Provides business context for the consent type.']
  consent_source_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the consent action was initiated. Used for fraud detection and audit trail verification.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the consent. Indicates whether the subscriber has granted, withdrawn, or not provided consent for the specified activity.']
  consent_text_presented varchar [note: 'The exact text of the consent disclosure or opt-in language presented to the subscriber at the time of consent. Immutable record for legal compliance and audit.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the consent action was captured. Represents the business event time of the consent grant or withdrawal.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of data processing or communication activity for which consent is being recorded. Defines the scope of the consent grant or withdrawal.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent policy or terms presented to the subscriber at the time of consent. Enables tracking of consent under different policy versions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first inserted into the system. Immutable audit field for record creation tracking.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days for which subscriber data may be retained under this consent. Nullable if no specific retention period applies. Governs data lifecycle management.']
  double_opt_in_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the subscriber confirmed their consent via double opt-in. Nullable if double opt-in is not required or not yet confirmed.']
  double_opt_in_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has confirmed their consent through a double opt-in process (e.g., email confirmation link). True if double opt-in is complete.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction under which this consent was collected. Determines applicable privacy regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last updated. For immutable audit trail design, this should remain equal to created_timestamp unless metadata corrections are applied.']
  opt_in_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the subscriber provided consent. Distinguishes between explicit opt-in, implicit acceptance, and other consent mechanisms.']
  parental_consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent is required for this subscriber under COPPA or equivalent regulations. True if the subscriber is a minor requiring parental consent.']
  parental_consent_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental consent has been verified through an acceptable verification method. True if verification is complete and valid.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code representing the subscribers preferred language for consent communications and disclosures.']
  third_party_processor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party data processor or partner to whom data may be shared under this consent. Nullable if consent does not involve third-party sharing.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the subscriber for withdrawing consent. Nullable for granted consents. Supports churn analysis and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of subscriber data privacy consents, opt-in/opt-out preferences, and communication channel preferences required under GDPR, CCPA, and COPPA. Captures consent type (marketing email, personalization, data sharing, third-party analytics, children\'s data processing), communication channel preferences (email, push, SMS, in-app, postal), preferred language, consent status, consent timestamp, consent version, collection channel, and jurisdiction. Immutable audit trail — new records are inserted for each consent or preference change rather than updating existing rows. Governs both legal compliance and operational communication routing.'
}

Table subscriber.communication_preference {
  communication_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the communication preference record.']
  aep_segment_id varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this preference to a specific audience segment in Adobe Experience Platform for personalization and targeting.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this communication preference profile.']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Customer service representatives update communication preferences during support calls. Required for customer care quality assurance, preference change audit trails, and agent training on privacy comp']
  aep_journey_suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscriber should be suppressed from Adobe Experience Platform automated journey orchestration for this preference type.']
  consent_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the subscriber provided or updated this communication preference, used for audit and compliance verification.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'Channel or touchpoint through which the subscriber provided or updated this communication preference (web signup, mobile app, customer service interaction, account creation, preference center, or third-party integration).']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber provided or last updated this communication preference, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  consent_user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user agent string captured when the subscriber provided or updated this communication preference, used for audit and compliance verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this communication preference record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  double_opt_in_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has confirmed their opt-in through a double opt-in verification process (e.g., email confirmation link).']
  double_opt_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber confirmed their opt-in through the double opt-in verification process, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this communication preference expires or is no longer effective, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd). Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this communication preference becomes effective and should be honored by outbound communication systems, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'The email address to which communications should be sent when email opt-in is enabled.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted in to receive email communications for this preference type.']
  frequency_preference varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s preferred frequency for receiving communications of this type (daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, or as needed).']
  in_app_messaging_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted in to receive in-app messages within the OTT (Over-The-Top) or SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand) application for this preference type.']
  last_communication_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the last communication of this preference type was successfully sent to the subscriber, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this communication preference record was last updated, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  mobile_phone_number varchar [note: 'The mobile phone number to which SMS and push notifications should be sent when those channels are opted in.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or context about this communication preference, such as special handling instructions or subscriber-provided reasons for preference changes.']
  postal_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the postal mailing address for physical mail communications when postal mail opt-in is enabled.']
  postal_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the postal mailing address (apartment, suite, unit number) for physical mail communications.']
  postal_city varchar [note: 'City name for the postal mailing address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the postal mailing address.']
  postal_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the postal mailing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  postal_mail_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted in to receive physical postal mail communications for this preference type.']
  postal_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the postal mailing address.']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this communication preference record (active and in use, inactive, pending verification through double opt-in, or suspended due to bounce or compliance issue).']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of communication this preference applies to (marketing campaigns, transactional notifications, service updates, editorial content, promotional offers, or administrative messages).']
  preferred_contact_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day the subscriber prefers to receive communications (morning, afternoon, evening, night, or anytime).']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code) indicating the subscriber\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., en, en-US, es, fr-CA).']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted in to receive push notifications on registered devices for this preference type.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted in to receive SMS text messages for this preference type.']
  unsubscribe_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has globally unsubscribed from all communications of this preference type across all channels.']
  unsubscribe_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber unsubscribed from this communication preference type, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']

  Note: 'Subscriber\'s channel and frequency preferences for all outbound communications — email, push notification, SMS, in-app messaging, and postal mail. Captures opt-in status per channel, preferred language, preferred contact time, unsubscribe flags, and Adobe Experience Platform journey suppression flags. Distinct from consent_record (legal compliance) — this governs operational communication routing.'
}

Table subscriber.mvpd_affiliation {
  mvpd_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MVPD affiliation record. Primary key.']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: MVPD authentication and TV Everywhere entitlements operate within specific DMA markets. Required for blackout enforcement, local station affiliation rules, retransmission consent compliance, and marke']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MVPD or vMVPD provider organization (e.g., Comcast, DirecTV, Hulu Live). Links to provider master record.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: MVPD partnerships generate distinct revenue streams (carriage fees, affiliate revenue) that must be tracked separately for financial reporting, revenue recognition, and partner settlement reconciliati']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who holds this MVPD affiliation. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: MVPD authentication verification requires manual review by operations staff for fraud prevention and authentication disputes. Critical for compliance audit trails, fraud investigation workflows, and M']
  affiliation_channel varchar [note: 'The platform or device type through which the subscriber first authenticated with the MVPD provider.']
  affiliation_end_date date [note: 'The date when the MVPD affiliation ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active affiliations.']
  affiliation_source varchar [note: 'The method by which the MVPD affiliation was established (direct subscriber login, single sign-on, API provisioning from MVPD, bulk import, or partner integration).']
  affiliation_start_date date [note: 'The date when the MVPD affiliation became active. Represents when the subscriber first authenticated through the MVPD provider.']
  affiliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MVPD affiliation. Active indicates valid authentication; expired or revoked indicates the subscriber must re-authenticate.']
  authentication_failure_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of failed authentication attempts since the last successful authentication. Reset to zero on successful authentication.']
  authentication_failure_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code or message for the most recent authentication failure (e.g., invalid credentials, account suspended, service unavailable).']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'The technical authentication protocol used for TV Everywhere login (SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, or proprietary MVPD method).']
  authentication_token varchar [note: 'The encrypted or hashed authentication token issued by the MVPD provider during TV Everywhere login. Used for session validation and entitlement checks.']
  auto_renewal_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MVPD affiliation is set to automatically renew authentication tokens. True if auto-renewal is enabled.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed under this MVPD affiliation, as defined by the MVPD provider\'s policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this MVPD affiliation record was first created in the system.']
  data_sharing_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted out of data sharing with the MVPD provider. True if the subscriber has opted out.']
  device_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of devices that can be registered under this MVPD affiliation, as defined by the MVPD provider\'s policy.']
  entitlement_package_code varchar [note: 'The code representing the subscriber\'s entitlement package from the MVPD (e.g., basic, premium, sports tier). Determines which content the subscriber can access.']
  entitlement_package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the entitlement package (e.g., Premium Plus, Sports Bundle, Basic Cable).']
  geographic_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing geographic or blackout restrictions imposed by the MVPD provider (e.g., DMA code, zip code range). Used to enforce regional content availability.']
  home_dma_code varchar [note: 'The Nielsen DMA code representing the subscriber\'s home market as reported by the MVPD provider. Used for local content and blackout enforcement.']
  last_authentication_failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent failed authentication attempt with the MVPD provider.']
  last_authentication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent successful authentication event with the MVPD provider.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this MVPD affiliation record was last updated.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the MVPD affiliation was last verified with the provider. Used to determine if re-authentication is required.']
  max_content_rating varchar [note: 'The maximum content rating allowed under this MVPD affiliation\'s parental controls. Content rated above this level is blocked. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mvpd_affiliate_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique subscriber identifier assigned by the MVPD provider for TV Everywhere authentication. This is the external identifier used in SAML assertions and OAuth flows.']
  mvpd_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MVPD provider type: traditional MVPD (cable/satellite), virtual MVPD (streaming), telco (IPTV), or other distribution model.']
  next_verification_due_date date [note: 'The date when the next verification check with the MVPD provider is due. Calculated from last_verified_timestamp plus verification_frequency_days.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the MVPD affiliation, such as special arrangements, support tickets, or escalation history.']
  parental_control_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are enabled for this MVPD affiliation. True if parental controls are active.']
  parental_control_pin varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN used to unlock parental control restrictions. Stored in hashed format for security.']
  privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has provided consent for sharing viewing data with the MVPD provider. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  privacy_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the subscriber provided or updated their privacy consent for MVPD data sharing.']
  verification_frequency_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between required re-verification checks with the MVPD provider. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days depending on provider policy.']

  Note: 'Records the affiliation between a subscriber and their MVPD or vMVPD provider for TV Everywhere authentication and carriage-based entitlement. Captures MVPD provider name, affiliate ID, authentication token, entitlement package, affiliation status, and last verified timestamp. Supports TV Everywhere login flows and MVPD-sourced subscriber entitlement grants.'
}

Table subscriber.win_back_offer {
  win_back_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for win_back_offer']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this win-back offer to the broader marketing campaign in Adobe Experience Platform or other campaign management systems.']
  superseded_win_back_offer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on win_back_offer (superseded_win_back_offer_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved this win-back offer for activation. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this win-back offer was approved for activation by authorized personnel. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  auto_renew_after_offer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews at standard pricing after the win-back offer period expires.']
  compliance_ccpa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this win-back offer has been reviewed and approved for CCPA compliance in California market.']
  compliance_coppa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this win-back offer has been reviewed and approved for COPPA compliance when targeting households with children under 13.']
  compliance_gdpr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this win-back offer has been reviewed and approved for GDPR compliance in European markets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this win-back offer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount and any monetary values associated with the offer.']
  current_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current number of times this win-back offer has been redeemed by subscribers, tracked against the total redemption cap.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the win-back offer including terms, conditions, and benefits, used for internal reference and subscriber communications.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount amount applied to the subscription price. Nullable if offer uses percentage discount or other incentive type.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the subscription price for the duration of the offer. Nullable if offer type is not discount-based.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the win-back offer expires and is no longer available for new redemptions. Nullable for open-ended offers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the win-back offer becomes active and available for churned subscribers to redeem.']
  eligibility_churn_days_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days since churn that a subscriber remains eligible for this win-back offer. Nullable for no upper limit.']
  eligibility_churn_days_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days a subscriber must have been churned to be eligible for this win-back offer.']
  free_trial_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of free service provided as part of the win-back offer. Nullable if offer type is not free trial.']
  marketing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channel through which this win-back offer is promoted and delivered to churned subscribers.']
  max_redemptions_per_subscriber varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single subscriber can redeem this win-back offer across their lifetime. Nullable for unlimited redemptions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this win-back offer record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this win-back offer record was last modified.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the win-back offer used in marketing campaigns and subscriber communications.']
  offer_duration_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the win-back offer discount or benefit applies before reverting to standard pricing.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the win-back offer displayed to subscribers and used in internal reporting.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the win-back offer type indicating the incentive mechanism used to re-engage churned subscribers.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking used to determine which win-back offer to present when a subscriber is eligible for multiple concurrent offers. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  requires_payment_method boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber must provide valid payment method details to redeem this win-back offer.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the win-back offer indicating whether it is available for subscriber enrollment.']
  target_arpu_lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage increase in average revenue per user expected from subscribers who redeem this win-back offer, used for performance measurement.']
  target_content_library varchar [note: 'Specific content library or catalog access granted as part of the win-back offer, such as premium channels or exclusive content collections.']
  target_ltv_impact decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target incremental lifetime value expected from subscribers who redeem this win-back offer, used for ROI analysis and offer optimization.']
  target_service_tier varchar [note: 'Subscription service tier that this win-back offer applies to, indicating which type of churned subscribers are eligible.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the full legal terms and conditions document for this win-back offer, required for regulatory compliance.']
  total_redemption_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of redemptions allowed for this win-back offer across all subscribers. Nullable for unlimited total redemptions.']
  zuora_product_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this win-back offer to the corresponding product rate plan in Zuora subscription billing system.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this win-back offer record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for win_back_offer. Referenced by win_back_offer_id.'
}

Table subscriber.offer {
  offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for offer']
  adobe_experience_segment_id varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this offer to the corresponding audience segment in Adobe Experience Platform for personalization.']
  superseded_offer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on offer (superseded_offer_id)']
  ad_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer includes advertising (AVOD model) or is ad-free.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews at the end of the billing cycle.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'The recurring billing frequency for this offer.']
  bundle_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer can be bundled with other service offerings.']
  commitment_period_months varchar [note: 'Minimum commitment period in months required for this offer. Nullable for no-commitment offers.']
  concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed under this offer.']
  content_quality varchar [note: 'Maximum video quality resolution available under this offer (Standard Definition, High Definition, 4K Ultra HD, 8K).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the offer price.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the offer including benefits, features, and terms presented to subscribers.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed discount amount applied to the standard price for promotional offers. Nullable for non-promotional offers.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the standard price for promotional offers. Nullable for non-promotional offers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this offer expires and is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for evergreen offers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this offer becomes available for subscriber enrollment.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this offer is available.']
  max_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered under this offer.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the offer, used in marketing campaigns and promotional materials.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the offer displayed to subscribers and in marketing materials.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the offer based on its business purpose and target audience.']
  offline_download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscribers can download content for offline viewing under this offer.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control features are available under this offer.']
  price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard price amount charged for this offer per billing cycle.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy applicable to this offer at the time of creation.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'The subscription service tier associated with this offer (Subscription Video On Demand, Advertising Video On Demand, Transactional Video On Demand, etc.).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer indicating its availability and operational state.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'The intended subscriber segment for this offer.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL to the legal terms and conditions document for this offer.']
  trial_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for the free trial period included in this offer. Nullable for offers without trial periods.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system name that last modified this offer record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offer record was last modified.']
  zuora_product_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this offer to the corresponding product rate plan in Zuora billing system.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system name that created this offer record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for offer. Referenced by offer_id.'
}

Table subscriber.add_on {
  add_on_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for add_on']

  ad_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this add-on provides an ad-free viewing experience.']

  add_on_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the add-on product displayed to subscribers.']
  add_on_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the add-on by product category.']

  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed for this add-on. Null if unlimited.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this add-on record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the add-on price.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the add-on features, benefits, and content included.']
  device_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered for this add-on. Null if unlimited.']
  dolby_atmos_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dolby Atmos immersive audio is included with this add-on.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether offline download capability is included with this add-on.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date until which this add-on configuration remains effective. Null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this add-on configuration becomes effective for pricing and entitlements.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this add-on is available for purchase.']
  hdr_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether High Dynamic Range video quality is included with this add-on.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this add-on should be prominently featured in marketing and user interface displays.']
  is_standalone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the add-on can be purchased without a base subscription plan.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the add-on was first made available to subscribers.']
  price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard monthly recurring price for the add-on in the base currency.']
  promotional_tag varchar [note: 'Marketing tag or label used to identify promotional campaigns or featured add-ons in user interfaces.']
  requires_base_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the add-on requires an active base subscription plan to be purchased.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'The subscription service tier this add-on is compatible with (Subscription Video On Demand, Advertising Video On Demand, Transactional Video On Demand, or hybrid).']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief marketing description of the add-on for display in user interfaces and promotional materials.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display order sequence for presenting add-ons in user interfaces and catalogs.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the add-on product.']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date when the add-on will be discontinued and no longer available for new purchases. Null if no sunset is planned.']
  trial_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for the free trial period before billing begins. Null if no trial is offered.']
  uhd_4k_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Ultra High Definition 4K streaming quality is included with this add-on.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this add-on record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this add-on record was last modified.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this add-on record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for add_on. Referenced by add_on_id.'
}

Table audience.nielsen_rating {
  nielsen_rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Nielsen audience measurement record in the Silver layer. Primary key for the nielsen_rating data product.']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Nielsen ratings must track originating broadcast facility for FCC regulatory compliance reporting, signal quality correlation analysis, and transmission verification. Broadcast facilities are the lice']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Ratings measurement tied to specific licensed broadcast facilities for regulatory reporting. Nielsen data aggregated by license for FCC public interest demonstration and license renewal applications.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.campaign. Business justification: Nielsen ratings measure campaign delivery performance for billing reconciliation, makegood determination, and audience guarantee compliance. Essential for post-campaign analysis and advertiser reporti']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or network on which the program aired and for which this rating was measured.']
  closed_caption_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.closed_caption_record. Business justification: Nielsen measurement includes closed captioning compliance verification for FCC reporting. Ratings data used to demonstrate accessibility compliance in public interest filings and license renewals.']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Nielsen ratings measurement requires content rating context for demographic targeting compliance (COPPA children\'s programming restrictions) and advertiser suitability verification. Essential for regu']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Rating measurement failures or data breaches constitute compliance incidents requiring documentation. Links ratings data to incident records for root cause analysis and regulatory notification.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Nielsen ratings drive programming cost center performance analysis and budget allocation decisions. Monthly P&L reporting by programming department requires linking ratings to responsible cost center ']
  daypart_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the daypart (time segment of the broadcast day) during which the program aired, such as Prime Time, Late Night, or Early Morning. Daypart is a core dimension for ad sales and programming decisions.']
  eas_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eas_log. Business justification: Nielsen measurement windows affected by EAS alert transmissions require correlation for accurate rating adjustments. EAS interruptions impact audience measurement; link enables rating correction and c']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic market (DMA or national) for which this rating measurement applies. Supports both national and local market measurement.']
  measurement_methodology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.measurement_methodology. Business justification: Each Nielsen rating uses a specific measurement methodology (PPM, diary, set-top box). The measurement_type string should be replaced with FK to measurement_methodology for MRC accreditation tracking ']
  measurement_technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Nielsen ratings require certified measurement technicians to validate panel compliance, equipment calibration, and sweeps period data accuracy for MRC accreditation audits. Technician assignment track']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Nielsen ratings directly inform ad sales opportunity valuation and pricing negotiations. Sales teams reference specific program ratings when building proposals and justifying CPM/CPRP rates to adverti']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Nielsen ratings for syndicated content must track which distribution partner\'s broadcast generated the rating for syndication performance reporting and partner revenue reconciliation. Essential for pa']
  political_ad_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.political_ad_record. Business justification: Nielsen ratings used for political advertising rate calculations (lowest unit charge) and public file disclosure. FCC requires ratings documentation for political ad pricing verification and equal tim']
  production_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_episode. Business justification: Episode-level Nielsen ratings are the primary measurement unit for episodic television. Required for per-episode performance analysis, makegood calculations, and syndication valuation. Standard indust']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Nielsen ratings reports measure performance of specific production projects/series. Essential for greenlight decisions, talent negotiations, and advertiser delivery verification. Standard operational ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Ratings directly measure profit center performance for network/channel business units. Quarterly profit center reviews and segment reporting for investors require tying advertising revenue and audienc']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Nielsen ratings data included in FCC filings for license renewal and public interest demonstration. Ratings evidence programming service to community and support renewal applications.']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Nielsen ratings data used in revenue recognition controls for SOX compliance (advertising revenue). Ratings support revenue assertions; links measurement data to financial reporting controls.']
  sweeps_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.sweeps_period. Business justification: Nielsen ratings during sweeps periods (Feb, May, July, Nov) are the currency for annual upfront negotiations and local ad sales. The sweeps_period_flag indicates relevance, but the specific sweeps per']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program entity for which this audience measurement record was captured. Links to the program master data product.']
  air_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the program episode aired on the specified channel and market. Used as the principal business event date for this measurement record.']
  air_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the program finished airing. Combined with air_start_time to derive program duration for audience measurement calculations.']
  air_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (including timezone offset) at which the program began airing. Used for exact scheduling alignment and daypart classification.']
  audience_composition_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the program\'s total audience represented by the primary target demographic (typically A18-49). Indicates how well the program indexes against its target audience and is used in ad sales proposals and programming strategy.']
  average_audience decimal(14,2) [note: 'The average number of persons or households tuned to the program during an average minute of the telecast. Expressed in thousands (000s). AA is the primary audience volume metric used in GRP and CPM calculations for ad sales.']
  cume_audience decimal(14,2) [note: 'The unduplicated number of different persons or households that tuned to the program for at least one minute across all airings within a defined period. Expressed in thousands (000s). Cume measures total reach and is critical for frequency management in ad campaign planning.']
  demo_a18_49_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of Adults aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. A18-49 is the most commercially valuable and widely traded demographic in US television advertising, serving as the primary currency for primetime ad sales.']
  demo_a25_54_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of Adults aged 25-54 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. A25-54 is a key secondary demographic used in news programming, cable networks, and certain advertiser categories.']
  demo_k2_11_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of Children aged 2-11 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. K2-11 is the primary demographic for children\'s programming and toy/food advertisers. Subject to COPPA compliance requirements for data handling.']
  demo_m18_49_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of Men aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. M18-49 is a primary demographic for sports, automotive, and technology advertisers.']
  demo_w18_49_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of Women aged 18-49 in the measured universe who were tuned to this program. W18-49 is a primary demographic for lifestyle, beauty, and household product advertisers.']
  frequency decimal(7,2) [note: 'The average number of times a unique person or household was exposed to the program within the measurement period. Frequency = GRP / Reach. Used with reach for ad campaign effectiveness analysis and makegoods calculations.']
  grp decimal(10,4) [note: 'The sum of all ratings delivered by an advertising schedule or program, representing total audience delivery without deduplication. GRP = Reach × Frequency. A fundamental currency metric in ad sales negotiations, upfront deals, and scatter market transactions.']
  household_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of all television households in the measured universe that were tuned to this program during the measurement period. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 5.2 = 5.2%). The foundational Nielsen rating metric used for ad pricing and programming evaluation.']
  household_share decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of households using television (HUT) that were tuned to this program. Share = (Program Audience / HUT) × 100. Measures competitive performance relative to active TV viewers at the same time.']
  hut_level decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of all television households in the universe that had their television sets in use during the measurement period. HUT is the denominator for household share calculations and a key indicator of overall TV viewing activity.']
  impressions decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total number of person-level exposures to the program, calculated as average audience multiplied by the number of airings. Expressed in absolute numbers (not thousands). Impressions are the transactional currency for digital and cross-platform ad sales.']
  ingested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this Nielsen rating record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer from the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for data freshness monitoring, SLA tracking, and audit trail.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this rating record in the Silver layer, reflecting revisions from Nielsen (e.g., preliminary to final, or restated ratings). Supports change tracking and downstream consumer notification.']
  nielsen_program_code varchar [note: 'Nielsen Media Research Platform\'s proprietary alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the program title within the Nielsen measurement system. Used as the business identifier for cross-referencing ratings data with Nielsen deliverables.']
  nielsen_report_date date [note: 'The date on which Nielsen Media Research officially published or delivered this rating record to the broadcaster. Distinguishes the measurement air date from the data delivery date, which is critical for tracking preliminary vs. final rating release cycles.']
  nielsen_report_week varchar [note: 'The ISO 8601 week identifier (e.g., 2024-W23) of the Nielsen measurement week in which this rating was reported. Nielsen measurement weeks run Monday through Sunday. Used for weekly ratings aggregation and trend analysis.']
  put_level decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of all persons in the measured universe who were watching television during the measurement period. PUT is the denominator for persons share calculations and complements HUT for demographic audience analysis.']
  rating_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this Nielsen rating record indicating whether the data is preliminary (fast nationals), final, or has been revised. Ad sales and programming decisions must distinguish preliminary from final ratings. Preliminary ratings are typically released within 24 hours; final ratings follow within days.']
  reach decimal(14,2) [note: 'The unduplicated number of unique persons or households exposed to the program at least once within the measurement period. Expressed in thousands (000s). Reach is a core metric for ad campaign planning and audience delivery reporting.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this rating record. Preserved in the Silver layer for data lineage, reconciliation, and re-ingestion traceability back to the authoritative source system.']
  sweeps_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rating was measured during a Nielsen Sweeps period (February, May, July, November intensive measurement periods). Sweeps ratings carry elevated importance for affiliate compensation, ad rate setting, and programming strategy.']
  time_spent_viewing_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average number of minutes that viewers in the measured audience spent watching the program. A key engagement metric used in programming decisions and content performance analysis. Expressed in minutes.']
  total_audience decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total number of persons or households that tuned to the program for at least six consecutive minutes. Expressed in thousands (000s). TA provides a broader reach measure than AA and is used for program promotion and scheduling decisions.']
  trp decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rating points delivered specifically to a defined target demographic audience (e.g., Adults 18-49). TRP = (Target Audience Impressions / Target Universe) × 100. Used by advertisers to evaluate efficiency of reaching their specific target demographic.']
  universe_estimate_hh decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total estimated number of television households in the measured market or national universe used as the denominator for household rating calculations. Expressed in thousands (000s). Nielsen updates universe estimates annually based on census data.']
  universe_estimate_persons decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total estimated number of persons in the measured market or national universe used as the denominator for persons rating calculations. Expressed in thousands (000s). Segmented by demographic group for TRP calculations.']

  Note: 'Authoritative audience measurement record capturing program-level ratings data including household rating, share, HUT/PUT levels, GRP, TRP, average audience (AA), total audience (TA), cume audience, and demographic breakdowns (A18-49, A25-54, W18-49, M18-49, K2-11, etc.) for a specific program airing on a specific channel, market, and daypart. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Includes universe estimates used as denominators for percentage calculations. The SSOT for all ratings intelligence consumed by ad sales, programming, and regulatory reporting. Supports both national and local market measurement.'
}

Table audience.demographic_segment {
  demographic_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each demographic segment record in the audience measurement reference master. Primary key for the demographic_segment product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Role classified as REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this is a reference master for standardized audience demographic classification cells; per-role minimums are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE minimums are applied given the entity carries rich descriptive and lifecycle attributes beyond a simple code list.]']
  adobe_segment_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding segment identifier in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) for digital audience activation. Enables cross-system reconciliation between Nielsen linear audience measurement and AEP digital audience profiles for cross-platform campaign targeting and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) on OTT/streaming platforms.']
  age_range_max varchar [note: 'Upper bound (inclusive) of the age range defining this demographic cell, expressed in years. For example, 49 for the A18-49 cell. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., Adults 55+). Null for non-age-based segments.']
  age_range_min varchar [note: 'Lower bound (inclusive) of the age range defining this demographic cell, expressed in years. For example, 18 for the A18-49 cell. Null for non-age-based segments such as psychographic or income quintile segments.']
  cpm_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Relative CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing index for this demographic segment compared to the baseline segment (index = 100). Premium segments such as A18-49 or W25-54 carry higher indices reflecting advertiser demand. Used in Salesforce Media Cloud ad sales proposals and upfront/scatter market pricing negotiations. Confidential commercial pricing data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this demographic segment record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse environment.']
  daypart_relevance varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of broadcast dayparts (e.g., \'Prime Time, Late Fringe, Daytime\') for which this demographic segment has primary relevance in programming and ad sales strategy. Informs Ericsson MediaFirst scheduling decisions and Wide Orbit daypart-based ad pod targeting. Null if the segment applies across all dayparts.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this demographic segment definition is valid and available for use in audience measurement, ad sales targeting, and programming decisions. Supports temporal versioning of segment definitions as Nielsen and IAB standards evolve.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date after which this demographic segment definition is no longer valid for new audience measurement or ad targeting use. Null for currently active segments with no planned expiry. Enables historical ratings data to reference the correct segment definition vintage.']
