  ethnicity_classification varchar [note: 'Standardized ethnicity classification label for ethnicity-based demographic segments (e.g., \'Hispanic/Latino\', \'African American\', \'Asian American\', \'Non-Hispanic White\'). Populated only for ethnicity segment types. Aligned with Nielsen multicultural audience measurement standards and used in targeted content programming and ad sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_latino|african_american|asian_american|non_hispanic_white|native_american|multiracial|other — promote to reference product]']
  gdpr_special_category boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demographic segment is based on GDPR Article 9 special category data (e.g., ethnicity, health status). When true, enhanced consent and data processing controls are required for EU audience data associated with this segment. Relevant for pan-European distribution platforms and Ofcom-regulated services.']
  gender_qualifier varchar [note: 'Gender dimension of the demographic cell. \'All\' denotes combined-gender segments (e.g., Adults 18-49, Persons 2+). Aligns with Nielsen standard gender breakdowns used in GRP/TRP calculations and ad sales targeting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic level at which this demographic segment definition is applied. National segments are used for network-level ratings and upfront sales; DMA (Designated Market Area) segments apply to local market measurement; local applies to individual station markets; international for global distribution platforms.']
  grp_weight_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Weighting factor applied to this demographic segment when calculating Gross Rating Points (GRP) and Target Rating Points (TRP) in audience measurement reports. Reflects the relative population size and measurement panel representation of the segment. Sourced from the Nielsen Media Research Platform and used in Wide Orbit ad scheduling and campaign delivery calculations.']
  household_composition varchar [note: 'Classification of household structure for household-based demographic segments. Used in HUT (Homes Using Television) and PUT (Persons Using Television) audience measurement reporting and in family-oriented programming and advertising targeting strategies.']
  iab_audience_code varchar [note: 'The IAB Audience Taxonomy 2.0 standardized code corresponding to this demographic segment. Enables cross-platform audience targeting alignment between linear broadcast, OTT/AVOD/SVOD digital campaigns, and programmatic advertising systems. Used in Salesforce Media Cloud ad proposals and Adobe Experience Platform segment mapping.']
  income_quintile varchar [note: 'Household income quintile ranking (1=lowest 20%, 5=highest 20%) for income-based demographic segments. Populated only for income quintile segment types. Used in premium CPM pricing tiers and upfront ad sales targeting for high-value audience packages.']
  is_children_segment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demographic segment includes individuals under 13 years of age (e.g., K2-11, K2-5). Triggers COPPA compliance controls on data collection, ad targeting restrictions, and content rating enforcement. Critical for regulatory compliance in digital and broadcast advertising.']
  is_key_demo boolean [note: 'Flags whether this segment is designated as a key demographic (key demo) for the organization\'s primary ad sales and programming strategy. Key demos (typically A18-49, W25-54) command premium CPM rates and are the primary currency in upfront and scatter market negotiations.']
  is_nielsen_standard_cell boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demographic segment corresponds to an official Nielsen standard demographic cell (e.g., A18-49, W25-54). True for Nielsen-defined cells used in GRP/TRP/HUT/PUT ratings calculations; False for custom or proprietary segments defined internally or via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'The audience measurement methodology used to quantify this demographic segment. Panel-based measurement uses Nielsen People Meter panels; census-based uses set-top box or streaming data; hybrid combines both; modeled uses statistical projection. Determines data quality, confidence intervals, and applicability for regulatory reporting.']
  nielsen_cell_label varchar [note: 'The exact Nielsen Media Research standard cell label for this demographic segment (e.g., \'A18-49\', \'W25-54\', \'M18-34\', \'K2-11\', \'P2+\'). Used as the authoritative cross-reference when ingesting Nielsen ratings data and reconciling audience measurement reports from the Nielsen Media Research Platform.']
  nielsen_segment_code varchar [note: 'The native segment identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this demographic cell. Used for direct API integration with Nielsen ratings data feeds, ensuring accurate mapping of audience measurement data to internal segment definitions.']
  platform_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates the distribution platforms for which this demographic segment definition is applicable. Linear segments apply to traditional broadcast/cable measurement; digital applies to OTT/streaming (SVOD, AVOD, FAST); cross-platform spans both; all applies universally. Drives segment availability in Wide Orbit (linear) vs. Adobe Experience Platform (digital) targeting.']
  psychographic_profile varchar [note: 'Descriptive label for psychographic segment classification (e.g., \'Early Adopters\', \'Value Seekers\', \'Sports Enthusiasts\', \'News Junkies\'). Populated only for psychographic segment types. Derived from Adobe Experience Platform audience profiling and used in premium ad sales targeting and programming strategy.']
  reach_potential_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated maximum reach potential expressed as a percentage of the segment universe achievable across the organization\'s full distribution footprint. Used in media planning to set realistic reach targets for ad campaigns and programming strategies. Sourced from Nielsen reach and frequency models.']
  regulatory_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demographic segment is subject to regulatory restrictions on data collection, targeting, or advertising. True for segments including minors (COPPA), sensitive demographic categories under GDPR Article 9, or segments subject to FCC advertising restrictions. Triggers compliance workflow in Adobe Experience Platform and Wide Orbit.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the demographic segment, aligned with Nielsen and IAB standard cell codes (e.g., A18-49, W25-54, M18-34, K2-11, P2+). Used as the business-facing identifier in ad sales proposals, ratings reports, and audience targeting systems.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the demographic segment\'s defining characteristics, measurement criteria, and intended use cases. Provides context for business users in ad sales, programming, and analytics teams on how to correctly apply and interpret the segment in audience measurement and targeting workflows.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the demographic segment (e.g., \'Adults 18-49\', \'Women 25-54\', \'Men 18-34\', \'Kids 2-11\'). Used in reporting dashboards, ad sales presentations, and EPG audience targeting configurations.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the demographic segment definition. Active segments are available for audience targeting, ad sales proposals, and ratings reporting. Deprecated segments are retained for historical continuity but no longer used in new campaigns or programming decisions.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment by its primary defining dimension. Distinguishes age/gender cells (Nielsen standard cells such as A18-49) from psychographic profiles, income quintiles, ethnicity classifications, behavioral clusters, and household composition segments. Drives which measurement methodology and data source applies.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system of record from which this demographic segment definition was sourced. Nielsen indicates the Nielsen Media Research Platform; adobe_aep indicates Adobe Experience Platform; internal indicates proprietary segment definitions; iab indicates IAB Taxonomy-derived segments. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sweeps_priority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demographic segment is a priority measurement target during Nielsen sweeps periods (February, May, July, November). Priority segments receive enhanced panel measurement and are the primary basis for affiliate compensation and ad rate card negotiations during sweeps.']
  universe_estimate varchar [note: 'Total estimated population count (universe) for this demographic segment in the measurement geography, as defined by Nielsen. Used as the denominator in ratings calculations (rating = audience / universe × 100). Updated periodically from Nielsen universe estimates and used in GRP, TRP, reach, and frequency reporting.']
  universe_estimate_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which the universe estimate is valid. Nielsen updates universe estimates annually; this field identifies the vintage of the estimate to ensure correct ratings calculations and year-over-year audience trend analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this demographic segment record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit compliance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Reference master for standardized audience demographic segments used across Nielsen ratings, ad sales, and programming decisions. Defines age/gender cells (A18-49, W25-54, M18-34, K2-11, etc.), psychographic segments, income quintiles, and ethnicity classifications aligned with Nielsen and IAB standards. Used as the authoritative classification for all audience targeting and measurement reporting.'
}

Table audience.sweeps_period {
  sweeps_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Nielsen sweeps measurement period record in the Media Broadcasting audience data product.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_calendar. Business justification: Sweeps periods drive compliance calendar deadlines for ratings-based regulatory filings. FCC public file updates and license renewal exhibits tied to sweeps completion dates.']
  measurement_methodology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.measurement_methodology. Business justification: Each sweeps period uses a specific measurement methodology (PPM, diary, or hybrid). The measurement_methodology string should be replaced with FK to measurement_methodology master for consistency and ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Sweeps period results required in regulatory filings for performance documentation. FCC license renewals and ownership transfer applications reference sweeps ratings as public interest evidence.']
  audience_sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of Nielsen panel respondents (diary participants or PPM device carriers) included in the measurement sample for this sweeps period. Larger sample sizes yield higher statistical confidence in ratings estimates.']
  blackout_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of DMA market codes where geographic broadcast blackout restrictions apply during this sweeps period. Blackout markets are excluded from certain ratings calculations and ad delivery reporting per FCC and contractual requirements.']
  ccpa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CCPA viewer data privacy requirements apply to the measurement data collected during this sweeps period (e.g., for California DMA markets in scope). Governs opt-out rights and data sale restrictions for audience panel data.']
  coppa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COPPA restrictions apply to audience data collected during this sweeps period, specifically for children\'s programming dayparts or platforms. Governs collection and use of audience data for viewers under 13.']
  data_release_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current release state of Nielsen audience data for this sweeps period. \'unreleased\' = data not yet available; \'preliminary\' = fast affiliate estimates published; \'final\' = confirmed ratings delivered; \'revised_final\' = post-delivery corrections applied.']
  daypart_coverage varchar [note: 'Indicates which dayparts (time segments of the broadcast day) are included in the sweeps measurement scope. \'all_dayparts\' covers the full broadcast day; other values indicate restricted measurement windows. Daypart coverage affects which ad inventory can be rated and guaranteed.']
  demographic_breakdowns varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of demographic segments for which Nielsen will provide audience measurement data during this sweeps period (e.g., \'A18-49,A25-54,W18-34,M18-49,HH\'). Determines which target audience guarantees can be supported for ad sales.']
  diary_return_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'For diary-methodology sweeps periods, the percentage of distributed Nielsen viewing diaries that were returned by respondents. A key data quality indicator; low return rates may trigger Nielsen data quality warnings or adjustments.']
  dma_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Nielsen DMA market list applied during this sweeps period (e.g., \'DMA-2024-v1\'). Nielsen periodically revises DMA boundaries; this field ensures correct market mapping for historical comparisons.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in the sweeps measurement window (end_date minus start_date inclusive). Typically 28 days for standard Nielsen sweeps periods. Used in pacing and audience guarantee calculations.']
  end_date date [note: 'The official last day of the Nielsen sweeps measurement window as defined by Nielsen Media Research. Audience data collection and ratings calculations conclude on this date.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR audience data privacy requirements apply to the measurement data collected during this sweeps period (e.g., for EU markets included in scope). Governs data retention, consent, and anonymization obligations for audience panel data.']
  grp_guarantee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Gross Rating Point (GRP) audience guarantees negotiated during the upfront or scatter market apply to advertising campaigns running within this sweeps period. True = GRP guarantees in effect; False = no guarantees applicable.']
  hut_measurement_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Homes Using Television (HUT) level data is captured and reported as part of this sweeps period measurement. HUT data is used to calculate share and contextualize ratings within total available audience.']
  is_special_programming_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether special programming (e.g., season premieres, finales, major sporting events, stunting) was scheduled during this sweeps period that may inflate or distort ratings relative to non-sweeps periods. True = special programming present; False = standard schedule.']
  makegood_window_end_date date [note: 'The last date by which makegoods (compensatory ad spots issued when audience guarantees are not met) must be aired following this sweeps period. Defined in advertiser contracts and governed by Wide Orbit traffic system scheduling.']
  market_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Designated Market Areas (DMAs) included in this sweeps measurement period. Used to assess geographic coverage and local affiliate reporting obligations.']
  market_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this sweeps period covers national audience measurement, local market (DMA) measurement, or both. Determines which ad sales rate cards and audience guarantees apply.']
  nielsen_contract_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Nielsen Media Research contract or service agreement under which this sweeps period measurement is conducted. Used for billing reconciliation and SLA enforcement with Nielsen.']
  nielsen_sweep_code varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned Nielsen Media Research Platform identifier for this sweeps period (e.g., \'SWEEP-2024-NOV\'). Used to cross-reference records with Nielsen source data and reconcile audience measurement deliverables.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sweeps period record. \'scheduled\' = future period confirmed by Nielsen; \'active\' = measurement currently in progress; \'completed\' = measurement concluded and data delivered; \'cancelled\' = period cancelled (rare); \'revised\' = dates or scope amended post-publication.']
  ppm_compliance_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'For PPM-methodology sweeps periods, the percentage of PPM panel members who met Nielsen\'s minimum daily wear-time compliance threshold. Low compliance rates may affect the statistical validity of ratings data.']
  preliminary_ratings_date date [note: 'The date on which Nielsen delivers preliminary (fast affiliate) ratings estimates for this sweeps period. Preliminary data is used for early ad sales negotiations before final ratings are confirmed.']
  put_measurement_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Persons Using Television (PUT) level data is captured and reported as part of this sweeps period measurement. PUT data enables person-level demographic audience analysis beyond household-level HUT data.']
  ratings_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which Nielsen is contractually obligated to deliver final audience ratings data for this sweeps period to the broadcaster. Drives downstream ad sales billing, makegoods calculations, and audience guarantee reconciliation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this sweeps period record was first created in the Media Broadcasting Databricks Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this sweeps period record was most recently modified in the Media Broadcasting Databricks Silver layer data product. Used for change tracking, SLA monitoring, and downstream refresh orchestration.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether audience measurement data from this sweeps period must be submitted to a regulatory body (e.g., FCC, Ofcom) as part of broadcast licensing compliance or public interest reporting obligations.']
  source_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which the source system (Nielsen Media Research Platform) last updated the record for this sweeps period. Used for incremental data ingestion and change detection in the Databricks Silver layer pipeline.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this sweeps period record was sourced. Primarily \'nielsen_media_research\' for official period definitions; \'adobe_experience_platform\' for digital engagement overlays; \'manual\' for internally-defined supplemental periods.']
  start_date date [note: 'The official first day of the Nielsen sweeps measurement window as defined by Nielsen Media Research. Audience data collection and ratings calculations begin on this date.']
  stunting_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any programming stunts or special events scheduled during this sweeps period (e.g., \'Season finale of flagship drama\', \'Super Bowl lead-out programming\'). Populated only when is_special_programming_flag is True.']
  sweep_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month in which the sweeps measurement period falls. Nielsen conducts four sweeps periods annually: February, May, July, and November. Drives programming strategy and ad rate negotiations.']
  sweep_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the sweeps period (e.g., \'November 2024 Sweeps\', \'February 2025 Sweeps\'). Used in reports, ad sales proposals, and programming strategy documents.']
  sweep_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit calendar year in which the sweeps measurement period occurs (e.g., 2024). Used to uniquely identify the period in combination with sweep_month.']
  trp_guarantee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Target Rating Point (TRP) audience guarantees for specific demographic targets apply to advertising campaigns running within this sweeps period. True = TRP guarantees in effect; False = no TRP guarantees applicable.']
  upfront_rate_card_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advertising rates negotiated during the upfront sales event apply to inventory sold within this sweeps period. True = upfront rate cards govern pricing; False = scatter market rates apply.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Nielsen sweeps measurement period (February, May, July, November) defining the official start and end dates, measurement methodology (diary vs. PPM), markets in scope, and special programming flags. Sweeps periods drive programming strategy, ad rate negotiations, and audience guarantee calculations. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform.'
}

Table audience.panel {
  panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the audience measurement panel record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the audience_panel entity.']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Panel recruitment and weighting tied to licensed station coverage areas. Nielsen panels structured around broadcast license service contours to ensure representative sampling for ratings currency.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audience panels require dedicated panel managers responsible for recruitment, retention, weighting methodology compliance, and MRC accreditation maintenance. Manager assignment critical for universe e']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Audience panels are defined for specific DMAs/markets. The dma_code and dma_name are redundant with market_coverage master data. Normalizing ensures consistency with market definitions used across nie']
  measurement_methodology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.measurement_methodology. Business justification: Each panel uses a specific measurement methodology (PPM, diary, set-top box). The measurement_provider string should be replaced with FK to measurement_methodology for consistency. Panel-specific impl']
  primary_broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Audience panels are geographically recruited and must link to the primary broadcast facility serving that DMA for local market measurement, signal quality validation, and MRC accreditation audits. Rol']
  adobe_segment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this audience panel definition to a corresponding audience segment or profile group in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). Enables cross-platform audience activation by mapping Nielsen panel demographics to digital audience segments for personalization and targeted ad delivery.']
  cooperation_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of recruited panel households or individuals who are actively and consistently providing usable measurement data. A declining cooperation rate is an early indicator of panel quality degradation and may trigger MRC review. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the primary country of coverage for this panel (e.g., USA, GBR, AUS). Required for international panels and regulatory reporting to Ofcom and the European Audiovisual Observatory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience panel record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance with SOX financial reporting controls that require traceability of audience data used in revenue recognition.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Technical mechanism used to collect viewing data from panel members. Electronic meters (people meters) require active button-pressing; set-top-box (STB) and smart TV ACR (Automatic Content Recognition) are passive; diary is self-reported. Determines the granularity and reliability of minute-by-minute audience data.']
  demographic_composition varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of the demographic breakdowns captured by this panel (e.g., \'Age 2+, Adults 18-49, Adults 25-54, Persons 2-17, Households by income quintile\'). Defines which demographic cuts are available for TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations and demographic audience guarantees in ad sales.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this panel was retired or superseded by a newer panel vintage. Null for currently active panels. Used to scope historical ratings queries to the correct panel vintage and to manage panel transitions during sweeps periods.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this panel became the active currency measurement source for audience projections. Marks the beginning of the period for which this panel\'s data is used in ratings reports, ad sales guarantees, and GRP/TRP delivery calculations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Breadth of geographic coverage represented by this panel. National panels project to the total US population; DMA (Designated Market Area) panels project to a specific local television market; International panels cover non-US territories.']
  measurement_currency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is designated as the official currency measurement source for audience guarantees in advertising transactions. True = this panel\'s data is used as the contractual basis for GRP/TRP delivery and makegood calculations in upfront and scatter market deals. False = supplemental or research-only panel.']
  min_reportable_rating decimal(6,4) [note: 'The lowest audience rating value (expressed as a decimal fraction of the universe) that this panel can reliably report without exceeding acceptable statistical error thresholds. Ratings below this threshold are suppressed or flagged as unreliable. Typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.0010 = 0.1%). Governed by MRC minimum cell size requirements.']
  mrc_accreditation_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRC most recently granted or renewed accreditation for this panel. Used to track accreditation currency and schedule renewal audits. Panels must maintain continuous accreditation to support upfront and scatter market ad sales guarantees.']
  mrc_accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current MRC accreditation for this panel expires and must be renewed. Expiry triggers a mandatory re-audit process. Ad sales teams must be notified of impending expiry to manage risk on guaranteed audience delivery commitments.']
  mrc_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status granted by the Media Rating Council (MRC) for this panel. MRC accreditation is the industry standard for currency-grade audience measurement. Accredited panels are required for guaranteed ad buys and upfront sales commitments. Non-accredited panels cannot be used as primary currency.']
  nielsen_panel_code varchar [note: 'Nielsen\'s proprietary internal identifier for this panel as maintained in the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for direct API integration, data feed reconciliation, and cross-referencing Nielsen ratings reports. Distinct from the internal audience_panel_id surrogate key.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing operational notes, known data quality issues, methodology changes, or special considerations associated with this panel (e.g., \'Sample boosted in Q3 2023 for Hispanic demographic\', \'Methodology change effective Jan 2024 — includes streaming\'). Used by audience analytics and ad sales teams.']
  out_of_home_measurement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel captures out-of-home (OOH) viewing, such as viewing in bars, hotels, airports, and other public venues. OOH measurement is captured via Portable People Meter (PPM) technology. OOH-inclusive panels produce higher total audience estimates than in-home-only panels.']
  panel_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned by the measurement provider (e.g., Nielsen) to uniquely identify this panel within their system. Used for cross-system reconciliation with the Nielsen Media Research Platform.']
  panel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the audience measurement panel (e.g., \'Nielsen National People Meter 2024\', \'Nielsen Local People Meter — New York DMA\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and ad sales presentations.']
  panel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audience panel. Active panels are used for currency-grade audience projections. Suspended panels are temporarily halted due to quality or compliance issues. Retired panels are decommissioned and replaced by newer vintages.']
  panel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audience measurement methodology used by this panel. National People Meter (NPM) and Local People Meter (LPM) are electronic metered panels; Portable People Meter (PPM) captures out-of-home exposure; Diary is self-reported; Return Path Data (RPD) is set-top-box passback data; Hybrid combines multiple methods.']
  recruitment_methodology varchar [note: 'Statistical sampling approach used to recruit panel members. Probability-based sampling (address-based or random-digit-dial) is required for MRC-accredited currency panels. Opt-in panels are used for supplemental or proprietary measurement. Affects the projectability and accreditation status of the panel.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which panel membership is rotated or refreshed to prevent panel conditioning effects and maintain demographic representativeness. Annual and biennial refreshes are standard for people meter panels; continuous recruitment is used for large-scale return path data panels.']
  reporting_granularity varchar [note: 'Finest time interval at which this panel can produce reliable audience estimates. Minute-by-minute granularity is available from electronic people meter panels and supports DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) optimization. Quarter-hour is standard for diary panels. Determines compatibility with EPG-based program-level ratings reporting.']
  sampling_error_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Estimated margin of error (as a percentage) for audience projections derived from this panel at a 95% confidence interval. Smaller panels have higher sampling error. Used by ad sales teams to communicate measurement uncertainty to advertisers and to set makegood thresholds for audience delivery shortfalls.']
  size_households varchar [note: 'Total number of sample households actively participating in this panel at any given time. Drives the statistical reliability and margin of error of projected audience estimates. A larger panel size yields lower sampling error for low-rated programs.']
  size_individuals varchar [note: 'Total number of individual persons (panelists) actively measured within this panel. Distinct from household count because multiple individuals per household are measured for demographic breakdowns used in Target Rating Point (TRP) calculations.']
  streaming_measurement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel captures streaming and OTT (Over-The-Top) viewing in addition to linear broadcast. Streaming-enabled panels support cross-platform audience measurement combining linear TV, SVOD, AVOD, and FAST channel viewing for total audience reporting.']
  sweeps_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is used during Nielsen Sweeps periods (February, May, July, November) for local market ratings measurement. Sweeps data drives local ad rates and program scheduling decisions. Only MRC-accredited panels with sufficient local market sample sizes are sweeps-eligible.']
  universe_estimate varchar [note: 'Total projected population size (in persons or households) that this panel is designed to represent. The universe estimate is the denominator used to convert raw panel viewing counts into ratings percentages (e.g., HUT, PUT, Share). Updated periodically based on census data.']
  universe_estimate_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the universe_estimate represents total households, total persons, or TV households (HUT-eligible). TV households are the standard denominator for HUT (Homes Using Television) calculations in broadcast ratings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience panel record was most recently modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect stale panel configurations and trigger downstream recalculation of audience projections dependent on this panel.']
  vintage_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which this panel sample was designed and recruited. Panels are periodically refreshed to maintain demographic representativeness as the population changes. Vintage year is used to track panel age and schedule mandatory refreshes per MRC accreditation requirements.']
  weighting_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Numeric multiplier applied to this panel\'s raw viewing data to project sample observations to the total universe estimate. A weighting factor greater than 1.0 indicates the panel under-represents a demographic segment relative to the population. Used in GRP and TRP calculations.']
  weighting_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the statistical weighting approach applied to panel data to correct for demographic imbalances and non-response bias (e.g., \'Iterative Proportional Fitting / Raking\', \'Post-stratification weighting by age/sex/race/income\'). Critical for ensuring projected audience estimates are representative of the total population.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a Nielsen or proprietary audience measurement panel — a statistically representative sample of households or individuals used to project viewership to the total population. Captures panel type (Nielsen National People Meter, Nielsen Local People Meter, PPM, Diary, Return Path Data), panel size, geographic coverage (national or DMA-specific), demographic composition, panel vintage, recruitment methodology, weighting factors, and MRC accreditation status. Foundation for all projected audience estimates and the basis for measurement credibility.'
}

Table audience.viewership_record {
  viewership_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each granular viewership record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.campaign. Business justification: Viewership records are attributed to campaigns for cross-platform measurement, addressable advertising delivery verification, and DAI performance tracking. Essential for programmatic ad insertion and ']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or streaming platform channel on which the content was delivered. Used for channel-level ratings aggregation, share calculations, and carriage fee reporting.']
  closed_caption_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.closed_caption_record. Business justification: Viewership sessions must track caption availability for accessibility compliance auditing. Supports FCC complaint investigation and CVAA compliance reporting by correlating viewing with caption delive']
  compliance_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_record. Business justification: Viewership data collection requires active consent record for GDPR/CCPA compliance. Direct regulatory link ensuring data processing legality and supporting privacy request fulfillment (access, deletio']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Viewership tracking must enforce age-appropriate content delivery based on rating system for parental controls and COPPA compliance. Core requirement for subscriber protection and regulatory adherence']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Viewership data breaches or unauthorized access trigger privacy compliance incidents. GDPR/CCPA breach notification requirements mandate linking affected records to incident documentation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Viewership data attributes content consumption to producing cost centers for ROI analysis. Content performance reports by cost center drive budget allocation and greenlight decisions for programming d']
  data_quality_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Viewership records undergo quality audits for billing reconciliation, ratings certification, and advertiser makegood disputes. Auditor assignment tracked for compliance trails, client dispute resoluti']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Viewership records are classified by demographic segment for aggregation into Nielsen ratings. The demographic_age_band and demographic_gender should be replaced with FK to demographic_segment for con']
  device_type_id varchar [note: 'Hashed device identifier (e.g., hashed advertising ID, set-top box serial hash, or streaming device fingerprint) from which the viewing session originated. Pseudonymized to prevent direct device tracking per GDPR and CCPA. Used for device-level reach and frequency analysis.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Hashed/anonymized identifier representing the household unit from which the viewing event originated, derived from set-top box or ACR data. Pseudonymized per GDPR Article 25 and CCPA Section 1798.100 to prevent direct re-identification. Used for Nielsen household-level ratings aggregation and HUT (Homes Using Television) calculations.']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Viewership records are tracked by DMA for local market measurement. The geographic_dma_code should be replaced with FK to market_coverage for consistency with nielsen_rating and other measurement prod']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Viewership data drives opportunity identification and sizing in ad sales. Sales teams analyze viewing patterns to identify high-value prospects and tailor pitches. Links actual audience behavior to sa']
  originating_broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Viewership records require originating facility tracking for CDN routing optimization, regional blackout enforcement, local ad insertion decisioning, and FCC local content compliance. Role prefix dist']
  privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_request. Business justification: Privacy requests (access, deletion, portability) require viewership record identification and processing. GDPR/CCPA compliance mandates linking requests to specific data records for fulfillment within']
  production_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_episode. Business justification: Episode-level viewership tracking is standard for streaming platforms. Required for completion rate analysis, binge-watching pattern detection, and dynamic ad insertion targeting. Core operational lin']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Streaming viewership records must link to production projects for rights reporting, content performance dashboards, and recommendation algorithm training. Essential for VOD/SVOD platform operations an']
  schedule_slot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific scheduled airing or playout event from the Electronic Program Guide (EPG) that the viewer tuned into. Links to the program schedule managed in Ericsson MediaFirst. Null for on-demand (VOD/SVOD) sessions.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the viewing session as assigned by the source system (Adobe Experience Platform, set-top box telemetry, or streaming platform). Used to correlate records across ingestion pipelines and deduplicate events from multiple sources.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authenticated subscriber account from Zuora subscription billing, populated for SVOD and authenticated AVOD sessions. Null for anonymous linear broadcast or unauthenticated OTT viewing.']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset being viewed, linking to the content master catalog. Enables join to content metadata including title, genre, production details, and rights windows.']
  viewer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Hashed/anonymized identifier representing an individual viewer within a household, derived from Adobe Experience Platform profile resolution or subscriber login. Pseudonymized per GDPR and CCPA. Used for PUT (Persons Using Television) and individual-level demographic analytics.']
  acr_source varchar [note: 'The data collection methodology or source system that generated this viewership record. ACR = Automatic Content Recognition from smart TV; set_top_box = return-path data from cable/satellite STB; streaming_telemetry = OTT platform event stream via Adobe Experience Platform; panel = Nielsen People Meter panel; return_path = MVPD return-path data.']
  ad_break_count varchar [note: 'Number of ad breaks (ad pods) encountered by the viewer during this viewing session. Used for DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) reconciliation, Wide Orbit affidavit verification, and advertiser delivery reporting.']
  ads_viewed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual advertisement spots viewed (not skipped) by the viewer during this session. Used for AVOD monetization reporting, CPM impression counting, and advertiser campaign delivery verification against Wide Orbit schedules.']
  audience_share decimal(6,4) [note: 'The percentage of HUT (Homes Using Television) or PUT (Persons Using Television) tuned to this channel/program at the time of viewing, as reported by Nielsen. Share differs from rating in that it measures the proportion of active viewers rather than the total universe. Used for competitive programming analysis.']
  blackout_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a geographic blackout restriction was applied to this viewing session, preventing delivery of specific content in the viewer\'s DMA or region per rights agreements. Used for blackout compliance auditing and retransmission consent reporting.']
  broadcast_date date [note: 'The calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) of the broadcast or streaming session, in the local broadcast timezone. Used as the primary date dimension for daily ratings aggregation, sweeps period attribution, and Nielsen overnight ratings reporting.']
  cdn_edge_node varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Akamai CDN edge node or point-of-presence (PoP) that served the streaming content for this session. Used for CDN performance analysis, SLA compliance monitoring, and geographic delivery optimization.']
  completion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of the content asset viewed during this session, expressed as a decimal between 0.0000 and 1.0000 (e.g., 0.7500 = 75% viewed). Sourced directly from platform telemetry; not derived within this product. Used for engagement scoring, programming decisions, and advertiser reporting.']
  content_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total runtime in seconds of the full content asset or scheduled airing being viewed. Used as the denominator for completion rate calculations and to determine whether a full viewing occurred.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the viewer\'s geographic location at time of viewing. Used for international rights windowing compliance, GDPR/CCPA jurisdictional determination, and Ofcom/FCC regulatory reporting.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this viewership record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Used for data lineage tracking, quality auditing, and source-system reconciliation.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The broadcast daypart time segment during which the tune-in occurred, as defined by industry standard time blocks. Daypart is a fundamental dimension for advertising sales (CPM pricing, GRP/TRP calculations), programming strategy, and Nielsen ratings reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|primetime|late_news|late_fringe|overnight — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the content was delivered to the viewer. Linear = traditional scheduled broadcast; VOD = on-demand; SVOD = subscription on-demand; AVOD = ad-supported on-demand; TVOD = transactional/pay-per-view; FAST = Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television; Simulcast = simultaneous multi-platform broadcast. Critical for rights windowing compliance and revenue attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|vod|svod|avod|tvod|fast|simulcast — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used to consume the content. Used for device-level audience segmentation, ad targeting eligibility, and platform-specific reach reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tv|set_top_box|mobile|tablet|desktop|smart_tv|streaming_stick|gaming_console|dvr — promote to reference product]']
  drm_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the content stream was delivered with DRM (Digital Rights Management) encryption active during this session. Used for rights compliance auditing, anti-piracy reporting to MPA, and content protection SLA verification.']
  duration_viewed_seconds varchar [note: 'Total number of seconds the viewer actively watched the content during this session, as reported by the source telemetry system. Accounts for pauses and buffering exclusions per platform telemetry rules. Used for TSR (Time Spent Reading/Viewing) calculations and completion rate derivation.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this viewership record was first loaded into the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data freshness monitoring, SLA compliance, and audit trail purposes. Distinct from tune_in_timestamp (business event time).']
  is_authenticated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the viewer was authenticated (logged in) at the time of viewing. Authenticated sessions enable individual-level audience measurement and subscriber-linked analytics. Unauthenticated sessions rely on household or device-level inference.']
  is_sweeps_period boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this viewership record falls within a Nielsen sweeps measurement period (February, May, July, November). Sweeps data is used for setting advertising rates and programming decisions. Critical for isolating sweeps vs. non-sweeps audience behavior.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this viewership record was last modified in the Silver Layer, for example due to late-arriving data corrections, Nielsen restatement, or session completion updates. Used for incremental processing and audit trail.']
  nielsen_program_rating decimal(6,4) [note: 'The Nielsen audience rating score associated with the program airing for this viewership record, representing the percentage of the total TV households or persons in the universe tuned to the program. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform integration. Used for GRP/TRP calculations and ad rate setting.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The distribution platform category through which the content was delivered. Used for multi-platform audience aggregation, MVPD/vMVPD carriage reporting, and OTT vs. linear audience segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|cable|satellite|ott|mvpd|vmvpd|web|mobile|podcast|social — promote to reference product]']
  playback_position_seconds varchar [note: 'The content offset position in seconds at which the viewer stopped watching (tune-out position within the asset). For linear broadcast, this reflects the offset from the scheduled start of the program. Used for resume-point analytics and mid-roll ad delivery attribution.']
  stream_quality varchar [note: 'The video quality level at which the content was delivered during this session, as reported by the ABR (Adaptive Bitrate Streaming) player or CDN telemetry from Akamai. Used for quality-of-experience reporting, CDN SLA compliance, and premium content delivery analytics.']
  tune_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the viewer began watching the content or tuned to the channel. This is the principal business event timestamp for the viewership record. Used for daypart analysis, time-spent calculations, and sweeps period attribution.']
  tune_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the viewer stopped watching, changed channel, or the session ended. Used in conjunction with tune_in_timestamp to calculate duration_viewed_seconds.']
  viewing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the viewing session record. \'completed\' = viewer watched to end or tune-out cleanly; \'in_progress\' = session still active at ingestion time; \'abandoned\' = viewer left before 10% completion; \'interrupted\' = session ended due to technical failure; \'error\' = telemetry data quality issue flagged.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record of audience viewing activity for a specific content airing or stream session, capturing tuning start/end timestamps, channel or platform, device type, household or individual viewer identifier (anonymized/hashed per GDPR/CCPA), duration viewed, and completion rate. Aggregated from set-top box data, ACR (Automatic Content Recognition), and streaming platform telemetry via Adobe Experience Platform integration.'
}

Table audience.reach_frequency_report {
  reach_frequency_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reach and frequency report record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.advertiser. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated for specific advertisers to show campaign performance across their portfolio, including cross-campaign reach, frequency distribution, and GRP/TRP delivery. Essent']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign for which this reach and frequency report was produced. Links to the campaign master record in Salesforce Media Cloud. Used for post-campaign delivery validation and upfront guarantee reconciliation.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated for specific networks/channels to measure campaign delivery by channel. Direct channel link enables automated report generation, channel-specific reach analysis, ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports measure advertising campaign effectiveness by sales team cost center. Sales commission calculations and territory performance analysis require linking campaign delivery metrics']
  daypart_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the daypart (time segment of the broadcast day) for which audience measurement was computed. Links to the daypart reference product. Used to segment reach and frequency metrics by time-of-day for programming and ad sales analysis.']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demographic segment (e.g., Adults 18-49, Women 25-54) against which reach and frequency metrics are measured. Links to the demographic segment reference product sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Central to Target Rating Point (TRP) calculations and ad sales guarantees.']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic market (DMA, national, or international market) for which this reach and frequency report applies. Links to the market reference product. Used for blackout compliance, scatter market pricing, and geographic audience analysis.']
  measurement_methodology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.measurement_methodology. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are generated using specific Nielsen methodologies. The nielsen_methodology string should be replaced with FK to measurement_methodology for consistency and MRC accreditation t']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are core deliverables in ad sales proposals and post-campaign analysis. Each R&F report is generated for a specific sales opportunity to demonstrate projected or actual campaig']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports are often generated for specific distribution or syndication partners to demonstrate audience delivery against contractual obligations. Required for partner performance reporti']
  political_ad_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.political_ad_record. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports for political campaigns require public inspection file linkage per FCC rules. Demonstrates delivery of reasonable access and documents audience delivery for political advertise']
  report_analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports require certified analysts to validate GRP/TRP calculations, effective reach thresholds, and makegood determinations for advertiser guarantees. Analyst assignment tracked for c']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports support revenue recognition controls for campaign delivery verification. SOX controls test delivery documentation; links reports to control testing evidence.']
  sweeps_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.sweeps_period. Business justification: Reach/frequency reports during sweeps periods are used for guarantee verification and makegood calculations. The is_sweeps_period flag indicates relevance, but the specific sweeps period should be ref']
  audience_share_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the total viewing audience (persons using television) that was watching the specific network or program during the measurement period. Calculated as the program\'s audience divided by the total HUT/PUT audience. Used for competitive programming analysis and ad sales positioning.']
  average_frequency decimal(8,4) [note: 'The average number of times a unique viewer within the target demographic was exposed to the content or campaign during the measurement period. Calculated as total impressions divided by unduplicated reach. Core metric for evaluating campaign saturation and makegoods calculation.']
  cpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'The cost per one thousand audience impressions for the campaign or programming block, expressed in USD. Used by ad sales for post-campaign delivery validation, scatter market pricing justification, and makegood valuation. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial pricing.']
  cprp decimal(10,4) [note: 'The cost per one rating point delivered against the target demographic segment, expressed in USD. The primary pricing metric for linear TV advertising transactions. Used for upfront guarantee reconciliation, makegood valuation, and scatter market pricing. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this reach and frequency report record was first ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage tracking and pipeline monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this report (CPM, CPRP). Defaults to USD for domestic US markets. Required for international market reports and multi-currency financial reconciliation under SOX compliance.']
  delivery_variance_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage difference between the guaranteed audience delivery (GRP or TRP) and the actual delivered audience for the campaign flight. A negative value indicates under-delivery triggering makegood obligations; a positive value indicates over-delivery. Used for upfront guarantee reconciliation and scatter market adjustments.']
  effective_reach_1plus varchar [note: 'The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least one time (1+) during the measurement period. Equivalent to reach_persons; included as an explicit effective reach threshold field for consistency with N+ reporting framework used in post-campaign analysis.']
  effective_reach_3plus varchar [note: 'The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least three times (3+) during the measurement period. The industry-standard effective reach threshold used to assess whether sufficient frequency was achieved for advertising message recall. Critical for makegoods determination and campaign effectiveness evaluation.']
  effective_reach_nplus varchar [note: 'The count of unique persons exposed to the content or campaign at least N times (as defined by effective_reach_threshold) during the measurement period. Provides a configurable effective reach metric for custom campaign guarantee structures and advanced frequency analysis.']
  effective_reach_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum number of exposures (N) used to define effective reach for this specific report. Configurable per campaign or client agreement (e.g., 3 for standard campaigns, 5 for high-frequency campaigns). Determines the N+ threshold applied in effective reach calculations beyond the standard 1+ and 3+ fields.']
  grp decimal(10,4) [note: 'The sum of all rating points delivered by the campaign or programming block during the measurement period, representing total audience weight without deduplication. Calculated as reach percentage multiplied by average frequency. The primary currency for linear TV advertising transactions and upfront sales guarantees.']
  hut_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of all television households in the market that had their television sets in use during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used as a contextual benchmark for evaluating audience share and competitive programming performance.']
  is_makegood_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reach and frequency report has identified an audience delivery shortfall that requires a makegood (compensatory ad spot) to be issued to the advertiser. Triggers the makegood workflow in Wide Orbit and Salesforce Media Cloud when set to True.']
  is_sweeps_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement period falls within a Nielsen sweeps period (an intensive ratings measurement period typically occurring in February, May, July, and November). Sweeps data carries elevated importance for programming decisions, ad rate setting, and affiliate compensation calculations.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the measurement window for which reach and frequency metrics are computed. Defines the end boundary of the campaign flight, programming block, or sweeps period being analyzed.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the measurement window for which reach and frequency metrics are computed. Defines the start boundary of the campaign flight, programming block, or sweeps period being analyzed. Used in conjunction with measurement_period_end_date to bound the audience measurement interval.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of measurement window for which this report was generated. \'campaign_flight\' covers a defined ad campaign run; \'programming_block\' covers a scheduled content block; \'daypart_window\' covers a standard broadcast daypart; \'sweeps_period\' covers an intensive Nielsen ratings measurement period; \'custom_window\' covers an ad hoc analysis window.']
  nielsen_report_code varchar [note: 'The native report identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for this reach and frequency dataset. Used for source system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the Nielsen source system.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The distribution platform type for which audience reach and frequency is measured. Distinguishes linear (traditional scheduled broadcast), OTT (Over-The-Top streaming), SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand), FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television), or simulcast (simultaneous multi-platform broadcast). Critical for cross-platform audience deduplication and multi-platform reach reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|OTT|SVOD|AVOD|TVOD|FAST|simulcast — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  put_rating decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of all persons in the target demographic who were watching television during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used alongside HUT to assess total available audience and calculate audience share for programming decisions.']
  reach_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the target demographic universe reached at least once during the measurement period, calculated as (reach_persons / universe_estimate) × 100. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 45.2500 = 45.25%). Used by ad sales for post-campaign delivery reporting and scatter market pricing justification.']
  reach_persons varchar [note: 'The unduplicated count of unique persons within the target demographic segment who were exposed to the content or campaign at least once during the measurement period. The primary reach metric sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used for post-campaign delivery validation and upfront guarantee reconciliation.']
  report_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when this reach and frequency report was generated and delivered by the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Represents the business event time, distinct from audit timestamps. Used to track Nielsen data delivery SLAs and post-campaign reporting timeliness.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this reach and frequency report, sourced from the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used by ad sales teams to reference specific post-campaign delivery reports in client communications, makegoods negotiations, and upfront guarantee reconciliation.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reach and frequency report. \'draft\' indicates in-progress computation; \'preliminary\' indicates initial Nielsen delivery pending validation; \'final\' indicates validated and approved for client delivery; \'revised\' indicates a corrected reissue; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'archived\' indicates retired from active use.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'The version number of this reach and frequency report, incremented each time a revised or corrected report is issued by Nielsen or reprocessed in the lakehouse. Version 1 represents the initial delivery; subsequent versions represent corrections or revisions. Used to ensure ad sales teams are working with the most current data.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'The total number of audience exposures (duplicated) to the content or campaign during the measurement period. Equals reach_persons multiplied by average_frequency. Used as the gross delivery metric for CPM-based advertising transactions and campaign pacing analysis.']
  trp decimal(10,4) [note: 'The sum of rating points delivered specifically against the target demographic segment during the measurement period. Analogous to GRP but scoped to the defined target audience rather than the total population. Used for targeted advertising guarantee reconciliation and CPRP calculations.']
  universe_estimate varchar [note: 'The total estimated population size (in persons or households) of the target demographic segment in the specified market, as defined by Nielsen. Serves as the denominator for reach percentage calculations and rating point computations. Expressed as an absolute count.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this reach and frequency report record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks revisions, status changes, and data corrections.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing computed reach (unduplicated audience count), frequency (average exposures per unique viewer), and effective reach (viewers exposed N+ times) for a defined campaign flight, programming block, or daypart window across a specified demographic segment, market, and measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform. Used by ad sales for post-campaign delivery validation, makegoods calculation, upfront guarantee reconciliation, and scatter market pricing justification.'
}

Table audience.market_coverage {
  market_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the market coverage record representing a Designated Market Area (DMA) or television market geographic unit.']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Market coverage definitions derive from broadcast license service contours and DMA assignments. License technical parameters (power, antenna height) determine market boundaries for audience measuremen']
  primary_broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Market coverage (DMA) must link to primary licensed broadcast facility for FCC must-carry compliance, retransmission consent negotiations, local content origination requirements, and emergency alert s']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Market coverage areas determine applicable regulatory obligations (must-carry, retransmission consent). DMA and market type drive specific FCC obligations; links market definitions to compliance requi']
  blackout_rule_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic broadcast blackout rules apply to this market for sports or syndication exclusivity purposes. True if blackout restrictions can be enforced in this DMA; False otherwise. Used by rights management (Rightsline) and CDN geo-restriction (Akamai) to enforce territorial exclusivity.']
  broadcast_standard varchar [note: 'Digital broadcast transmission standard applicable to this market. \'ATSC\' (Advanced Television Systems Committee) is the US standard; \'DVB\' (Digital Video Broadcasting) is the European standard; \'ISDB\' is the Japanese/Brazilian standard; \'DTMB\' is the Chinese standard. Determines technical playout and transmission requirements.']
  cable_penetration_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that subscribe to cable television (MVPD or vMVPD service). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6234 = 62.34%). Used for carriage fee negotiations, distribution planning, and ad sales reach projections.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the country in which this market is located (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\'). Required for international rights management, windowing decisions, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  daylight_saving_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMA observes Daylight Saving Time (DST). True if DST is observed; False if not (e.g., markets in Arizona). Affects daypart scheduling, playout timing, and EPG accuracy in broadcast automation systems.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'Nielsen-assigned three-digit numeric code uniquely identifying the Designated Market Area (DMA). This is the externally-known business identifier used across ad sales, ratings reporting, and FCC filings. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform.']
  dma_rank varchar [note: 'Nielsen-assigned national rank of the DMA by television household universe size, where rank 1 is the largest market (New York). Used for market prioritization in ad sales, upfront negotiations, and carriage fee calculations. Updated annually by Nielsen.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this market coverage record became effective and the DMA definition was valid. Used for temporal versioning of DMA boundary and universe changes, ensuring historical ratings data is associated with the correct market definition.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this market coverage record ceased to be effective, typically due to a DMA boundary redefinition, market merger, or discontinuation. Null for currently active market definitions. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) history tracking.']
  fcc_market_code varchar [note: 'FCC-assigned market identifier used for must-carry and retransmission consent compliance filings. Maps the Nielsen DMA to the FCC\'s designated market definition for broadcast licensing, spectrum regulation, and MVPD carriage obligations. Required for regulatory reporting under FCC 47 CFR Part 76.']
  geographic_boundary_wkt varchar [note: 'Well-Known Text (WKT) representation of the DMA\'s geographic boundary polygon. Used for spatial analysis, blackout rule enforcement, retransmission consent territory mapping, and OTT/streaming geo-restriction via CDN (Akamai). Conforms to OGC WKT standard.']
  hispanic_tv_household_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that are Hispanic/Latino, as measured by Nielsen. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2341 = 23.41%). Used for Spanish-language content distribution decisions, targeted ad sales, and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations for Hispanic demographic segments.']
  hut_base_estimate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Nielsen baseline estimate of the percentage of television households in the DMA that are using television during an average primetime daypart. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.5823 = 58.23%). Used as a reference benchmark for HUT-based audience projections and ad sales planning.']
  market_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the Designated Market Area (DMA) or television market as defined by Nielsen (e.g., \'New York\', \'Los Angeles\', \'Chicago\'). Used in audience reports, ad sales proposals, and regulatory filings.']
  market_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the market coverage record. \'active\' indicates the DMA is currently measured and used in audience reporting; \'inactive\' indicates it is no longer measured; \'pending\' indicates a newly defined market awaiting first measurement; \'discontinued\' indicates a market that has been merged or eliminated by Nielsen.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the market scope. \'local\' indicates a single-city DMA; \'regional\' indicates a multi-city or multi-state DMA; \'national\' indicates a national aggregate market; \'international\' indicates a non-US market territory. Used for ad sales segmentation, carriage fee tiers, and programming distribution decisions.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which Nielsen provides audience measurement data for this market. \'continuous\' indicates year-round metered measurement (PPM markets); \'sweeps_only\' indicates measurement only during sweeps periods (diary markets); \'quarterly\' or \'annual\' for smaller markets with less frequent measurement cycles.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Nielsen measurement methodology applied to this market. \'PPM\' (Portable People Meter) is the electronic passive measurement used in large markets; \'diary\' is the paper-based recall method used in smaller markets; \'hybrid\' indicates a combination of both. Determines the precision and frequency of ratings data available for this market.']
  median_household_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Median annual household income (in USD) for the DMA, sourced from U.S. Census Bureau data as incorporated into Nielsen demographic profiles. Used for audience demographic segmentation, CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing tiers, and advertiser targeting qualification.']
  must_carry_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FCC must-carry rules apply to broadcast stations in this market, requiring cable operators (MVPDs) to carry local broadcast signals. True if must-carry obligations exist; False otherwise. Critical for retransmission consent negotiations and MVPD carriage compliance.']
  nielsen_source_market_code varchar [note: 'The native market identifier as assigned and used within the Nielsen Media Research Platform source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Nielsen source system, and audit of universe estimate ingestion.']
  nielsen_station_index varchar [note: 'Total number of television stations measured by Nielsen within this DMA. Indicates the competitive landscape of the local market and is used in share calculations and ad sales competitive analysis.']
  ott_penetration_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of television households in the DMA that access video content via OTT (Over-The-Top) streaming services. Expressed as a decimal. Used for digital distribution planning, SVOD/AVOD audience projections, and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) inventory estimation.']
  population_universe varchar [note: 'Nielsen-estimated total population (persons 2+) residing within the DMA boundary. Used as the denominator for person-based ratings metrics including PUT (Persons Using Television) and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations. Updated annually in the Nielsen universe estimates release.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the predominant language spoken in the DMA (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'es\' for Spanish). Used for content localization decisions, Spanish-language network distribution, and audience demographic targeting.']
  principal_city varchar [note: 'Name of the primary city or metropolitan area that anchors and names the DMA (e.g., \'New York\', \'Los Angeles\', \'Chicago\'). Used in market identification, ad sales materials, and regulatory filings.']
  put_base_estimate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Nielsen baseline estimate of the percentage of persons (2+) in the DMA who are using television during an average primetime daypart. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4612 = 46.12%). Used as a reference benchmark for PUT-based audience projections and TRP calculations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market coverage record was first created in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with SOX financial reporting controls that require data provenance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market coverage record was most recently updated in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary broadcast regulatory authority governing this market. \'FCC\' for US markets; \'Ofcom\' for UK markets; \'CRTC\' for Canadian markets; \'ACMA\' for Australian markets; \'other\' for all other jurisdictions. Determines applicable must-carry, retransmission consent, content standards, and licensing requirements.']
  retransmission_consent_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retransmission consent rules apply to broadcast stations in this market, allowing broadcasters to negotiate compensation from MVPDs for carriage of their signals. True if retransmission consent is applicable; False otherwise.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 two-letter state or province code of the primary state in which the DMA\'s principal city is located (e.g., \'NY\', \'CA\', \'IL\'). Used for geographic segmentation, blackout rule enforcement, and retransmission consent jurisdiction determination.']
  sweeps_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market participates in Nielsen sweeps periods (intensive ratings measurement periods in February, May, July, and November). True if the market is measured during sweeps; False if it is a non-sweeps market. Affects programming scheduling strategy and ad sales pricing.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary time zone of the DMA (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Critical for daypart scheduling, playout automation in Ericsson MediaFirst, EPG (Electronic Program Guide) alignment, and ad scheduling in Wide Orbit Traffic system.']
  total_counties varchar [note: 'Total number of counties or county-equivalents included within the DMA boundary. Nielsen defines DMA boundaries at the county level. Used for geographic coverage analysis, must-carry compliance, and retransmission consent territory verification.']
  tv_household_universe varchar [note: 'Nielsen-estimated total number of television households within the DMA boundary. This is the principal quantitative measurement fact for the market and serves as the denominator for all local ratings calculations, GRP/TRP computations, and CPM-based ad pricing. Updated annually in the Nielsen universe estimates release.']
  universe_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the current Nielsen universe estimates (TV household and population) became effective for this market. Used to track the currency of universe data and ensure ratings calculations use the correct denominator for the applicable measurement period.']
  universe_estimate_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which the Nielsen universe estimates (TV household and population figures) are valid. Nielsen releases updated universe estimates annually, typically effective in the fall. Used to version and audit the universe figures used in ratings calculations.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a Designated Market Area (DMA) or television market — the geographic unit used for local audience measurement. Captures DMA rank, DMA code, market name, TV household universe estimate, population universe, geographic boundaries, and applicable measurement methodology (PPM vs. diary). Aligns with Nielsen DMA definitions and FCC market designations for must-carry and retransmission consent compliance.'
}

Table audience.engagement_event {
  engagement_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each discrete digital audience engagement action captured on OTT/streaming platforms. Primary key for this product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
