  measurement_period_start date [note: 'The first date of the audience measurement observation window, aligned to the content airing schedule or campaign flight start. Used for sweeps period identification and daypart analysis.']
  measurement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this cross-platform measurement record, used in reporting, ad sales proposals, and audience currency reconciliation. Sourced from Nielsen Media Research Platform or internal measurement workflow.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cross-platform measurement record. \'draft\' indicates data is being assembled; \'preliminary\' indicates initial estimate released; \'certified\' indicates MRC-audited final figure; \'revised\' indicates a post-certification correction; \'archived\' indicates superseded by a newer measurement.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp representing when the audience measurement observation period was captured or the measurement run was executed. Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  mvpd_impressions varchar [note: 'Audience impressions attributed to Multichannel Video Programming Distributor (MVPD) and virtual MVPD (vMVPD) platforms, including cable, satellite, and virtual pay-TV providers within the measurement period.']
  nielsen_rating decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Nielsen Rating for the content or program within the measurement period, representing the percentage of all television households or persons in the universe tuned to the program. The foundational audience currency metric for linear broadcast.']
  ott_streaming_impressions varchar [note: 'Audience impressions attributed to Over-The-Top (OTT) and Subscription Video On Demand (SVOD) streaming platforms within the measurement period. Includes authenticated streaming on connected TV, mobile, and desktop devices.']
  panel_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of panel households or persons in the Nielsen (or equivalent vendor) measurement panel that contributed to this cross-platform measurement. Indicates statistical reliability; low sample sizes may trigger data suppression per MRC minimum reporting standards.']
  put_rating decimal(8,4) [note: 'Persons Using Television (PUT) rating for the measurement period, representing the percentage of all persons in the universe who are watching television during the measured time period. Used for person-level audience share calculations.']
  social_video_impressions varchar [note: 'Audience impressions attributed to social video platforms (e.g., YouTube, Facebook Video, TikTok, Instagram Reels) within the measurement period. Enables full cross-platform audience aggregation.']
  target_universe_size varchar [note: 'The estimated total population size of the target demographic universe used as the denominator for GRP and TRP calculations. Sourced from Nielsen universe estimates updated annually.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of audience exposures (impressions) across all platforms within the measurement period, including duplicate exposures across platforms. Used for CPM-based advertising currency and GRP calculation inputs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this cross-platform measurement record was last modified, supporting data lineage and revision tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record aggregating deduplicated audience measurement across linear broadcast, OTT/SVOD streaming, MVPD/vMVPD, FAST channels, and social video platforms for a specific content title or campaign flight. Captures deduplicated reach, total impressions, platform-level audience breakdown, co-viewing adjustment factor, cross-platform GRP/TRP, and big data + panel fusion methodology indicators. Aligns with Nielsen ONE, Comscore, and MRC cross-platform measurement standards. Enables unified audience currency reporting for converged TV buying.'
}

Table audience.market_distribution_agreement {
  market_distribution_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this market-partner distribution agreement record']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the DMA/market coverage area where this distribution agreement applies']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution partner (MVPD, vMVPD, affiliate, syndicator) party to this agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this market-partner distribution agreement.']
  carriage_fee_per_subscriber decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated monthly or annual fee paid per subscriber for carriage rights in this specific market. This rate varies by market size, partner type, and competitive dynamics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this market distribution agreement record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this market-specific distribution agreement expires or terminates. Null indicates an evergreen or currently active agreement.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this market-specific distribution agreement becomes effective and carriage rights commence.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution agreement grants exclusive carriage rights to the partner in this market, preventing competing distribution deals.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date when this market-specific agreement was most recently renewed or renegotiated.']
  must_carry_status varchar [note: 'FCC regulatory classification for this market-partner carriage relationship: must-carry obligation, retransmission consent negotiation, or not applicable.']
  next_negotiation_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next contract renegotiation or renewal discussion for this market-partner relationship.']
  penetration_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage of total TV households in the market that subscribe to this partner\'s service. Calculated as subscriber_count divided by market TV household universe.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner\'s performance in this specific market based on subscriber growth, payment compliance, and audience delivery metrics.']
  retransmission_consent_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retransmission consent has been granted to this partner for broadcast signals in this market, per FCC regulations.']
  subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Current number of subscribers the partner serves in this market. Used to calculate total carriage fees and assess market penetration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this market distribution agreement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual distribution agreement between a market coverage area and a distribution partner. It captures market-specific carriage terms, subscriber economics, and territorial rights that exist only in the context of this market-partner relationship. Each record links one DMA/market to one distribution partner with commercial terms, performance metrics, and exclusivity provisions that vary by market-partner combination.. Existence Justification: Distribution partners (MVPDs, vMVPDs, affiliates, syndicators) operate across multiple geographic markets, each with market-specific carriage agreements, subscriber counts, and commercial terms. Conversely, each DMA/market has multiple distribution partners competing or coexisting with different penetration rates, carriage fees, and exclusivity provisions. This is the operational foundation of territorial rights management and market-based distribution economics in media broadcasting.'
}

Table audience.talent_demographic_appeal {
  talent_demographic_appeal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each talent demographic appeal measurement record. Primary key for the association.']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the demographic segment for which this appeal measurement applies']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the talent profile being measured for demographic appeal']
  appeal_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Normalized appeal index score measuring the talent\'s overall appeal within this specific demographic segment. Scale and methodology aligned with industry standards for talent appeal measurement.']
  awareness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the demographic segment that is aware of or recognizes this talent. Key metric for talent reach and recognition within the target demographic.']
  casting_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this talent is recommended for casting in content targeting this demographic segment based on appeal thresholds. Used by casting directors and programming teams.']
  confidence_interval decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence interval (margin of error) for the appeal metrics in this measurement, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess measurement precision.']
  favorability_rating decimal(10,4) [note: 'Favorability or likability rating of the talent among respondents in this demographic segment who are aware of the talent. Measures positive sentiment.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date when this appeal measurement was conducted or published. Critical for tracking appeal trends over time and ensuring measurement currency.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'The research methodology used to measure this talent\'s appeal within this demographic (e.g., Q_Score_Standard, Custom_Survey, Panel_Research). Ensures comparability and appropriate interpretation of metrics.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this appeal measurement record. Current measurements are used for active casting and programming decisions; Historical measurements are retained for trend analysis.']
  measurement_vendor varchar [note: 'The research vendor or organization that conducted the appeal measurement (e.g., Marketing Evaluations Inc., Nielsen, custom internal research). Used for data provenance and methodology validation.']
  q_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The Marketing Evaluations Q Score for this talent within this demographic segment. Measures familiarity and appeal, used industry-wide for talent valuation and casting decisions.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this talent demographic appeal record was created in the data platform.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this talent demographic appeal record was last updated.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of respondents from this demographic segment included in the appeal measurement study. Used to assess statistical confidence and measurement reliability.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'The directional trend of this talent\'s appeal within this demographic compared to prior measurements (Rising, Stable, Declining). Used for talent investment and booking strategy.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the measured appeal and awareness metrics of talent across specific demographic segments. It captures Q Score ratings, awareness percentages, favorability ratings, and appeal indices that exist only in the context of a specific talent-demographic combination. Each record links one talent_profile to one demographic_segment with measurement data used for casting decisions, talent booking, programming strategy, and advertising sales targeting. This is the operational system of record for talent appeal research used by programming, casting, and ad sales teams.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, talent appeal is measured across multiple demographic segments simultaneously (e.g., a host has different Q Scores for A18-49, W25-54, M18-34), and each demographic segment has appeal measurements for multiple talent profiles. Casting directors, programming strategists, and ad sales teams actively query and manage this appeal data to make talent booking decisions, programming placement, and advertising targeting decisions. This is operational audience research data, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table audience.partner_demographic_target {
  partner_demographic_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each partner demographic targeting agreement record']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the demographic segment being targeted in this partner agreement']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the partner who is targeting this demographic segment']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this partner demographic target record was first created']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this partner demographic targeting agreement expires or is superseded. Null indicates an ongoing evergreen agreement.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this partner demographic targeting agreement becomes active. Aligns with contract effective dates or campaign flight dates.']
  guaranteed_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed audience rating percentage for this demographic segment that the broadcaster commits to deliver to the partner. Used for make-good calculations if performance falls short.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this partner demographic target record was last updated']
  performance_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Performance tracking index measuring actual delivery against guaranteed rating for this partner-demographic target. Values above 1.0 indicate over-delivery, below 1.0 indicate under-delivery requiring make-goods.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this demographic target within the partner relationship. Tier 1 represents highest priority demographics for the partner with premium inventory allocation and first-right-of-refusal on premium placements.']
  target_cpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Negotiated cost per thousand impressions (CPM) rate for this partner-demographic combination. This is the commercial pricing agreed between the broadcaster and partner for reaching this specific demographic segment.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this demographic targeting agreement. Active indicates currently in-force, Suspended indicates temporarily paused, Expired indicates contract term ended.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual audience targeting agreement between partners and demographic segments. It captures partner-specific demographic targeting strategies where each partner (advertiser, agency, network) negotiates specific performance guarantees, CPM rates, and priority levels for each demographic segment they target. Each record links one partner to one demographic segment with commercial terms that exist only in the context of this business relationship.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, partners (advertisers, agencies, networks) negotiate targeting agreements for multiple demographic segments simultaneously, each with distinct CPM rates, performance guarantees, and priority levels. Conversely, each demographic segment (e.g., A18-49, W25-54) is targeted by multiple partners with varying commercial terms. Media sales teams actively manage these partner-demographic targeting agreements as contractual commitments that drive inventory allocation, pricing, and make-good obligations.'
}

Table audience.segment_regulatory_compliance {
  segment_regulatory_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this segment-obligation compliance record. Primary key.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to audience_segment. Part of the natural key.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation that governs this segment']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audience segment being evaluated for regulatory compliance']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this audience segment was activated for use under this specific regulatory obligation. Represents when the segment was first deployed in campaigns subject to this regulation.']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the most recent compliance audit. Documents any violations, concerns, or confirmations of compliance. Used for audit trail and remediation tracking.']
  ccpa_opt_out_honored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) opt-out signals are honored when activating this segment for California residents. When True, individuals who have exercised their CCPA right to opt out of sale/sharing of personal information are excluded from segment activation. Required for compliance with CCPA and its amendment CPRA. [Moved from audience_segment: CCPA opt-out handling is a compliance attribute specific to the CCPA regulatory obligation. This should be tracked in the segment-obligation compliance relationship where it can be audited, dated, and linked to the specific CCPA obligation record. The segment itself shouldn\'t carry obligation-specific compliance flags.]']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of this segment with respect to this specific obligation. Values: compliant (segment meets all requirements), non_compliant (violations identified), under_review (audit in progress), remediation_in_progress (corrective actions underway), exempt (exemption granted).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance record was created in the system.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this segment was deactivated or removed from use under this obligation. Null if segment remains active. Used to track compliance history.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the segment was deactivated under this obligation. Examples: compliance violation, obligation no longer applicable, segment retired, business decision. Null if segment is active.']
  exemption_basis varchar [note: 'Legal or regulatory basis for any exemption granted for this segment under this obligation. Null if no exemption applies. Examples: safe harbor provision, de minimis exception, grandfathered status, regulatory waiver reference.']
  exemption_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when any granted exemption expires and full compliance is required. Null if no exemption or if exemption is permanent.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 6 lawful basis under which audience data is processed to build and activate this segment for EU audiences. \'consent\' requires explicit opt-in; \'legitimate_interest\' requires a balancing test; \'contract\' applies to subscriber-based segments; \'not_applicable\' for non-EU segments. Required for regulatory compliance reporting. [Moved from audience_segment: GDPR lawful basis is specific to the GDPR regulatory obligation and represents compliance metadata that belongs in the segment-obligation relationship. Different segments may have different lawful bases under GDPR, and this should be tracked as part of the compliance record with audit dates and status, not as a standalone attribute on the segment.]']
  is_coppa_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is subject to COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) restrictions, meaning it includes or may include children under 13. COPPA-restricted segments are prohibited from behavioral targeting and require parental consent mechanisms. Critical compliance flag for ad sales and digital activation workflows. [Moved from audience_segment: This boolean flag indicates COPPA applicability but doesn\'t capture the compliance relationship details. The segment_regulatory_compliance association should track the actual COPPA obligation (via regulatory_obligation_id) with full audit history, compliance status, and exemption basis. The flag in audience_segment can remain as a derived/cached indicator for quick filtering, but the authoritative compliance relationship lives in the association.]']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date when this segment-obligation pairing was last audited for compliance. Used to track audit frequency and schedule next review.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance record was last modified.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit of this segment under this obligation. Calculated based on obligation compliance_frequency and risk_level.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which remediation actions must be completed to avoid penalties or enforcement action. Null if no remediation required.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions planned or in progress to address any compliance gaps. Null if segment is compliant or exempt.']
  responsible_compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the compliance officer accountable for managing this segment-obligation compliance relationship.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level specific to this segment-obligation combination. May differ from the obligation\'s general risk_level based on segment characteristics (e.g., a children\'s segment under COPPA has critical risk even if COPPA\'s general risk is high).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between audience segments and regulatory obligations. It captures the activation compliance status, audit history, and risk assessment for each segment-obligation pairing. Each record links one audience segment to one regulatory obligation with attributes that track when the segment was activated under that obligation, its current compliance state, audit findings, and any exemptions or special handling requirements.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, audience segments are subject to multiple regulatory obligations simultaneously (COPPA for children\'s content, GDPR for EU audiences, accessibility requirements, advertising restrictions), and each regulatory obligation governs multiple audience segments across the portfolio. The compliance team actively manages these relationships by tracking activation dates, conducting periodic audits, assessing risk levels specific to each segment-obligation pairing, documenting exemptions, and maintaining remediation plans. This is an operational compliance management process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table talent.talent_profile {
  talent_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the talent profile record. Primary key for the talent master entity.']
  coppa_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.coppa_declaration. Business justification: Child performer profiles must track COPPA compliance status, parental consent, and data collection restrictions. Casting child talent requires COPPA declaration verification; production legal confirms']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Talent relations/artist development employees manage talent rosters in media orgs. Supports talent relations reporting, contract oversight workflows, internal escalation paths, and manager performance']
  privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_request. Business justification: Talent profiles are subject to GDPR/CCPA data subject access requests, deletion requests, and opt-out requests. Privacy teams process talent data requests; talent management systems must respond to pr']
  talent_agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to talent.talent_agency']
  biometric_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent has provided consent for collection and use of biometric data (facial recognition, voice prints) for digital effects, deepfake prevention, and AI training. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent has opted out of the sale or sharing of their personal information under CCPA. Required for California-based talent or productions.']
  clearance_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the talent\'s current clearance status expires and requires renewal. Used for compliance tracking and production eligibility verification.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'The current clearance status of the talent for production work. Indicates whether background checks, work authorization, insurance verification, and legal clearances are complete and current.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the talent profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The talent\'s date of birth. Required for age verification, child labor law compliance (COPPA), insurance underwriting, and residual payment calculations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'The primary email address for talent communication, contract delivery, and digital correspondence.']
  exclusivity_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is currently bound by an exclusivity clause that restricts their ability to work with competing networks, studios, or brands. Used for conflict checking during casting and booking.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the talent\'s GDPR consent for data processing. Tracks whether the talent has provided, withdrawn, or is pending consent for personal data usage under EU regulations.']
  gender_identity varchar [note: 'The talent\'s self-identified gender. Used for diversity reporting, casting analytics, and compliance with equal opportunity regulations.']
  imdb_identifier varchar [note: 'The talent\'s unique identifier in the Internet Movie Database (IMDb). Used for cross-referencing filmography, public profile linking, and industry data integration.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is currently covered by production insurance (liability, workers compensation, errors and omissions). Required for on-set work authorization.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number for the talent\'s production insurance coverage. Used for claims processing and certificate of insurance verification.']
  isni_code varchar [note: 'The International Standard Name Identifier (ISNI) for the talent. A globally unique identifier for creators used in rights management and royalty distribution systems.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the talent as it appears on official documents and contracts. Used for contract execution, payroll, and legal compliance.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'The talent\'s nationality represented as a 3-letter ISO country code. Used for work authorization, tax treaty eligibility, and international co-production compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special requirements, or contextual information about the talent. Used for casting notes, production preferences, and internal communication.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'The primary contact phone number for the talent. Used for production scheduling, emergency contact, and direct communication.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'The talent\'s primary working language represented as a 2-letter ISO language code. Used for casting, dubbing, and localization workflows.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the talent profile. Determines availability for casting, contract execution, and system access.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of their work in syndication, streaming, or international distribution. Determined by union status and contract terms.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'The professional or stage name used by the talent for public appearances, credits, and marketing materials. May differ from legal name.']
  talent_tier varchar [note: 'The classification tier of the talent based on industry recognition, box office draw, and compensation level. Used for budgeting, marketing strategy, and billing rate determination.']
  talent_type varchar [note: 'The primary professional category or role type of the talent. Determines applicable guild rules, contract templates, and credit attribution standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: actor|director|writer|producer|host|correspondent|crew|voice_artist|composer|cinematographer — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'The talent\'s tax identification number (SSN, EIN, or international equivalent). Required for payroll processing, tax withholding, and IRS 1099 reporting.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'The primary labor union or guild affiliation of the talent. Determines contract terms, minimum compensation, residual eligibility, and working conditions.']
  union_member_number varchar [note: 'The talent\'s membership identifier within their primary union or guild. Required for residual payment processing and contract compliance verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the talent profile record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness verification.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'The talent\'s current work authorization status in the primary production jurisdiction. Determines eligibility for employment and production participation.']
  work_visa_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the talent\'s work visa. Critical for production scheduling and compliance with immigration regulations.']
  work_visa_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of work visa held by the talent (e.g., O-1, P-1, H-1B). Used for compliance tracking and production planning for international talent.']

  Note: 'Master identity record for all on-screen and off-screen talent — actors, directors, writers, producers, hosts, correspondents, crew, and voice artists. Stores legal name, stage name, date of birth, nationality, gender identity, primary language, union membership references, talent tier classification, representation agency references, IMDb/ISNI identifiers, biometric consent flags, data privacy status (GDPR/CCPA), clearance status, and active/inactive lifecycle state. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for talent identity referenced by production, rights, royalty, and scheduling workflows across the enterprise.'
}

Table talent.guild_affiliation {
  guild_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guild affiliation record. Primary key.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Guild affiliations may require regulatory filings for membership verification, dues reporting, and CBA compliance documentation. HR teams file guild membership reports; compliance teams track guild re']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, crew member) who holds this guild membership.']
  cba_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement became effective for this membership.']
  cba_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement expires. Used to track contract renewal cycles and potential rate changes.']
  cba_version varchar [note: 'Version or year identifier of the collective bargaining agreement that governs this membership. Critical for determining applicable residual rates, working conditions, and compensation structures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild affiliation record was first created in the system.']
  dues_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of membership dues payment obligations. Current (all dues paid up to date), overdue (payment past due but within grace period), delinquent (significantly past due, may affect standing), exempt (not required to pay dues), waived (dues forgiven by guild).']
  guild_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the entertainment industry guild or union. SAG-AFTRA (Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists), WGA (Writers Guild of America), DGA (Directors Guild of America), IATSE (International Alliance of Theatrical Stage Employees), AFM (American Federation of Musicians), BECTU (Broadcasting Entertainment Communications and Theatre Union - UK), ACTRA (Alliance of Canadian Cinema Television and Radio Artists). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG-AFTRA|WGA|DGA|IATSE|AFM|BECTU|ACTRA|OTHER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  guild_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the guild or union organization.']
  health_benefits_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible for guild-provided health insurance benefits based on this membership and earnings thresholds. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  join_date date [note: 'Date when the talent officially became a member of this guild. Used to determine seniority and eligibility for certain benefits.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction or country where this guild membership is registered and governed. USA (United States), CAN (Canada), GBR (United Kingdom), AUS (Australia), NZL (New Zealand), IRL (Ireland). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|AUS|NZL|IRL|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_dues_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent membership dues payment received by the guild.']
  local_chapter varchar [note: 'Local chapter, branch, or regional division of the guild to which this membership is assigned. Used for regional governance and event coordination.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Unique membership identifier assigned by the guild to the talent member. Required for residual payment processing and production compliance verification.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current standing of the talent within the guild. Good standing (active member in compliance), suspended (temporarily barred due to dues or violations), resigned (voluntarily terminated membership), expelled (involuntarily removed), inactive (not currently active but not terminated), pending (application under review).']
  membership_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of membership level within the guild. Full member (standard active membership with full benefits), fi-core (financial core member with limited participation), apprentice (trainee or provisional member), honorary (recognition membership), lifetime (permanent membership status), emeritus (retired member with recognition).']
  next_dues_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the next membership dues payment is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or remarks about this guild membership. May include information about membership restrictions, special accommodations, or historical context.']
  pension_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible for guild pension plan contributions and benefits. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guild membership makes the talent eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of their work. True if eligible, false otherwise. Critical for royalty calculation workflows.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the guild membership ended, whether through resignation, expulsion, or other termination. Null for active memberships.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild affiliation record was last modified in the system.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the guild membership status was last verified with the guild organization. Used to ensure data accuracy for production compliance.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the guild membership status. API (automated guild system integration), manual (direct guild contact), document (membership card or certificate scan), self-reported (talent declaration), third-party (verification service).']

  Note: 'Tracks each talent\'s formal membership and standing within entertainment industry guilds and unions — SAG-AFTRA, WGA (Writers Guild of America), DGA (Directors Guild of America), IATSE, AFM (American Federation of Musicians), and international equivalents (BECTU, ACTRA). Records membership number, guild code, membership tier (full member, fi-core, apprentice), join date, current standing (good standing, suspended, resigned), dues payment status, and applicable collective bargaining agreement (CBA) version. Critical for residual eligibility determination and production compliance.'
}

Table talent.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the talent contract record. Primary key for the talent contract entity.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Talent contracts require billing accounts for payment processing, AP/AR reconciliation, tax reporting, and financial audit trails. Standard production finance practice links contracts to billing accou']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Talent contracts reference content ratings for age-appropriate casting, union restrictions on minors, and compensation adjustments based on content maturity. Casting directors verify rating constraint']
  coppa_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.coppa_declaration. Business justification: Contracts for child performers must reference COPPA declarations to ensure compliance with data collection, usage rights, and parental consent requirements. Business affairs includes COPPA compliance ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Talent costs must be allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L, overhead allocation, EBITDA reporting, and management accountability. Production companies track talent spend by cost center (Drama']
  finance_production_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.production_budget. Business justification: Talent contracts are charged against production budgets for cost control, variance analysis, and financial reporting. Every above-the-line deal must track to a specific budget for production accountin']
  negotiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Business affairs employees negotiate talent contracts. Required for contract approval workflows, negotiator performance tracking, workload analysis, audit trails, and compliance documentation. Column ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Talent contracts are executed with production partners (studios, networks, streamers) as the contracting entity. Essential for contract administration, payment routing, rights clearance, and partner r']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production (series, film, episode, special) for which this talent is contracted. Nullable for overall deals and first-look deals not tied to a specific production.']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Talent contracts for episodic content are series-level agreements with episode guarantees, option periods, and multi-season terms. Fundamental to TV production contracting and long-form content busine']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, host, correspondent, crew member) engaged under this contract.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of formal amendments made to this contract since initial execution. Tracks contract modification history.']
  backend_participation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of net profits, adjusted gross, or other revenue streams the talent is entitled to receive after recoupment of production costs. Common for above-the-line talent.']
  backend_participation_type varchar [note: 'The revenue calculation basis for backend participation. Net profits are after all costs; adjusted gross is after specific deductions; gross receipts are from first dollar. None indicates no backend participation.']
  base_compensation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The guaranteed base fee or salary payable to the talent for services rendered under this contract, excluding bonuses, backend participation, and residuals.']
  billing_credit_position varchar [note: 'The contractually specified position and format of the talent\'s on-screen credit (e.g., \'First Position Main Title\', \'Shared Card\', \'Single Card\', \'Above the Title\'). Critical for talent reputation and guild compliance.']
  compensation_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base compensation amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  compensation_structure varchar [note: 'The payment model for the base compensation. Flat fee is a single lump sum; per-episode is paid per episode produced; weekly/annual/day/hourly rates define periodic payment schedules.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the contract, typically assigned by legal or business affairs. Used for external reference and tracking.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the contract in its lifecycle. Deal memo represents initial short-form agreement; countersigned indicates mutual acceptance; long-form executed is the final detailed contract; amended reflects modifications; suspended indicates temporary hold; terminated is early cancellation; expired is natural end. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: deal_memo|countersigned|long_form_executed|amended|suspended|terminated|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the talent engagement type. Series regular deals cover multi-episode commitments; episodic guest agreements are single or limited appearances; overall deals provide exclusive services across multiple projects; first-look deals grant priority rights to talent-developed content; crew contracts cover technical and production staff; talent holding deals reserve talent availability; development deals fund content creation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: series_regular|episodic_guest|overall_deal|first_look_deal|crew_contract|talent_holding_deal|development_deal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system.']
  credit_placement_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed contractual requirements for credit placement, including main title vs end credits, paid advertising inclusion, promotional materials, and any special formatting or adjacency requirements.']
  credit_size_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum size of the talent\'s on-screen credit as a percentage of the title or other reference credit size. Null if not contractually specified.']
  document_reference_uri varchar [note: 'The storage location or document management system URI for the executed contract document and all amendments. Used for legal reference and audit.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract expires or the engagement period ends. Nullable for open-ended overall deals or holding deals with option-based extensions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract becomes binding and the talent engagement period begins.']
  engagement_role varchar [note: 'The specific role or position the talent is contracted to perform (e.g., Lead Actor, Director, Showrunner, Writer, Executive Producer, Director of Photography, Host).']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes exclusivity restrictions preventing the talent from working on competing projects or for other networks/studios during the contract period.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exclusivity restrictions, including prohibited activities, competing platforms, genre restrictions, and geographic scope. Null if exclusivity_flag is false.']
  governing_cba varchar [note: 'The specific collective bargaining agreement that governs this contract, including version and effective date (e.g., SAG-AFTRA Television Agreement 2020-2023). Defines minimum compensation, working conditions, and residual structures.']
  guaranteed_episodes varchar [note: 'The minimum number of episodes for which the talent is guaranteed compensation, regardless of actual production. Common in series regular deals. Null for non-episodic contracts.']
  guild_affiliation varchar [note: 'The labor union or guild to which the talent belongs. SAG-AFTRA covers actors and broadcasters; WGA covers writers; DGA covers directors; IATSE covers crew and technical staff. Multiple indicates membership in more than one guild. Non-union indicates no guild membership.']
  holdback_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after contract end during which the talent is restricted from working on similar projects or for competing entities. Common in overall deals and first-look agreements.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent contract amendment. Null if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract record was most recently updated in the system.']
  option_exercise_deadline date [note: 'The date by which the company must notify the talent of its decision to exercise or decline the next option period. Null if no active option exists.']
  option_exercise_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent option period. Not applicable if no options exist; pending if decision deadline has not passed; exercised if company extended; declined if company chose not to extend; expired if deadline passed without action.']
  option_periods_count varchar [note: 'The number of option periods (typically annual) the company holds to extend the contract beyond the initial term. Common in series regular and overall deals.']
  pay_or_play_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a pay-or-play provision, guaranteeing full compensation even if the talent services are not ultimately used or the production is cancelled.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of the content (reruns, streaming, international distribution, home video). Governed by guild CBAs.']
  step_up_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The incremental increase in base compensation for each exercised option period or season renewal. Null if no step-up provision exists.']
  talent_representative_agency varchar [note: 'The name of the agency or firm representing the talent (e.g., CAA, WME, UTA, law firm name).']
  talent_representative_name varchar [note: 'The name of the talent\'s agent, manager, or attorney who negotiated on behalf of the talent.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract was terminated prior to its natural expiration. Null if contract was not terminated early.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason for early contract termination. Null if contract was not terminated early. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mutual_agreement|breach_of_contract|force_majeure|production_cancellation|talent_unavailability|company_convenience|talent_request|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of each talent engagement from initial deal memo through executed long-form contract — covering series regular deals, episodic guest agreements, overall deals, first-look deals, and crew contracts. Captures contract lifecycle stage (deal memo, countersigned, long-form executed), compensation structure (base fee, guarantees, backend participation, step-ups, pay-or-play), exclusivity and holdback restrictions, option periods with exercise status, amendment history, billing credit position, governing CBA, and document references. Source of truth for all commercial terms, obligations, and contractual modifications throughout the talent engagement lifecycle.'
}

Table talent.compensation_structure {
  compensation_structure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compensation structure record. Primary key.']
  cba_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.cba_rate_card. Business justification: compensation_structure defines detailed compensation terms for talent contracts. Currently has union_scale_tier (STRING) and guild_affiliation (STRING) but no FK to the CBA rate card that defines mini']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Compensation structures define GL account classification for talent payments. Different compensation types (base fee, residuals, backend participation) map to specific GL accounts for expense recognit']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent talent contract to which this compensation structure is attached.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Business affairs employees modify compensation structures. Required for audit trails, approval workflows, financial controls, and compliance tracking. Column modified_by_user is denormalized employee ']
  backend_gross_participation_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of adjusted gross receipts or backend profits the talent is entitled to receive (e.g., 0.05 for 5% backend participation). Null if no backend participation is granted.']
  base_episode_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'The guaranteed compensation amount per episode for episodic talent (actors, writers, directors). Expressed in the contract currency.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fixed or maximum bonus amount payable when the bonus threshold is met. Expressed in the contract currency. Null if no bonus provision exists.']
  bonus_threshold_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the performance or milestone thresholds that trigger bonus payments (e.g., \'Nielsen rating above 2.0 in key demo\', \'box office exceeds $100M domestic\').']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compensation arrangement: union scale (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA minimum), over-scale (above union minimum), flat fee, episodic rate, weekly guarantee, daily rate, backend gross participation, deferred compensation, or residual-only deal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: union_scale|over_scale|flat_fee|episodic|weekly|daily|backend_participation|deferred|residual_only — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compensation structure record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts in this compensation structure are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, CAD).']
  daily_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The guaranteed daily compensation amount for talent engaged on a day-player or daily basis. Expressed in the contract currency.']
  deferred_compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount of compensation deferred to a future payment date or contingent event (e.g., series pickup, profitability milestone). Expressed in the contract currency.']
  deferred_payment_trigger varchar [note: 'The business event or milestone that triggers payment of deferred compensation (e.g., \'series_pickup\', \'profitability_threshold_met\', \'syndication_sale\', \'specific_date\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this compensation structure ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended structures or those tied to contract termination.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this compensation structure becomes effective and applicable to the talent contract.']
  exclusivity_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compensation structure includes an exclusivity provision restricting the talent from working on competing projects (True) or not (False).']
  guild_affiliation varchar [note: 'The labor union or guild governing this compensation structure: SAG-AFTRA (Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists), WGA (Writers Guild of America), DGA (Directors Guild of America), IATSE (International Alliance of Theatrical Stage Employees), or non-union.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compensation structure record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special provisions, or clarifications regarding this compensation structure that do not fit into structured fields.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'The multiplier applied to the base rate for overtime hours worked (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double-time). Null if overtime is not applicable.']
  pay_or_play_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compensation structure includes a pay-or-play provision, guaranteeing payment even if the talent is not used (True) or not (False).']
  pension_health_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage rate of gross compensation contributed to the guild pension and health plan (e.g., 0.185 for 18.5% SAG-AFTRA P&H). Used to calculate employer contributions.']
  residual_base_formula varchar [note: 'The formula or method used to calculate the residual base for reuse payments (e.g., \'initial_compensation\', \'applicable_minimum\', \'pro_rata_share\'). Defines how residuals are computed per guild rules.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of the content under this compensation structure (True) or not (False). Typically True for union deals, may be False for buyouts.']
  step_up_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The incremental compensation increase amount when the step-up trigger is met. Expressed in the contract currency. Null if no step-up provision exists.']
  step_up_trigger varchar [note: 'The condition or event that triggers an automatic increase in compensation (e.g., \'season_2_pickup\', \'episode_13_renewal\', \'ratings_threshold\'). Null if no step-up provision exists.']
  structure_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this compensation structure (e.g., \'Series Regular Season 1\', \'Guest Star Rate Card\').']
  structure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation structure: draft (under negotiation), active (in force), amended (modified after execution), superseded (replaced by a newer structure), or terminated (no longer applicable).']
  usage_rights_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the media usage rights covered by this compensation structure (e.g., \'initial broadcast only\', \'all media in perpetuity\', \'theatrical and SVOD\', \'domestic free television\'). Defines what exploitation is covered by the base compensation.']
  weekly_guarantee decimal(15,2) [note: 'The guaranteed weekly compensation amount for talent engaged on a weekly basis (e.g., series regulars, production staff). Expressed in the contract currency.']

  Note: 'Defines the detailed compensation terms attached to a talent contract — including base episode fee, weekly guarantee, daily rate, overtime multipliers, pension and health (P&H) contribution rates, residual base formula, backend gross participation percentage, deferred compensation schedule, and currency. Supports both union scale (SAG-AFTRA scale, DGA scale) and over-scale deals. Tracks step-up triggers (e.g., series pickup escalators), bonus thresholds, and pay-or-play provisions. Used by payroll and royalty workflows to calculate gross compensation and residual bases.'
}

Table talent.exclusivity_clause {
  exclusivity_clause_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exclusivity clause record. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent talent contract to which this exclusivity clause is attached. Links this clause to the overarching contractual agreement.']
  breach_incident_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of times the talent has breached this exclusivity clause. Used to track compliance history and inform enforcement decisions.']
  clause_number varchar [note: 'The business identifier or reference number assigned to this exclusivity clause within the contract document (e.g., Section 5.2, Clause EX-001). Used for legal and operational reference.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this exclusivity clause record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this exclusivity clause record became effective and enforceable within the talent contract. Represents the business event timestamp for this clause.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current enforcement state of the exclusivity clause. Active indicates the clause is fully enforceable. Suspended indicates temporary non-enforcement. Waived indicates a waiver is in effect. Expired indicates the holdback window has ended. Terminated indicates the clause has been permanently voided.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the scope and boundaries of the exclusivity restriction. Defines what activities, roles, or engagements are covered by the exclusivity clause.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'The category of exclusivity restriction imposed by this clause. Category exclusivity restricts talent from working in specific content genres or categories. Network exclusivity restricts talent from appearing on competing networks. Platform exclusivity restricts talent from appearing on competing streaming or distribution platforms. Geographic exclusivity restricts talent from working in specific territories. Time exclusivity restricts talent during specific dayparts or time windows. Project exclusivity restricts talent from working on competing projects or productions.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or territory identifiers where the exclusivity restriction applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). If empty or null, the exclusivity applies globally.']
  holdback_window_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the exclusivity or holdback restriction expires. After this date, the talent is no longer bound by the exclusivity terms of this clause. Nullable for open-ended or perpetual exclusivity.']
  holdback_window_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the exclusivity or holdback restriction becomes effective. Marks the beginning of the period during which the talent is bound by the exclusivity terms.']
  last_breach_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent breach of this exclusivity clause by the talent. Nullable if no breach has occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this exclusivity clause record was most recently updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']
  legal_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this exclusivity clause has been reviewed and approved by legal counsel. True indicates legal review is complete; False indicates pending or no review.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'The date on which legal counsel completed their review of this exclusivity clause. Nullable if legal review has not been completed.']
  legal_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the legal counsel or attorney who reviewed and approved this exclusivity clause. Used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  negotiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business affairs executive, legal counsel, or talent agent who negotiated the terms of this exclusivity clause. Used for accountability and future reference.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or context regarding this exclusivity clause. May include negotiation history, special conditions, or operational guidance.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary penalty amount (in the contract currency) that the talent must pay if they breach the exclusivity clause. Represents liquidated damages or breach compensation. Nullable if penalty is non-monetary or not specified.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required if penalty_amount is populated.']
  penalty_provision varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the financial or contractual penalties that apply if the talent breaches the exclusivity clause. May include liquidated damages amounts, contract termination rights, or other remedies available to the broadcaster.']
  permitted_carveout_activities varchar [note: 'Textual description of specific activities, roles, or engagements that are explicitly exempted or carved out from the exclusivity restriction. Examples include charity appearances, personal social media, pre-existing commitments, or non-competing content categories.']
  restricted_competitor_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of specific competitors, networks, platforms, or entities with whom the talent is prohibited from engaging during the exclusivity period. May include network names, streaming platform identifiers, production company names, or brand names.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive, legal counsel, or business unit that approved the waiver to the exclusivity clause. Used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which a granted waiver expires and the exclusivity clause becomes fully enforceable again. Nullable if the waiver is permanent or no waiver has been granted.']
  waiver_granted_date date [note: 'The date on which a waiver or exception to the exclusivity clause was officially granted. Nullable if no waiver has been granted.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a waiver or exception to this exclusivity clause has been granted by the broadcaster. True indicates a waiver is in effect; False indicates the clause is fully enforceable.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the business or legal reason for granting a waiver to the exclusivity clause. Examples include strategic partnership, talent goodwill, promotional opportunity, or force majeure.']

  Note: 'Records the specific exclusivity and holdback restrictions embedded within or appended to a talent contract. Captures exclusivity type (category exclusivity, network exclusivity, platform exclusivity, geographic exclusivity), restricted competitor list, holdback window start and end dates, permitted carve-out activities, penalty provisions for breach, and waiver history. Enables rights and scheduling teams to validate talent availability for new productions and advertising campaigns without violating contractual obligations.'
}

Table talent.appearance_schedule {
  appearance_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the appearance schedule record. Primary key for the appearance schedule entity.']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Talent appearances at broadcast facilities require facility access clearance, security badging, parking coordination, and insurance verification. Operations teams use this to manage facility access li']
  content_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_episode. Business justification: Appearance schedules for episodic content must specify exact episode for production call sheets, per-episode compensation tracking, and daily production scheduling. Critical for episodic production op']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Production coordinators/schedulers create appearance schedules. Required for scheduling accountability, workload management, audit trails, and dispute resolution. Column created_by_user is denormalize']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production (film, television episode, live broadcast, promotional event) for which the talent is scheduled.']
  rescheduled_from_appearance_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original appearance schedule record if this schedule is a rescheduled version of a previous booking.']
  resulting_media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.media_asset. Business justification: Links scheduled talent appearances (talk shows, live events, promotional shoots) to the resulting recorded media assets. Required for archival cross-reference, rights verification, and tracking which ']
  studio_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.studio_facility. Business justification: Production scheduling systems book talent into specific studios for live shows, interviews, panel discussions. Studio booking workflow requires linking talent call sheets to physical studio resources ']
  sweeps_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.sweeps_period. Business justification: Programming strategy concentrates high-profile talent appearances during Nielsen sweeps periods to maximize ratings that set advertising rates. Production schedulers coordinate talent bookings with sw']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, host, correspondent, crew member) associated with this appearance schedule.']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content title (series, film, special) associated with this appearance.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual number of hours the talent was engaged for this appearance, recorded after completion for payroll and residual calculation purposes.']
  appearance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of appearance or activity scheduled for the talent. ADR (Automated Dialogue Replacement) refers to post-production voice recording sessions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: principal_photography|adr_session|promotional_appearance|press_junket|live_broadcast|table_read|rehearsal|wardrobe_fitting|makeup_test|screen_test — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  availability_window_end date [note: 'The end date of the period during which the talent has indicated availability for scheduling.']
  availability_window_start date [note: 'The start date of the period during which the talent has indicated availability for scheduling.']
  booking_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference code assigned to this appearance schedule by the production scheduling system.']
  call_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which the talent is required to appear for the activity. Follows yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  call_time timestamp [note: 'The specific date and time when the talent must report to the location. Follows yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the appearance schedule was cancelled, such as production delays, talent unavailability, or budget constraints.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this appearance schedule was cancelled, if applicable.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appearance schedule indicating whether the booking is pending confirmation, confirmed, cancelled, rescheduled, completed, or resulted in a no-show.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this appearance schedule record was first created in the system.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The time segment of the broadcast day during which the appearance is scheduled, relevant for live broadcast appearances and promotional activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|morning|midday|afternoon|prime_time|late_night|overnight — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated number of hours the talent is expected to be engaged for this appearance, used for scheduling and compensation planning.']
  exclusivity_conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this appearance schedule conflicts with any exclusivity clauses in the talent\'s contract.']
  guild_notification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether guild notification is required for this appearance per union agreements (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA).']
  guild_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when notification was sent to the relevant guild regarding this appearance schedule.']
  hold_level varchar [note: 'Priority level of the talent booking indicating the strength of the commitment. First hold is highest priority, second and third holds are lower priority backups, first refusal gives talent right to accept or decline before offering to others.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who most recently modified this appearance schedule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this appearance schedule record was most recently updated.']
  playout_system_sync_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether this appearance schedule has been synchronized with the playout and channel management system (Ericsson MediaFirst) for live broadcast coordination.']
  playout_system_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this appearance schedule was last synchronized with the playout system.']
  release_tracking_status varchar [note: 'Status of obtaining necessary talent releases, waivers, or permissions required for the appearance, particularly for promotional and publicity activities.']
  scheduling_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional scheduling information, special requirements, or coordination notes relevant to this appearance.']
  unavailability_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the talent is unavailable during certain periods, such as personal commitments, conflicting bookings, or contractual exclusivity.']
  wrap_time timestamp [note: 'The actual or estimated date and time when the talent completed their scheduled activity and was released from the set or location.']

  Note: 'Operational schedule of talent time allocation — covering confirmed bookings, holds (first/second/third), first refusals, availability windows, and personal unavailability alongside specific call times for principal photography, ADR sessions, promotional appearances, press junkets, and live broadcast slots. Records production reference, call date/time, wrap time, location, appearance type, hold level, confirmation status, and release tracking. Integrates with production scheduling and playout systems to ensure talent availability aligns with broadcast calendars.'
}

Table talent.credit_attribution {
  credit_attribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit attribution record. Primary key.']
  role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.talent_role. Business justification: credit_attribution defines on-screen and promotional credits for a talent on a specific production. talent_role defines the role/function performed (role_name, character_name, role_category). Currentl']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.season. Business justification: Season-specific credits (e.g., season showrunner changes, guest producers) require season-level attribution for accurate metadata, season-specific residuals, and platform EPG data.']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Series-level credits (Created by, Executive Producer) require direct series link for proper attribution across all episodes, series-level residuals, and metadata distribution to platforms.']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, host, correspondent, crew member) receiving this credit.']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset (title, episode, series, production) for which this credit is attributed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this credit attribution was formally approved by all relevant parties (talent, guild, production company).']
  billing_block varchar [note: 'The section or block within the credits where this attribution appears: above title, title card, opening sequence, closing sequence, end crawl, or promotional materials.']
  billing_position varchar [note: 'The numerical position of this credit in the credit sequence. Lower numbers indicate higher prominence (e.g., 1 = first position, 2 = second position). Null indicates alphabetical or unordered placement.']
  card_size_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The size of the talent credit card relative to the title card, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 100.00 means same size as title, 75.00 means 75% of title size). Contractually mandated for high-profile talent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credit attribution record was first created in the system.']
  credit_approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval status of this credit attribution: draft (not finalized), pending approval (awaiting talent or guild review), approved (finalized and contractually binding), disputed (under arbitration), or waived (talent declined credit).']
  credit_determination_method varchar [note: 'The method by which this credit was determined: contractual (per talent agreement), guild arbitration (resolved by guild dispute process), mutual agreement (negotiated between parties), or statutory (mandated by regulation).']
  credit_duration_seconds decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum duration in seconds that the credit card must be displayed on screen, as contractually required.']
  credit_format varchar [note: 'The format in which the credit is displayed: single card (talent alone), shared card (multiple talents on one card), alphabetical (ordered alphabetically with peers), or separate card (dedicated card for this talent).']
  credit_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding this credit attribution, including special instructions, historical context, or clarifications for production and distribution teams.']
  credit_placement varchar [note: 'Specifies where the credit must appear: opening credits, closing credits, both, or promotional materials only.']
  credit_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this credit attribution was sourced (e.g., Dalet Galaxy, Rightsline, production management system).']
  credit_text varchar [note: 'The exact contractually mandated credit language or text that must appear on screen or in promotional materials (e.g., \'Directed by\', \'Written by\', \'Starring\', \'A Film By\', \'Executive Producer\').']
  credit_type varchar [note: 'The type of credit being attributed: main title (opening credits), end title (closing credits), possessory credit (e.g., \'A Film By\'), and credit, with credit, special appearance, or guest star designation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: main_title|end_title|possessory_credit|and_credit|with_credit|special_appearance|guest_star — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent has waived their contractual right to receive this credit (e.g., for creative or personal reasons).']
  credit_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for the credit waiver, if applicable (e.g., \'Creative decision\', \'Pseudonym requested\', \'Guild arbitration outcome\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this credit attribution expires or is superseded (e.g., due to contract renegotiation, credit correction, or content retirement). Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this credit attribution becomes effective and must be applied to all content distributions and promotional materials.']
  epg_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit should be displayed in the Electronic Program Guide (EPG) metadata for linear and on-demand platforms.']
  guild_affiliation varchar [note: 'The guild or union affiliation governing this credit (e.g., SAG-AFTRA, DGA, WGA, IATSE, PGA). Multiple affiliations may apply and should be comma-separated.']
  possessory_credit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a possessory credit (e.g., \'A Film By [Name]\'), which is a special contractual entitlement typically reserved for directors or executive producers with significant creative control.']
  promotional_materials_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit must appear in promotional materials (posters, trailers, advertisements) as contractually required.']
  pseudonym varchar [note: 'The pseudonym or stage name to be used in the credit if the talent does not wish to use their legal name (e.g., \'Alan Smithee\' for directors, pen names for writers).']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit attribution makes the talent eligible for residual payments (reuse payments) under guild agreements when the content is rebroadcast, syndicated, or distributed on secondary platforms.']
  streaming_metadata_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit should be included in streaming platform metadata (e.g., Netflix, Hulu, Disney+) for title cards and cast/crew information.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier that last modified this credit attribution record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credit attribution record was last modified.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier that created this credit attribution record.']

  Note: 'Defines the on-screen and promotional credit entitlements for each talent on a specific production or content asset — capturing credit type (main title, end title, possessory credit, \'and\' credit, \'with\' credit), billing position (first position, second position, alphabetical), credit card size relative to title card, credit placement (opening, closing, both), contractually mandated credit language, and any approved credit waivers. Feeds EPG metadata, digital asset packaging, and streaming platform title cards. Governed by DGA, WGA, and SAG-AFTRA credit determination rules.'
}

Table talent.residual_eligibility {
  residual_eligibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the residual eligibility record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent contract that governs the residual terms and eligibility rules.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific content asset (episode, film, program) for which residual eligibility applies.']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, host, correspondent, crew member) for whom residual eligibility is being determined.']
  buyout_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The one-time payment amount paid to the talent in lieu of ongoing residual payments for specified uses, if a buyout was applied.']
  buyout_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a residual buyout was negotiated and applied, eliminating ongoing residual payment obligations for specified uses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this residual eligibility record was first created in the system.']
