Business justification: Ad billing orders must link directly to source ad_order for order-to-invoice reconciliation, payment application, and revenue recognition. Critical for billing cycle processing and dispute resolution ']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser organization that purchased the advertising inventory.']
  agency_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Ad sales in broadcasting involve agency partners as intermediaries for commission splits, co-op advertising budgets, and consolidated billing. Agency relationship management requires partner domain li']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Ad billing order is billed to ONE billing account (advertiser or agency account). Advertisers and agencies have billing accounts in the billing domain. Need FK to link ad orders to their billing accou']
  billing_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ad orders have designated billing contacts within the broadcaster\'s billing team who coordinate with agencies on discrepancies, makegoods, and payment issues. Tracking billing contact employee is oper']
  billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_dispute. Business justification: Ad billing order can have ONE formal billing dispute raised against it. Currently stores dispute_reason and dispute_date as STRING/DATE; need FK to the formal billing_dispute record for full dispute l']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Ad campaigns specify broadcast facilities for geographic targeting, signal coverage verification, local vs. national spot differentiation, and delivery confirmation required in advertising sales and b']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Ad orders are sold against specific licensed stations. Revenue reporting, license renewal applications, and FCC ownership attribution rules require station-level revenue attribution for each order.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this billing order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Ad orders attributed to sales team or channel cost centers for sales performance tracking, cost allocation, and departmental P&L. Enables media orgs to track advertising revenue against sales team cos']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Ad orders specify the guaranteed target demographic for pricing, rate card application, and delivery measurement. Determines CPM/CPRP rates and is contractually binding for makegood triggers. Core ele']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Ad billing order results in ONE invoice (the formal billing document). Currently stores invoice_number and invoice_date as STRING/DATE; need FK to the master invoice record. Remove invoice_number and ']
  nielsen_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.nielsen_rating. Business justification: Ad orders reference expected Nielsen ratings for pricing and actual ratings for delivery verification. Drives makegood determination, billing adjustments, and advertiser reporting. Central to upfront/']
  political_ad_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.political_ad_record. Business justification: FCC requires broadcasters to maintain public inspection files linking political ad orders to disclosure records (candidate name, rate, air dates). Essential for reasonable access and equal time compli']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Ad orders must be attributed to profit centers (channels, platforms) for advertising revenue segment reporting and business unit P&L. Essential for media broadcasters tracking advertising performance ']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Ad orders must classify revenue by stream (linear TV, digital, programmatic) for revenue recognition, sales commission calculation, and advertising revenue reporting. Critical for media broadcasters w']
  sales_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising agency representing the advertiser, if applicable.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who closed this advertising order.']
  transmission_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.transmission_equipment. Business justification: Ad orders specify transmission equipment for spot delivery scheduling, makegoods tracking when equipment fails, affidavit generation, and proof-of-performance documentation required by advertisers in ']
  upfront_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.upfront_deal. Business justification: Upfront orders must link to parent deal for commitment tracking, pricing validation, audience guarantee monitoring, and upfront vs. scatter revenue classification in media sales.']
  affidavit_status varchar [note: 'Status of proof-of-broadcast affidavit documentation for this order. Affidavits provide legal confirmation that contracted spots aired as scheduled.']
  billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount invoiced to the advertiser or agency for this order, including any adjustments or makegoods.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the billing order in the revenue cycle. Tracks progression from unbilled inventory through invoicing, payment, and dispute resolution.']
  contracted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted revenue amount for the advertising order as agreed in the sales contract, before any makegoods or adjustments.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions rate applied to this order, if CPM pricing is used.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this billing order.']
  daypart_breakdown varchar [note: 'Distribution of advertising spots across broadcast dayparts (time segments of the broadcast day such as Prime Time, Early Morning, Late Night). Stored as structured text or JSON showing spot count and value per daypart.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'The last date on which advertising spots for this order were scheduled to air. Defines the end of the campaign flight period.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'The first date on which advertising spots for this order were scheduled to air. Defines the beginning of the campaign flight period.']
  grp_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost per gross rating point applied to this order, if GRP pricing is used.']
  isci_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISCI codes identifying the specific commercial creative assets that aired as part of this order. ISCI is the industry standard for uniquely identifying TV commercials.']
  makegoods_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of compensatory ad spots provided to the advertiser due to underdelivery, technical issues, or audience guarantee shortfalls.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this billing order.']
  order_date date [note: 'The date on which the advertising order was placed or finalized in the traffic system.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this billing order, calculated as billed amount minus paid amount.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount received in payment from the advertiser or agency against this billing order.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is contractually due from the advertiser or agency.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net 30 = payment due 30 days after invoice date).']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Pricing model used for this advertising order. CPM (Cost Per Mille) = cost per thousand impressions, GRP (Gross Rating Point) = reach × frequency, CPRP (Cost Per Rating Point) = cost per single rating point, flat rate = fixed campaign price, cost per spot = per-unit pricing.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this order is recognized in financial statements, per ASC 606 revenue recognition standards.']
  total_spots_aired varchar [note: 'Total number of advertising spots that successfully aired as confirmed by affidavits and playout logs.']
  total_spots_ordered varchar [note: 'Total number of advertising spots (commercial units) contracted in this order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing order record was last modified.']
  wide_orbit_order_reference varchar [note: 'External order identifier from the Wide Orbit traffic and billing system. Business key linking this billing record to the source ad sales order.']

  Note: 'Advertising billing record derived from Wide Orbit traffic system representing a finalized ad sales order ready for invoicing. Captures Wide Orbit order ID, advertiser name, agency name, campaign flight dates, total contracted amount, makegoods value, affidavit status, ISCI codes for aired spots, daypart breakdown, CPM/GRP rate basis, and billing status (unbilled, invoiced, paid, disputed). Links advertising campaign execution to the financial billing process.'
}

Table billing.billing_carriage_fee_invoice {
  billing_carriage_fee_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carriage fee invoice issued to MVPDs and vMVPDs. Primary key for this transaction header entity.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Carriage fee invoice is billed to ONE billing account (MVPD or vMVPD account). Distribution partners have billing accounts in the billing domain. Need FK to link carriage fee invoices to their billing']
  billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_dispute. Business justification: Carriage fee invoice can have ONE formal billing dispute raised against it (common in retransmission consent negotiations). Need FK to formal billing_dispute record for dispute lifecycle tracking. No ']
  carriage_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying retransmission consent or must-carry agreement governing this invoice.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carriage invoices attributed to distribution or affiliate relations cost centers for cost tracking and departmental P&L. Enables media orgs to track carriage revenue against distribution team costs.']
  distribution_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MVPD or vMVPD operator being invoiced for channel carriage fees.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Carriage fee invoice is a specialized invoice that should link to the master invoice record. Currently duplicates invoice_number, invoice_status, invoice_date; need FK to master invoice for single sou']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Carriage fees are paid BY distribution partners (MVPDs, cable operators). Linking to partner domain enables consolidated partner financial management, credit risk assessment, and cross-agreement analy']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Carriage fee invoices are high-value, complex documents prepared by specialized distribution billing analysts. Tracking preparer employee supports quality control, dispute resolution, and operational ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Carriage invoices attributed to profit centers (channels, networks) for distribution revenue segment reporting and business unit P&L. Essential for broadcasters tracking carriage performance by channe']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Carriage fee invoices need revenue stream classification for distribution revenue reporting, distinct from advertising and subscription streams. Critical for broadcasters with MVPD relationships where']
  base_carriage_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base carriage fee amount calculated as subscriber count multiplied by per-subscriber rate before adjustments.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the billing contact at the MVPD operator for invoice delivery and correspondence.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the billing contact at the MVPD operator for invoice correspondence.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this carriage fee invoice.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this carriage fee invoice.']
  blackout_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credit amount applied for geographic broadcast blackout periods where channel was unavailable to MVPD subscribers.']
  channel_lineup_description varchar [note: 'Description of the channel lineup or package included in this carriage fee invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice amount.']
  dispute_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment amount applied due to billing disputes or makegoods for service level failures.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due from the MVPD operator per contractual payment terms.']
  escalation_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment amount applied due to contractual escalation clause (e.g., annual rate increase, CPI adjustment).']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the invoice is delivered to the MVPD operator.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to this carriage fee invoice for internal reference or MVPD communication.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due per contractual payment terms.']
  per_subscriber_fee_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Contractual per-subscriber fee rate applied to calculate the total carriage fee amount.']
  retransmission_consent_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory framework governing this carriage fee: retransmission consent (negotiated) or must-carry (mandatory).']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this carriage fee invoice is recognized per ASC 606 revenue recognition standards.']
  sap_posting_reference varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial posting document reference for revenue recognition and accounts receivable.']
  subscriber_count_basis varchar [note: 'Number of MVPD subscribers used as the basis for calculating the carriage fee amount.']
  total_carriage_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total carriage fee amount invoiced after all adjustments, credits, and escalations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified in the billing system.']

  Note: 'Invoice issued to MVPDs and vMVPDs for channel carriage fees under retransmission consent or must-carry agreements. Captures MVPD operator name, channel lineup, subscriber count basis, per-subscriber fee rate, total carriage fee amount, billing period, contractual escalation clause applied, blackout adjustment credits, and SAP posting reference. Distinct from subscription invoices due to B2B wholesale billing mechanics and regulatory retransmission consent framework.'
}

Essential for media companies tracking content licensing perfo']

  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of this syndication license fee installment.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this fee is recognized under ASC 606 accounting standards.']
  revenue_recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current revenue recognition status for this syndication license fee under ASC 606.']
  total_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of installments in the payment schedule (null for lump-sum payments).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this syndication license fee record was last modified.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated withholding tax amount deducted from the fee payment.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Withholding tax rate applied to the fee amount based on licensee jurisdiction and tax treaty provisions (expressed as decimal, e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']

  Note: 'Billing record for syndication license fee payments due from content licensees (broadcast stations, streaming platforms, international distributors). Captures licensee name, licensed content title, license window (first-run, off-network, international), fee amount, payment schedule (lump sum, installment), installment due dates, currency, withholding tax rate, and Rightsline contract reference. Supports revenue recognition for content licensing under ASC 606.'
}

Table billing.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit memo. Primary key for the credit memo entity.']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_order. Business justification: Makegoods, underdelivery credits, and preemption adjustments must trace to originating ad_order for accurate credit application, revenue reversal, and advertiser account reconciliation in advertising ']
  advertising_makegood_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.makegood. Business justification: Makegood credits must reference the makegood record for audit trail, revenue reversal tracking, and reconciliation of makegood obligations with actual delivery in advertising billing.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Credit memos issued as remediation for audit findings (e.g., makegoods for closed captioning failures, political ad rate corrections) must reference the finding for audit trail and financial impact tr']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Billing account or subscription account to which the credit is applied.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign for which a makegood credit is being issued due to under-delivery. Null for non-advertising credits.']
  communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.communication_template. Business justification: Credit memo notification uses ONE communication template to notify customer of the credit. Need FK to track which template was used for audit and customer service purposes. No redundant columns to rem']
  compliance_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.compliance_finding. Business justification: Regulatory violations (FCC fines, content rating breaches) trigger credit memos to advertisers/distributors as makegoods or contractual penalties. Compliance and finance teams track which findings gen']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Credit memos issued as remediation for compliance incidents (political ad disputes, accessibility failures, content rating violations) must link to the incident for financial impact analysis and regul']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carriage agreement, syndication license, or subscription contract associated with this credit. Null if not contract-related.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Credit memos need cost center attribution for variance analysis and departmental impact tracking. Enables media orgs to track revenue adjustments and makegoods against responsible cost centers for acc']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Credit memo can reference ONE specific invoice line item (not just the entire invoice). Credits are often issued for specific line items (e.g., one ad spot that didn\'t air, one subscription month). Ne']
  original_ad_spot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_spot. Business justification: Spot-level credits for technical failures, preemptions, or affidavit discrepancies require direct ad_spot reference for precise credit calculation and makegood tracking in broadcast advertising operat']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice being credited, adjusted, or written off. Null for credits not tied to a specific invoice.']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.outage_record. Business justification: Credits for service interruptions must reference outage records for SLA breach validation, revenue impact calculation, regulatory reporting, and automated credit approval workflows in broadcast servic']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Credits issued for partner disputes (makegood spots for under-delivery, SLA breach penalties, content delivery failures) require partner attribution for dispute resolution workflows, relationship heal']
  primary_credit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the credit memo, for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Credit memos must be attributed to profit centers for accurate segment P&L and revenue adjustments. Critical for media broadcasters tracking makegoods and revenue corrections by channel or platform fo']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer, advertiser, subscriber, or carriage partner receiving the credit.']
  tertiary_credit_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the credit memo record, for audit trail purposes.']
  transmission_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.transmission_equipment. Business justification: Credits issued for equipment failures, signal outages, or missed ad spots require equipment tracking for root cause analysis, liability determination, vendor warranty claims, and insurance recovery in']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date the credit was actually applied to the customer account or invoice balance. Null if not yet applied.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was approved by authorized personnel. Required for credits above threshold amounts.']
  bad_debt_write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit memo represents a bad debt write-off for an uncollectable balance. True for write-offs, false for other credit types.']
  collection_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party collection agency to which the debt was referred, if applicable.']
  collection_agency_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original debt was referred to a collection agency before write-off. Used for tracking recovery potential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was first created in the billing system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the credit being issued, always positive. Represents the amount to be deducted from customer balance or written off.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the credit memo, used in customer communications and financial reporting. Typically follows format CM-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  credit_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit memo in the approval and application workflow.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the credit or write-off is posted in SAP S/4HANA for financial reporting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was formally issued and became effective for accounting purposes.']
  makegood_spots_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of compensatory advertising spots to be provided as makegood for under-delivered campaigns. Null for non-makegood credits.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was last modified, for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net credit amount after tax adjustments. Equals credit_amount minus tax_amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to the credit memo processing and approval.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason category for issuing the credit memo. Makegood refers to compensatory ad spots for under-delivered campaigns; billing_error for invoice corrections; service_outage for subscriber service credits; goodwill for customer retention; bad_debt_write_off for uncollectable balances.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the credit memo was issued, including specific circumstances, campaign details, or error descriptions.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount subsequently recovered from a written-off debt through collection efforts or customer payment. Null if no recovery occurred.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously written-off amount was recovered. Null if no recovery occurred.']
  revenue_recognition_impact varchar [note: 'Accounting period impact classification for revenue recognition under ASC 606. Indicates whether the credit affects current, prior, or future period revenue.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial document number for the credit memo posting, used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was posted to SAP S/4HANA general ledger for financial reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax portion of the credit amount, if applicable. Used for tax reporting and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Formal credit or write-off document issued to reduce, reverse, or write off a previously billed amount — covering makegoods for under-delivered ad campaigns, billing error corrections, subscriber service credits, carriage fee adjustments, and bad debt write-offs for uncollectable balances. Captures credit memo number, reason code (makegood, billing error, service outage, goodwill, bad_debt_write_off), original invoice reference, credit amount, currency, approval status, applied date, collection agency referral flag, recovery amount if subsequently collected, and SAP credit/bad-debt posting reference. SSOT for all billing credits, adjustments, and write-offs.'
}

Table billing.billing_dispute {
  billing_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing dispute record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.advertiser. Business justification: Advertiser disputes require direct advertiser context for resolution workflow, escalation routing, credit hold decisions, and advertiser relationship management in media sales operations.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.campaign. Business justification: Advertising disputes often center on campaign-level delivery metrics (GRP, reach, frequency). Direct campaign link enables dispute resolution teams to access performance data and audience guarantees f']
  communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.communication_template. Business justification: Billing dispute correspondence uses ONE communication template for dispute notifications and updates. Need FK to track which template was used for audit and customer service purposes. No redundant col']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Billing disputes often arise from compliance incidents (political ad rate challenges per FCC lowest unit charge rules, advertiser disputes over closed captioning failures). Links financial and complia']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo document issued to resolve the dispute, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or team member assigned to investigate and resolve the dispute.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice being disputed. Links to the invoice that triggered this dispute.']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.outage_record. Business justification: Billing disputes often stem from service outages; linking enables automated validation of customer claims against actual downtime records, SLA breach confirmation, and dispute resolution acceleration ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the entity (customer, advertiser, MVPD, licensee) who raised the dispute. Polymorphic reference resolved via disputing_party_type.']
  write_off_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.write_off. Business justification: Billing dispute can be resolved via ONE write-off decision (when dispute is valid and amount is written off). Need FK to write_off for dispute resolution tracking. No redundant columns to remove.']
  assigned_resolver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or team assigned to resolve the dispute, for operational tracking.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for the disputing party, used for dispute correspondence.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for the disputing party, used for dispute resolution communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the data system, for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual monetary credit amount issued to the disputing party as resolution, in the invoice currency.']
  credit_memo_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit memo was issued as part of the dispute resolution (True) or not (False).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the dispute as provided by the disputing party, capturing the specific issue and context.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute: open (newly raised), under_review (being investigated), resolved (closed with outcome), escalated (sent to higher authority), withdrawn (retracted by disputing party), rejected (dispute denied without resolution).']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute reason: amount_error (billing calculation error), service_not_received (content or service not delivered), makegood_claim (compensatory ad spot request), duplicate_charge (same charge billed twice), rate_discrepancy (incorrect pricing applied), contract_dispute (terms interpretation disagreement).']
  disputed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount being contested by the disputing party, in the invoice currency.']
  disputing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or individual who raised the dispute, for reporting and communication purposes.']
  disputing_party_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity raising the dispute: subscriber (end consumer), advertiser (ad campaign buyer), MVPD (Multichannel Video Programming Distributor), licensee (content license holder), distributor (content distribution partner).']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times the dispute has been escalated (0 = no escalation, 1 = first escalation to supervisor, 2 = second escalation to manager, etc.).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the dispute was escalated beyond the initial resolver, capturing complexity or policy exception requirements.']
  external_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from an external system (e.g., CRM case ID, legal case number) if the dispute is tracked in multiple systems.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last updated the dispute record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record, for audit trail and change tracking.']
  open_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was formally raised and entered into the dispute management system.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute was created in the system, used for SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the dispute based on amount, customer tier, or contractual importance: low (routine), medium (standard), high (significant customer or amount), critical (executive escalation or legal risk).']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the dispute, used in customer communications and tracking. Format: DSP-YYYYMMDD sequence.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was formally closed with a final outcome.']
  resolution_deadline_date date [note: 'Target date by which the dispute must be resolved per Service Level Agreement (SLA) or contractual terms.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting the investigation findings, resolution rationale, and actions taken by the resolver.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the dispute resolution: full_credit (entire disputed amount credited), partial_credit (portion credited), no_adjustment (dispute rejected, no change), makegood_issued (compensatory ad spot provided), contract_amendment (terms renegotiated), escalated_unresolved (sent to legal or executive review).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute was resolved, used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying cause of the dispute for process improvement and trend analysis: billing_system_error (system calculation bug), data_entry_error (manual input mistake), rate_card_error (incorrect pricing configuration), service_delivery_failure (content or ad not delivered), contract_misinterpretation (terms unclear or misread), customer_misunderstanding (customer expectation mismatch).']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute resolution exceeded the contractual SLA deadline (True) or was resolved within SLA (False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which the dispute originated: zuora (subscription billing disputes), wide_orbit (advertising billing disputes), sap_s4hana (financial disputes), salesforce (CRM-initiated disputes), manual_entry (disputes entered directly by billing operations).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created the dispute record in the billing system.']

  Note: 'Formal billing dispute record raised by a subscriber, advertiser, MVPD, or licensee contesting an invoice or charge. Captures dispute reference number, dispute type (amount error, service not received, makegood claim, duplicate charge, rate discrepancy), disputed amount, dispute open date, resolution deadline, assigned resolver, resolution status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution outcome, credit memo issued flag, and root cause category. Drives the dispute management workflow.'
}

Table billing.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the refund record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account receiving the refund.']
  billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute record that triggered this refund, if the refund is the result of a resolved dispute.']
  communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.communication_template. Business justification: Refund notification uses ONE communication template to notify customer of the refund. Need FK to track which template was used for audit and customer service purposes. No redundant columns to remove.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Refund can be triggered by ONE credit memo. Credit memos often result in refunds when the credit amount exceeds the outstanding balance. Need direct FK from refund to credit_memo (not just via billing']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this refund is associated with, if applicable.']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.outage_record. Business justification: Refunds for service failures require outage documentation for approval justification, financial impact reporting, root cause tracking, and regulatory compliance in subscriber service refund processing']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment transaction that is being refunded.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: Refund is issued to ONE payment method (refund destination). Currently stores payment_method_last_four as STRING; need FK to the stored payment_method record. Remove payment_method_last_four as it can']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Refund processing requires employee authorization for financial control. Tracking which billing staff member processed each refund is standard practice for fraud prevention and audit compliance in med']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who is receiving the refund.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount being refunded to the subscriber or account, before any adjustments or fees.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved for processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the refund was initiated (web portal, mobile app, call center, retail location, automated system, batch process).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  expected_settlement_date date [note: 'Projected date when the refund is expected to be settled and funds returned to the subscriber. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any processing fee deducted from the refund amount, if applicable (e.g., payment gateway fees, administrative charges).']
  is_chargeback_related boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this refund is related to a payment chargeback or dispute initiated by the subscriber through their financial institution.']
  is_partial_refund boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a partial refund (true) or a full refund (false) of the original payment amount.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount actually returned to the subscriber or account after deducting any processing fees (refund_amount - refund_fee).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the refund transaction, used by customer service and finance teams.']
  processing_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was submitted for payment processing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the refund (e.g., CANCEL, DISPUTE, OVERPAY, SVCFAIL, GOODWILL).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the refund was issued, providing business context beyond the reason code.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is being returned to the subscriber: back to original payment instrument, check, account credit, wire transfer, or other mechanism. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original_payment_method|credit_card|bank_transfer|check|account_credit|wire_transfer|paypal|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the refund, used in customer communications and financial reconciliation. Format: RFD-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction in the processing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|processing|completed|failed|cancelled|reversed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund based on the revenue stream being reversed: subscription billing, advertising billing, carriage fee, syndication license fee, overpayment, or other.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was originally requested by the subscriber or initiated by the system. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a completed refund was reversed or cancelled, if applicable.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial document number for the refund posting in the general ledger, used for financial reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was posted to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the refund funds were actually settled and returned to the subscriber or account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  wideorbit_refund_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Wide Orbit traffic and billing system for refunds originating from advertising revenue, used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  zuora_refund_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Zuora subscription billing system for refunds originating from subscription revenue, used for system integration and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Record of a monetary refund issued back to a subscriber or billing account following an overpayment, cancellation, or resolved dispute. Captures refund amount, currency, refund method (original payment method, check, account credit), refund reason, originating payment reference, approval status, processing date, settlement date, and SAP financial posting reference. Distinct from credit memos (which reduce invoice balances) — refunds represent actual cash outflows.'
}

Table billing.dunning_event {
  dunning_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dunning event record. Primary key.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delinquent billing account against which this dunning action was taken.']
  billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_dispute. Business justification: Dunning event can trigger or relate to ONE billing dispute. Customers often raise disputes during dunning process. Need FK to billing_dispute for dispute lifecycle tracking. No redundant columns to re']
  communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the message template used for this dunning communication.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who manually intervened or handled this dunning event, if applicable.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice that triggered this dunning action, if applicable.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction record if a payment retry was successful.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method used for retry attempts, if applicable.']
  refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.refund. Business justification: Dunning event can result in ONE refund (e.g., when dunning reveals an overpayment or billing error). Need FK to refund for dunning outcome tracking. No redundant columns to remove.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber associated with the delinquent account.']
  write_off_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.write_off. Business justification: Dunning event can result in ONE write-off decision (final step in dunning process when account is uncollectable). Need FK to write_off for dunning outcome tracking. No redundant columns to remove.']
  account_balance_at_action decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance on the billing account at the time the dunning action was taken.']
  account_termination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing account was terminated as a result of this dunning action.']
  account_termination_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing account was terminated, if applicable.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dunning action was executed.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of dunning action taken against the delinquent account.']
  amount_recovered decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount successfully recovered as a result of this dunning action, if any.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score indicating the likelihood of subscriber churn as a result of this dunning action (0-100 scale).']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the dunning communication was delivered to the subscriber.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this dunning event.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'Number of days the account has been delinquent at the time of this dunning action.']
  dunning_step_number varchar [note: 'Sequential step number in the dunning workflow (e.g., 1 for first reminder, 2 for second reminder, 3 for suspension warning, 4 for service suspension).']
  next_dunning_step_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next dunning action if this step did not resolve the delinquency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by agents or system regarding this dunning action.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result or outcome of the dunning action taken. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: payment_received|payment_failed|promise_to_pay|dispute_raised|no_response|account_suspended|account_terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recipient_address varchar [note: 'Email address, phone number, or postal address to which the dunning communication was sent.']
  response_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber responded to this dunning action (e.g., made a payment, contacted support, updated payment method).']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber responded to the dunning action, if applicable.']
  retry_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated payment retry attempts made for this dunning event, if action type is payment retry.']
  service_suspension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriber services were suspended as a result of this dunning action.']
  service_suspension_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when subscriber services were suspended, if applicable.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'Source system that generated or recorded this dunning event (e.g., Zuora, Wide Orbit, SAP S/4HANA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning event record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Record of a dunning action taken against a delinquent billing account — automated payment retry, reminder notice, suspension warning, or service suspension. Captures dunning step number, dunning action type (email reminder, SMS alert, payment retry, service suspension, account termination), action date, account balance at time of action, retry attempt count, communication channel, response received flag, and outcome. Supports subscriber churn prevention and revenue recovery workflows.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition_schedule {
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition schedule entry. Primary key for ASC 606 revenue recognition tracking.']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_order. Business justification: Order-level revenue recognition schedules for ASC 606 compliance require direct ad_order link to track performance obligations, flight dates, and deferred revenue by advertising order.']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules for content licensing deals track which titles are amortized over contract periods for ASC 606 compliance, deferred revenue tracking, content asset valuation, and perform']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue schedules need cost center attribution for internal reporting and cost allocation. Enables media orgs to track deferred revenue and recognition timing by department or business unit for manage']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Multi-year carriage fee agreements require revenue recognition schedules for upfront payments or minimum guarantees. Links recognition schedules to distribution agreements for carriage fee amortizatio']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: ASC 606 revenue recognition schedules track how invoice revenue is recognized over time across multiple accounting periods. Multi-period contracts generate invoices that have associated recognition sc']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedule can be triggered by ONE payment (payment timing affects ASC 606 revenue recognition). Need FK to payment for revenue recognition audit trail. No redundant columns to remov']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules (ASC 606 compliance) are prepared by revenue accounting staff. Tracking preparer employee (replacing text field approved_by) supports audit trail, compliance verification']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue schedules must be attributed to profit centers for segment-level deferred revenue and revenue recognition reporting. Critical for media companies tracking deferred revenue by channel, platform']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules must classify by revenue stream for ASC 606 compliance and deferred revenue tracking by category. Essential for media companies with multi-year content licensing or subsc']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty receiving the service or content (subscriber, advertiser, MVPD, syndication buyer). Links to the customer master data for reporting and analytics.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.vendor_contract. Business justification: Revenue recognition for multi-year technology service contracts requires contract reference for ASC 606 compliance, performance obligation tracking, milestone-based recognition, and deferred revenue c']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for any modification to the original recognition schedule (e.g., \'Contract amendment - extended term by 6 months\', \'Makegood issued for underdelivered GRPs\', \'Early termination - subscriber cancellation\', \'Price adjustment - volume discount applied\'). Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state for the revenue recognition schedule. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties. Pending Approval (awaiting finance review), Approved (authorized for recognition posting), Rejected (requires correction or additional documentation).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule was approved for posting. Part of the SOX audit trail for revenue recognition controls.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'High-level business division or operating segment responsible for the revenue (e.g., Streaming Services, Linear Broadcasting, Content Licensing, Advertising Sales). Used for executive reporting and segment disclosure.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the contract (e.g., subscription number, license agreement number, upfront deal code). Used for reconciliation with Zuora, Rightsline, and Wide Orbit systems.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue-generating contract. Determines recognition method and accounting treatment. Subscription (SVOD recurring), Syndication License (content resale), Upfront Ad Deal (advance advertising commitment), Carriage Agreement (MVPD distribution fee), Transactional VOD (TVOD pay-per-view), Sponsorship (branded content deal).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue recognized to date from contract inception through the current reporting period. Increases each period as revenue is earned. Used for deferred revenue balance calculation and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value and recognized amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Used for multi-currency revenue reporting and consolidation.']
  current_period_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount recognized in the current accounting period (month or quarter). Posted to the SAP revenue account. May differ from monthly recognized amount for usage-based or milestone-based contracts.']
  deferred_revenue_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unearned revenue balance on the contract. Calculated as total contract value minus cumulative recognized amount. Represents the liability on the balance sheet under ASC 606. Decreases as revenue is recognized over time.']
  last_recognition_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revenue recognition posting. Used to track recognition progress and identify schedules requiring current-period processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule record was last updated. Tracks changes to recognition amounts, dates, or status for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  monthly_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard monthly revenue amount to be recognized under straight-line method. Calculated as total contract value divided by recognition period months. May vary for usage-based or milestone-based contracts.']
  next_recognition_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next revenue recognition posting. Used by automated revenue recognition jobs to process monthly or quarterly recognition batches.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or audit notes related to the revenue recognition schedule (e.g., \'Upfront deal with makegood provision\', \'Syndication license with holdback period\', \'Early termination - prorated final month\').']
  performance_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the performance obligation under ASC 606 (e.g., \'Provide streaming access to content library for 12 months\', \'Deliver 52 episodes of syndicated series\', \'Deliver 1000 GRPs in Q4 upfront commitment\'). Documents the basis for revenue recognition timing.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition is scheduled to complete. Corresponds to the contract expiration date or end of the performance obligation period. Null for evergreen contracts until termination notice.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition method applied to this contract. Straight-Line (equal monthly recognition for subscriptions), Milestone-Based (triggered by delivery events for syndication), Usage-Based (actual consumption for AVOD/TVOD), Proportional Performance (percentage-of-completion for production contracts), Point-in-Time (immediate recognition for transactional sales).']
  recognition_period_months varchar [note: 'Total number of months over which revenue will be recognized. Calculated from recognition start date to end date. Used for straight-line recognition calculations and forecasting.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition begins. Corresponds to the contract effective date or service commencement date under ASC 606. Marks the beginning of the performance obligation period.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue recognition schedule. Scheduled (future start date), In Progress (actively recognizing), Completed (fully recognized), Suspended (temporarily paused), Terminated (contract cancelled early), Adjusted (modified due to contract amendment or makegood).']
  sap_deferred_revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) liability account code in SAP S/4HANA where deferred revenue balance is maintained. Decreases as revenue is recognized and transferred to the revenue account.']
  sap_revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA where recognized revenue is posted. Maps to the appropriate revenue account based on contract type and business unit (e.g., subscription revenue, advertising revenue, licensing revenue).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated the contract and revenue schedule. Zuora (subscription billing), Wide Orbit (ad sales and trafficking), Rightsline (rights and royalties), Salesforce (ad sales CRM), SAP (manual GL entries), Manual (finance team adjustments).']
  source_system_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier in the source system (e.g., Zuora subscription ID, Wide Orbit order number, Rightsline license agreement ID). Used for data lineage and reconciliation back to operational systems.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract to be recognized over the performance period. Represents the transaction price under ASC 606. Denominated in the contract currency.']

  Note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition schedule defining how and when deferred revenue from multi-period contracts (subscriptions, syndication licenses, upfront ad deals) is recognized into earned revenue. Captures contract reference, total contract value, recognition start date, recognition end date, recognition method (straight-line, milestone, usage-based), monthly recognized amount, cumulative recognized amount, deferred balance, and SAP revenue account posting reference. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA revenue accounting module.'
}

Table billing.tax_record {
  tax_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax calculation and reporting record. Primary key for the tax_record product.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Tax record can apply to ONE credit memo (tax on credits or tax reversals). Credit memos have tax implications that need to be tracked separately from invoice tax. Need FK to credit_memo for tax compli']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing invoice transaction to which this tax record applies. Links tax calculation to the parent billing document.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Tax record can be calculated at invoice line level (line-level tax calculation). Tax_record has tax_line_number suggesting line-level detail. Need FK to invoice_line for granular tax tracking and comp']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Tax record can be associated with ONE payment (tax withholding on payments, especially for international syndication payments). Need FK to payment for tax withholding tracking and compliance. No redun']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax records are prepared by tax accounting staff for compliance reporting and remittance. Tracking preparer employee supports audit trail, tax authority inquiries, and operational oversight in broadca']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Tax records for international syndication (withholding tax, VAT) may require regulatory filing references for cross-border compliance (e.g., IRS Form 1042, EU VAT MOSS filings). Links tax and regulato']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the taxable and tax amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, JPY).']
  customer_tax_classification varchar [note: 'Tax classification of the customer for determining applicable tax treatment. Business (B2B), Individual (B2C), Government, Non-Profit, Exempt Organization.']
  customer_tax_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s tax identification number (TIN, VAT number, GST number, EIN, etc.) used for tax reporting and validation. Format varies by jurisdiction.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax record is under dispute with the tax authority or customer (True = disputed, False = not disputed).']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the tax dispute (e.g., Incorrect jurisdiction, Rate disagreement, Exemption certificate rejected, Nexus challenge). Populated when dispute_flag is True.']
  nexus_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where tax nexus is established (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA, CAN).']
  nexus_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the business has established tax nexus (physical or economic presence triggering tax obligation). Critical for US sales tax and Canadian GST/PST compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, manual adjustments, or audit notes related to this tax calculation.']
  remittance_date date [note: 'Date when the tax amount was remitted (paid) to the tax authority. Null if remittance_status is pending or disputed.']
  remittance_reference_number varchar [note: 'Payment reference or confirmation number from the tax authority acknowledging receipt of the tax remittance. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of tax payment to the tax authority. Pending (not yet paid), Remitted (paid in full), Partially Remitted (partial payment made), Disputed (under review or appeal), Waived (exempted or forgiven).']
  reverse_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reverse charge mechanism applies (True = customer is responsible for self-assessing and remitting VAT/GST, False = supplier charges and remits tax). Common in EU cross-border B2B transactions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this tax record (e.g., Zuora, Wide Orbit, SAP S/4HANA FI, Avalara AvaTax). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_tax_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this tax record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the originating system.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount for this jurisdiction and tax type (taxable_amount × tax_rate). Denominated in the invoice currency.']
  tax_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental tax authority responsible for collection and enforcement (e.g., IRS, HMRC, California Department of Tax and Fee Administration, Direction Générale des Finances Publiques).']
  tax_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the tax amount. Automated (system-calculated), Manual (finance team entry), Third-Party Service (Avalara, Vertex, TaxJar), Override (manual adjustment to automated calculation).']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Business categorization of the taxable item or service (e.g., Subscription Services, Advertising Services, Content Licensing, Equipment Rental, Digital Downloads). Used for tax rate determination and reporting segmentation.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the tax exemption certificate on file, if applicable. Required for audit trail when exemption is claimed.']
  tax_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tax exemption was applied to this transaction (True = exemption applied, False = no exemption).']
  tax_exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or regulatory basis for the tax exemption (e.g., Non-profit organization, Government entity, Educational institution, Resale certificate, Export transaction).']
  tax_filing_due_date date [note: 'The statutory due date for filing the tax return that includes this transaction, based on the tax jurisdiction and filing period.']
  tax_filing_period varchar [note: 'The tax reporting period to which this transaction applies, formatted as YYYY-Q# for quarterly or YYYY-M## for monthly (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-M03). Aligns with jurisdiction-specific filing schedules.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction (country, state, province, county, or municipality) where the tax obligation arises. Format: ISO country code optionally followed by subdivision code (e.g., US-CA, GB, FR-75).']
  tax_jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., California, United Kingdom, Paris) for human-readable reporting and compliance documentation.']
  tax_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number for this tax record within the parent invoice, supporting multiple tax jurisdictions or tax types per invoice.']
  tax_point_date date [note: 'The date when the tax liability arises according to tax regulations (also known as time of supply). May differ from transaction_date based on jurisdiction-specific rules for services, advance payments, or continuous supplies.']
  tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Tax rate applied to the taxable amount, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0825 for 8.25%). Supports precision to six decimal places for complex multi-jurisdictional calculations.']
  tax_treatment_code varchar [note: 'Internal or regulatory code defining the specific tax treatment applied (e.g., standard rate, reduced rate, zero-rated, exempt, out-of-scope). Maps to jurisdiction-specific tax rules.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Type of tax applied to the transaction. Sales Tax (US state/local), VAT (Value-Added Tax, EU/international), GST (Goods and Services Tax), Withholding Tax (international syndication/licensing), Excise Tax (specific content types), Use Tax (cross-border digital services).']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice amount subject to taxation in this jurisdiction, after applying exemptions and exclusions. Denominated in the invoice currency.']
  third_party_tax_service varchar [note: 'Name of the external tax calculation service provider if third-party method was used (e.g., Avalara AvaTax, Vertex Cloud, TaxJar, Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE).']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The date of the underlying billing transaction that triggered the tax calculation. Used to determine applicable tax rates and rules in effect at the time of transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tax calculation and reporting record associated with a billing transaction — capturing applicable tax jurisdiction, tax type (VAT, GST, sales tax, withholding tax), tax rate applied, taxable amount, tax amount, tax exemption certificate reference, nexus state/country, tax authority, filing period, and remittance status. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance for subscriber billing (CCPA, GDPR jurisdictions) and international syndication license fee withholding.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment allocation record. Primary key.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Payment allocation can apply to ONE credit memo (not just invoices). Payments can be allocated to credit memos to settle negative balances. Need FK to credit_memo for complete payment allocation track']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice receiving the payment allocation. Links to the invoice record that is being settled or partially paid by this allocation.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction being allocated. Links to the payment record that is being distributed across one or more invoices.']
  primary_payment_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person or automated process that created this allocation. Used for audit trails, accountability, and troubleshooting allocation errors. May be system account for automated allocations.']
  refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.refund. Business justification: Payment allocation can be reversed by ONE refund. When a refund is issued, the original payment allocation needs to be tracked for audit trail. Need FK to refund for allocation reversal tracking. No r']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The financial accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which this allocation is posted. Determines which month/quarter/year the revenue and cash receipt are recognized. Used for period close, financial reporting, and revenue assurance.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional adjustment applied during allocation, such as currency conversion differences, rounding adjustments, or billing corrections. Can be positive or negative. Used for reconciliation and variance analysis.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount from the payment that is allocated to this specific invoice. For partial payments, this represents the portion applied to this invoice. For full settlements, this equals the invoice outstanding balance. Critical for accounts receivable aging and revenue assurance calculations.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business date when the payment allocation was executed and applied to the invoice. This date determines the accounting period for revenue recognition and accounts receivable aging. Critical for financial reporting and compliance with ASC 606.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The business rule or algorithm used to determine this allocation. Manual indicates user-specified allocation; Automatic indicates system-applied based on aging rules; Prorated indicates proportional distribution across multiple invoices; FIFO (First In First Out) indicates oldest invoice paid first; LIFO (Last In First Out) indicates newest invoice paid first; Specific indicates customer-directed allocation to particular invoice.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the payment allocation record. Format: PA-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where YYYYMMDD is allocation date and NNNN is sequence number.']
  allocation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order number for this allocation within the parent payment transaction. When a single payment is split across multiple invoices, this field indicates the application order. Used for FIFO/LIFO allocation logic and audit trails.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment allocation. Pending indicates allocation is awaiting confirmation; Applied indicates allocation has been posted to accounts receivable; Reversed indicates allocation has been undone; Voided indicates allocation was cancelled before application; Disputed indicates allocation is under review; Reconciled indicates allocation has been verified and closed.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the payment allocation was processed in the billing system. Used for audit trails and transaction sequencing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment allocation method. Full Settlement indicates payment completely satisfies invoice balance; Partial Payment indicates payment covers portion of invoice; Advance Deposit indicates payment received before invoice generation; Unapplied Credit indicates payment not yet matched to specific invoice; Overpayment indicates payment exceeds invoice amount; Prepayment indicates payment for future services.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation was approved by authorized personnel. Null if no approval required or pending approval. Used for audit trails and compliance with financial controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this payment allocation record was first created in the database. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and troubleshooting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Immutable after initial insert.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Must match the currency of both the payment and invoice for proper reconciliation.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount or settlement discount applied at the time of this allocation. Reduces the allocated amount required to satisfy the invoice balance. Common in advertising billing for prompt payment incentives.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation is currently under dispute by the customer or internal review. True if disputed; False if not disputed. Disputed allocations may be reversed or adjusted pending resolution.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Business explanation for why this allocation is disputed. Examples include incorrect invoice matching, payment amount mismatch, duplicate allocation, unauthorized payment application, or customer contest of charges. Required when dispute_flag is True.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this allocation is considered effective for revenue recognition purposes. May differ from allocation_date for backdated or future-dated allocations. Used in revenue assurance and financial period close processes.']
  gl_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA general ledger document number generated when this allocation was posted to the financial system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails between billing and finance.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date when this allocation was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA. Used for financial reconciliation between billing system and ERP. May differ from allocation_date due to batch processing or period close timing.']
  invoice_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the invoice receiving this allocation. Denormalized from invoice table for quick reference and reporting without joins. Typically matches invoice_number from the invoice product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, special instructions, or explanatory comments about this allocation. Used for documenting unusual allocation scenarios, customer requests, or manual intervention reasons.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment transaction, such as check number, wire transfer confirmation, credit card authorization code, or ACH trace number. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when this allocation was successfully reconciled between billing and financial systems. Null if reconciliation is pending or variance exists. Used for revenue assurance reporting and SOX compliance.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation has been reconciled between billing system and general ledger. Unreconciled indicates pending reconciliation; Reconciled indicates amounts match across systems; Variance indicates discrepancy requiring investigation; Under Review indicates active reconciliation in progress.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The accounting date when revenue from this allocation is recognized in financial statements. May differ from allocation_date based on revenue recognition rules for subscription services, advance payments, or deferred revenue scenarios. Critical for ASC 606 compliance.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date when this allocation was reversed or voided. Null if allocation is still active. Used for audit trails and accounts receivable reconciliation when allocations are corrected or disputed.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business explanation for why the allocation was reversed or voided. Examples include payment dispute, incorrect invoice matching, duplicate allocation, customer refund request, or billing error correction. Required when allocation_status is reversed or voided.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system that created this payment allocation record. Zuora for subscription billing allocations; Wide Orbit for advertising billing allocations; SAP for direct financial postings; Manual for user-entered allocations; Batch Import for bulk allocation uploads.']
  source_system_allocation_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this allocation in the source system (Zuora, Wide Orbit, or SAP). Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and troubleshooting integration issues.']
  unapplied_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining payment amount not allocated to any invoice after this allocation. Represents credit balance available for future invoice matching. Critical for cash application and accounts receivable management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this payment allocation record was last modified. Updated automatically on every change to track data currency and support incremental processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  writeoff_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the invoice balance written off as uncollectible at the time of this allocation. Used when accepting partial payment as final settlement. Impacts bad debt reserves and revenue recognition adjustments.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a payment to one or more invoices it partially or fully settles, enabling many-to-many payment-to-invoice matching. Captures payment reference, invoice reference, allocated amount, allocation date, allocation type (full settlement, partial payment, advance deposit, unapplied credit), and allocation status. Critical for revenue assurance and accounts receivable aging accuracy when a single payment covers multiple invoices or a payment is split across periods.'
}

Table billing.run {
  run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for run']
