  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing cycle for which this run was executed. Links to the billing cycle configuration that defines the schedule and account population.']
  communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.communication_template. Business justification: Billing run uses ONE communication template for invoice delivery (batch invoice distribution). Need FK to track which template was used for this run for audit purposes. No redundant columns to remove.']
  run_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this billing run for distribution. Null if approval not required or run not yet approved.']
  run_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or automated process that triggered this billing run. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  accounts_with_errors varchar [note: 'Count of customer accounts that failed to process successfully during this billing run. Used to identify accounts requiring manual intervention.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the billing run execution began. Used to measure processing delays and operational performance.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing run requires management approval before invoice distribution. True for high-value runs, correction runs, or runs with significant adjustments.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run was approved for invoice distribution. Null if approval not required or run not yet approved.']
  billing_date date [note: 'The effective billing date applied to all invoices generated in this run. Determines invoice date, due date calculation, and revenue recognition period.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period covered by invoices in this billing run. Defines the cutoff for usage charges and subscription calculations.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period covered by invoices in this billing run. Used for subscription proration and usage aggregation.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run finished execution, whether successfully or with errors. Null for runs still in progress or suspended.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all amounts in this billing run (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures consistent currency handling across invoices.']
  distribution_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when invoice distribution to customers completed. Null for runs still distributing or not yet started.']
  distribution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when invoice distribution to customers began. Used to track delivery SLA and customer communication timing.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Total number of errors encountered during billing run execution. Includes account processing failures, invoice generation errors, and system exceptions.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the error or issue that caused the billing run to fail. Null for successful runs. Used for root cause analysis and troubleshooting.']
  invoice_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of invoice delivery to customers. Pending runs await distribution; in-progress runs are actively sending; completed runs finished delivery; failed runs encountered distribution errors; cancelled runs were stopped.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes about this billing run. May include special instructions, manual adjustments applied, or explanations for off-cycle execution.']
  processing_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from actual start to completion of the billing run. Used for performance monitoring and capacity planning.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this billing run has been retried after failure. Used to identify chronic processing issues and trigger escalation.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Primary revenue classification for this billing run. Subscription runs bill SVOD/linear subscribers; advertising runs bill CPM/GRP campaigns; carriage runs bill MVPD distribution fees; syndication runs bill content licensing; transactional runs bill TVOD purchases.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-friendly identifier for the billing run, typically formatted as cycle-sequence (e.g., 2024-01-R001). Used for operational tracking and reporting.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing run. Scheduled runs are queued; in-progress runs are actively generating invoices; completed runs finished successfully; failed runs encountered errors; cancelled runs were aborted; suspended runs are paused pending resolution.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing run execution. Regular runs follow standard cycle schedules; off-cycle runs address mid-period changes; final runs close out terminated accounts; correction runs fix prior errors; makegood runs issue compensatory credits; adjustment runs apply manual corrections.']
  sap_posting_batch_reference varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA batch identifier for financial posting of billing run results. Links billing transactions to general ledger entries.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the billing run was scheduled to begin execution. Used for operational planning and SLA tracking.']
  total_accounts_processed varchar [note: 'Total count of customer accounts included in this billing run execution. Used for operational monitoring and reconciliation.']
  total_amount_billed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all invoice totals generated in this billing run, representing gross revenue before payments. Used for revenue assurance and financial reconciliation.']
  total_invoice_lines_generated varchar [note: 'Total count of invoice line items created across all invoices in this billing run. Used for detailed processing metrics and performance analysis.']
  total_invoices_generated varchar [note: 'Total count of invoices successfully generated during this billing run. May differ from accounts processed if some accounts had zero charges or errors.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  warning_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warnings generated during billing run execution. Warnings indicate potential issues that did not prevent invoice generation but require review.']
  wide_orbit_batch_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Wide Orbit traffic and billing system for advertising billing runs. Used for ad sales invoice reconciliation.']
  zuora_billing_run_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Zuora subscription billing system for this billing run. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a scheduled billing cycle execution — the batch process that generates invoices for all accounts in a billing cycle. Captures billing run date, billing cycle reference, run type (regular, off-cycle, final), total accounts processed, total invoices generated, total amount billed, run status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, failed), error count, and Zuora billing run ID. Enables billing operations monitoring and revenue assurance reconciliation.'
}

Table billing.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bad debt write-off record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Write-offs require management approval per financial policy. Tracking approver employee (replacing text field approving_authority) is mandatory financial control for bad debt management and supports a']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account under which the invoice was issued.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Write-offs must be attributed to cost centers for bad debt expense allocation and departmental impact tracking. Enables media orgs to track uncollectible revenue by department or business unit for acc']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Write-off can be accompanied by ONE credit memo (formal credit document for the written-off amount). Write-offs often generate credit memos for accounting purposes. Need FK to credit_memo for write-of']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that contains the uncollectable balance being written off.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Write-offs attributed to profit centers for segment-level bad debt expense and EBITDA impact. Critical for media broadcasters tracking uncollectible revenue by channel or platform for accurate segment']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or advertiser associated with the uncollectable invoice.']
  aging_bucket_at_write_off varchar [note: 'Accounts receivable aging category of the invoice at the time of write-off (e.g., current, 30 days, 60 days, 90 days, 120+ days).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the uncollectable balance being written off as bad debt.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the write-off was formally approved by the authorized authority.']
  collection_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external collection agency to which the debt was referred, if applicable.']
  collection_agency_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the uncollectable debt was referred to an external collection agency before or after the write-off decision (True/False).']
  collection_referral_date date [note: 'The date on which the debt was referred to the external collection agency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this write-off record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the write-off amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  days_outstanding_at_write_off varchar [note: 'Number of days the invoice was outstanding (from invoice date to write-off date) at the time the write-off decision was made.']
  ebitda_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this write-off has a material impact on EBITDA reporting for the period (True/False).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-01) in which the write-off was recorded for financial reporting purposes.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the write-off was recorded.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA general ledger account code to which the bad debt expense is posted (typically a bad debt expense account).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the write-off decision, approval process, or special circumstances.']
  original_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount originally invoiced before any payments, adjustments, or write-offs were applied.']
  outstanding_balance_at_write_off decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the invoice at the time the write-off decision was made.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the write-off decision (e.g., subscriber churn, advertiser insolvency, dispute settled at zero, statute of limitations, bankruptcy, fraud, uncollectable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subscriber_churn|advertiser_insolvency|dispute_settled_zero|statute_limitations|bankruptcy|fraud|uncollectable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances and business justification for the write-off decision.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount subsequently recovered from the customer after the write-off was recorded, if any partial or full recovery occurred.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'The date on which a recovery payment was received after the write-off, if applicable.']
  recovery_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA general ledger account code to which any recovered amount is posted (typically a bad debt recovery income account).']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue stream associated with the written-off invoice (e.g., subscription, advertising, carriage fee, syndication, licensing).']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which the write-off was reversed, if applicable.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this write-off has been reversed due to subsequent payment or correction (True/False).']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the write-off was reversed (e.g., customer payment received, accounting error correction).']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial document number generated when the write-off was posted to the general ledger.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the write-off transaction was posted in SAP S/4HANA for financial reporting purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this write-off record was last modified.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the bad debt write-off decision was executed and recorded in the financial system.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the write-off transaction, used for tracking and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Record of a bad debt write-off decision for an uncollectable invoice balance — capturing write-off amount, write-off date, reason code (subscriber churn, advertiser insolvency, dispute settled at zero, statute of limitations), approving authority, SAP bad debt expense account posting, collection agency referral flag, and recovery amount if subsequently collected. Supports revenue assurance, EBITDA reporting, and accounts receivable aging management.'
}

Table billing.ad_billing_reconciliation {
  ad_billing_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising billing reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  ad_billing_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising billing order that this reconciliation record is associated with.']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_order. Business justification: Reconciliation process requires direct link to ad_order to verify contracted vs. delivered spots, calculate underdelivery, and determine makegood obligations. Essential for advertising billing accurac']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser party for whom the advertising spots were delivered and billed.']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.broadcast_facility. Business justification: Spot delivery verification requires facility records for signal coverage confirmation, geographic delivery validation, affidavit generation, and proof-of-performance documentation in advertising billi']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this reconciliation record.']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Reconciliation processes verify contracted spots aired during specific programming. Teams link discrepancies and makegoods to content that carried ads for affidavit validation, dispute resolution, and']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reconciliations attributed to sales or finance cost centers for process ownership tracking and variance analysis. Enables media orgs to track advertising billing accuracy and makegood costs by respons']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing operations user who performed and approved the reconciliation process.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Ad billing reconciliation results in ONE final invoice (after reconciling ordered vs. aired spots and makegoods). Need FK to the invoice generated after reconciliation. No redundant columns to remove.']
  nielsen_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.nielsen_rating. Business justification: Reconciliation process compares contracted audience guarantees against actual Nielsen ratings delivered to calculate under-delivery, determine makegood spots, and adjust billing. Direct operational de']
  original_ad_billing_reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_billing_reconciliation (original_ad_billing_reconciliation_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Reconciliations attributed to profit centers for segment-level revenue assurance and makegood tracking. Critical for media broadcasters tracking advertising delivery accuracy and revenue adjustments b']
  sales_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising agency that placed the order on behalf of the advertiser, if applicable.']
  transmission_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.transmission_equipment. Business justification: Reconciliation of ordered vs. aired spots requires equipment logs for discrepancy investigation, makegood determination, equipment failure tracking, and advertiser dispute resolution in broadcast adve']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net adjustment amount applied to the billing due to makegoods, credits, or other reconciliation adjustments.']
  affidavit_count varchar [note: 'The total number of proof-of-broadcast affidavits associated with this reconciliation record, documenting actual spot delivery.']
  affidavit_verification_status varchar [note: 'The status of affidavit verification indicating whether proof-of-broadcast documentation has been validated and accepted.']
  contracted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the advertising order as originally contracted, before any adjustments or makegoods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record.']
  delivered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of advertising spots that were successfully delivered and verified through affidavits.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date when the dispute was formally raised by the advertiser or agency regarding this reconciliation.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation record is currently under dispute by the advertiser or agency.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for dispute if the reconciliation is contested by the advertiser or agency.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the advertising campaign flight period during which spots were scheduled to air.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the advertising campaign flight period during which spots were scheduled to air.']
  makegood_spots_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of compensatory advertising spots provided to the advertiser to remedy missed or underdelivered spots.']
  makegood_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the makegood spots provided as compensation, calculated at the original contracted rate.']
  net_billable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount billable to the advertiser after all reconciliation adjustments, makegoods, and credits have been applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding the reconciliation process, special circumstances, or resolution details.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when the reconciliation process was completed and the advertising delivery was verified against the billing order.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for this reconciliation record, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation process indicating whether the advertising delivery has been verified and settled.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the reconciliation record was finalized, used for audit trail and settlement timing.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'The type of reconciliation record indicating whether this is a standard delivery verification, makegood settlement, credit issuance, or billing adjustment.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when the reconciliation dispute was resolved and the final settlement was agreed upon.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date when the advertising revenue from this reconciliation is recognized in the financial system per ASC 606 standards.']
  sap_posting_reference varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA document number or posting reference for the financial transaction associated with this reconciliation.']
  total_spots_aired varchar [note: 'The actual number of advertising spots that were successfully broadcast and verified through affidavits.']
  total_spots_missed varchar [note: 'The number of ordered advertising spots that failed to air as scheduled, requiring makegood remediation.']
  total_spots_ordered varchar [note: 'The total number of advertising spots that were contracted and ordered for this campaign flight.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation record was last modified or updated.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between contracted and delivered advertising value, calculated as (delivered - contracted) / contracted * 100.']
  wide_orbit_order_reference varchar [note: 'External order identifier from the Wide Orbit traffic and billing system for cross-system reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Consolidated advertising billing reconciliation record combining proof-of-broadcast affidavits and compensatory makegood spots for advertising revenue settlement — the billing-side view of ad delivery confirmation and remediation'
}

Table billing.communication_template {
  communication_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for communication_template']
  parent_communication_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on communication_template (parent_communication_template_id)']
  parent_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this template, enabling version history tracking. Null for initial versions.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template requires management or compliance approval before it can be activated.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this template for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this template was approved for use.']
  attachment_file_name_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern or template for generating attachment file names. May contain merge field placeholders.']
  attachment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template requires an attachment (such as an invoice PDF or payment receipt) to be included when sent.']
  bcc_recipients varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of email addresses to be blind copied on communications generated from this template.']
  body_content varchar [note: 'Main content body of the communication template. May contain HTML markup and merge field placeholders for dynamic data insertion.']
  cc_recipients varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of email addresses to be copied on communications generated from this template.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel through which the communication is sent to the recipient.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the communication for regulatory compliance purposes (e.g., CAN-SPAM, GDPR, TCPA).']
  content_format varchar [note: 'Format type of the template content defining how the body content is structured and rendered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this template record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this communication template is no longer active. Null indicates no end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this communication template becomes active and available for use.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 country code) indicating the language of the template content.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this template was most recently used to generate a communication.']
  merge_fields varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of merge field placeholders available in this template for dynamic data insertion (e.g., customer_name, invoice_number, amount_due).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this template record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this template record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments about the template for administrative reference.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether recipients can opt out of receiving communications generated from this template.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for communications sent using this template, affecting delivery queue and recipient notification settings.']
  reply_to_email varchar [note: 'Email address to which recipient replies are directed.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that communications generated from this template must be retained for compliance and audit purposes.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address used as the sender for email communications generated from this template.']
  sender_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the sender shown to recipients in the communication.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the communication template indicating whether it is available for use.']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Subject line or title used for email and portal communications. May contain merge field placeholders.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the template used in system integrations and references.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the communication template.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the communication template by its business purpose within billing operations.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event or condition that triggers the use of this communication template (e.g., invoice_generated, payment_overdue, dispute_opened).']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this template has been used to generate communications.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the template, following semantic versioning (major.minor format) to track template revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this template record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for communication_template. Referenced by communication_template_id.'
}

Table billing.product {
  product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for product']
  parent_product_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on product (parent_product_id)']
  base_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard list price for the product before any discounts, promotions, or volume adjustments. Expressed in the default currency (USD).']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the product is billed to customers: one-time (single charge), daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or usage-based (billed based on consumption).']
  billing_system varchar [note: 'Source billing system that processes transactions for this product: Zuora (subscription billing), Wide Orbit (advertising billing), SAP S/4HANA (financial posting), legacy (older systems), or manual (manually invoiced).']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Content rating classification for media products, indicating age-appropriateness and content warnings. Follows MPAA (film) and TV Parental Guidelines standards. NR indicates not rated or not applicable.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the product, used for internal financial reporting and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the product pricing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the product, including features, benefits, and target audience. Used in marketing materials and billing communications.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the product is discontinued and no longer available for new sales. Null for products with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the product becomes available for sale and billing. Products cannot be sold before this date.']
  genre varchar [note: 'Primary genre or content category for media products (e.g., Drama, Comedy, News, Sports, Documentary, Reality, Kids). Used for content classification and recommendation engines.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which revenue from this product is posted in SAP S/4HANA financial system.']
  is_bundled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is sold as part of a bundle or package. True if bundled, False if sold standalone.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is offered as a promotional or limited-time offer. True if promotional, False if standard offering.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is subject to sales tax, VAT, or other consumption taxes. True if taxable, False if tax-exempt.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  last_price_change_date date [note: 'Date when the base price was last modified. Used for pricing history tracking and customer communication.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the product was first introduced to the market. Used for product lifecycle analytics and reporting.']
  minimum_commitment_period_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract commitment period in months required for the product. Null if no minimum commitment.']
  pci_dss_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product involves payment card processing and falls under PCI DSS compliance scope. True if in scope, False if out of scope.']
  platform_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms where the product is available (e.g., iOS, Android, Web, Roku, Apple TV, Fire TV, Smart TV). Used for distribution planning and customer support.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the product by distribution channel: linear TV (broadcast/cable), streaming (OTT/SVOD), digital (web/mobile), audio (radio/podcast), print (magazine), bundle (multi-service package), or ancillary (merchandise/licensing).']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the product, used across billing systems and customer communications.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product as displayed to customers and used in billing statements.']
  product_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for managing the product lifecycle, pricing, and strategy.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the product by revenue model: subscription (recurring subscriber fees), advertising (ad inventory sales), carriage fee (distribution fees from cable/satellite operators), syndication (content licensing), pay-per-view (one-time event purchases), or transactional (on-demand rentals/purchases).']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center to which product revenue and costs are allocated for P&L reporting and business unit performance measurement.']
  requires_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product requires a formal contract or subscription agreement. True if contract required, False if available without contract.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition treatment for the product: point-in-time (recognize at delivery), over-time (recognize ratably over contract period), proportional (recognize based on performance obligations), or deferred (recognize upon specific milestone).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which the product is distributed: direct (direct sales team), retail (physical stores), online (e-commerce), partner (third-party distributors), affiliate (affiliate marketing), or call_center (telesales).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product: active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), discontinued (permanently retired), pending_launch (approved but not yet available), or sunset (scheduled for retirement).']
  target_market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment the product is designed for: consumer (individual subscribers), business (small/medium business), enterprise (large corporations), government (public sector), education (schools/universities), or nonprofit (charitable organizations).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to determine applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST rates for the product. Integrates with tax calculation engines.']
  trial_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for free trial period offered for the product. Null if no trial period available.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for product. Referenced by product_id.'
}

Table partner.partner_partner {
  partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner_partner data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the partner entity. Primary key for the partner master record.']
  parent_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent partner entity in cases where this partner is a subsidiary or division of a larger corporate entity. Enables tracking of corporate hierarchies and consolidated relationships. Null for top-level parent entities.']
  annual_content_volume_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual annual volume of content hours acquired from or distributed through this partner. Used for capacity planning, content pipeline forecasting, and partner performance evaluation.']
  annual_revenue_contribution_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual annual revenue contribution from this partner relationship in USD. Includes content licensing fees, distribution revenue share, advertising revenue, or other monetization streams. Used for partner valuation and ROI analysis.']
  content_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary content genres, formats, or categories that the partner specializes in producing or distributing. Examples include scripted drama, unscripted reality, sports, news, children\'s programming, documentary, or multi-genre. Supports content acquisition strategy and partner matching.']
  contract_renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for master agreement renewal or renegotiation with this partner. Used for relationship management planning, contract pipeline tracking, and ensuring continuity of content supply or distribution.']
  corporate_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the corporate hierarchy tree where 1 represents the ultimate parent entity and higher numbers represent deeper subsidiary levels. Used for roll-up reporting and consolidated analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and record age analysis.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by rating agencies such as Moody\'s, S&P, or Fitch. Used for financial risk assessment, contract negotiation, and determining payment terms or guarantees required.']
  distribution_territories varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or markets where the partner operates or holds distribution rights. Stored as comma-separated list of country codes or region identifiers. Critical for rights management and windowing strategy.']
  domicile_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the legal domicile jurisdiction where the partner entity is incorporated or registered. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and tax treaties.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to identify business entities globally. Used for credit assessment, vendor management, and corporate hierarchy tracking.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the physical headquarters address including street number and street name. Used for legal correspondence, contract execution, and regulatory filings.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the physical headquarters address for suite, floor, building name, or other secondary address details.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the partner headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the headquarters location. May differ from domicile_country_code if the operational headquarters is in a different jurisdiction than legal incorporation.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the headquarters address. Format varies by country postal system.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the partner headquarters is located. Format varies by country.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partnership includes exclusivity provisions that restrict either party from engaging with competing partners in specified territories, content categories, or distribution channels. Critical for rights management and competitive strategy.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that performed the most recent modification to this partner record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special considerations, relationship history, or operational notes about the partner that do not fit structured fields. Used by relationship managers for institutional knowledge capture.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the partner onboarding workflow. Tracks progression from initial prospect through due diligence, contract negotiation, legal review, approval, and final onboarding completion.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the partner used in operational systems, contracts, and inter-company communications. Typically assigned during onboarding.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the partner organization as registered with governing authorities. Used for contracts, invoicing, and legal documentation.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the partner based on their role in the media ecosystem. Studios produce original content, syndicators distribute content to multiple outlets, content providers supply licensed material, MVPDs are traditional cable/satellite distributors, vMVPDs are virtual distributors, and OTT aggregators operate streaming platforms.']
  preferred_payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the partner for content licensing, royalties, or service fees. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, advance against royalties, or milestone-based payments. Feeds into accounts payable and cash flow planning.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the main business contact at the partner organization. Used for operational communications, contract notifications, and relationship management.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the partner organization responsible for day-to-day relationship management and operational coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the main business contact at the partner organization. Includes country code and extension where applicable.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this partner was formally terminated or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for historical analysis and transition planning.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this partner was formally established, typically corresponding to the first executed agreement or onboarding completion. Used for relationship tenure analysis and anniversary tracking.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the business relationship. Active indicates ongoing business, pending indicates onboarding in progress, suspended indicates temporary hold, inactive indicates no current agreements, and terminated indicates relationship ended.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Internal risk classification based on financial stability, contract compliance history, operational reliability, and strategic importance. Used for relationship management prioritization and escalation protocols.']
  strategic_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner\'s strategic importance to the business. Tier 1 represents critical strategic partners with significant content volume or distribution reach, Tier 2 represents important but not critical partners, Tier 3 represents transactional relationships, and emerging represents new partners under evaluation.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the partner type. Examples include major studio vs independent studio, first-run syndicator vs off-network syndicator, FAST channel operator, co-production house, or affiliate network.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the partner entity. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in other regions). Used for tax reporting and compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all external business partners including studios, syndicators, content providers, MVPDs, vMVPDs, FAST channel operators, and third-party distribution partners. Serves as the SSOT for partner identity, classification, legal entity details, and relationship status. Tracks partner type (studio, syndicator, MVPD, OTT aggregator, co-production house), corporate hierarchy, domicile jurisdiction, and onboarding lifecycle stage.'
}

Business justification: Acquisition deals for broadcast distribution must verify licensed coverage matches contracted territories. Compliance officers cross-reference deal territories against FCC-licensed footprints to ensur']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Acquisition deals often specify target broadcast channels for content placement (e.g., primetime slot on flagship channel). Critical for content scheduling coordination, deal fulfillment tracking, and']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Acquisition deals specify required content ratings (TV-MA, TV-14, PG, etc.) as contractual obligations. Buyers require sellers to deliver content with appropriate ratings for licensed territories and ']
  coppa_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.coppa_declaration. Business justification: Acquisition deals for children\'s content (directed to kids under 13) require COPPA compliance declarations covering data collection on associated websites, apps, and interactive platforms. Contracts s']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Content acquisition costs are allocated to cost centers for budget management, variance analysis, and content spend tracking. Essential for production vs. acquisition cost analysis and departmental bu']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Acquisition deals are evaluated and priced based on target demographic appeal of the content being acquired. Essential for content acquisition ROI analysis and portfolio strategy.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal business development or acquisitions executive responsible for negotiating and managing this deal.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcaster\'s legal entity that is the contracting party for this acquisition deal, relevant for multi-entity corporate structures.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the studio, syndicator, or independent content provider from whom content is being acquired. Links to partner master data.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Acquisition deals impact specific profit center EBITDA through content amortization and revenue generation. Critical for segment P&L reporting, content ROI analysis, and business unit performance meas']
  source_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Acquisition deals for content rights often originate from identified sales opportunities when proactively pursuing content for the portfolio. Tracks deal pipeline from opportunity identification throu']
  content_delivery_format varchar [note: 'Technical specifications for content delivery including file formats, resolution standards, and delivery methods (e.g., HD 1080p ProRes, 4K HEVC, physical media, digital file transfer).']
  content_package_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the content included in this acquisition deal, including series titles, episode counts, seasons, or film packages.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the executed contract document stored in the document management system.']
  contract_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the acquisition deal contract was formally signed and executed by all parties, marking the transition from negotiation to binding agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this acquisition deal record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this acquisition deal (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  deal_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the acquisition deal terms become effective and rights begin to accrue, which may differ from execution date.']
  deal_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the acquisition deal expires and rights revert to the content provider, marking the end of the license period.']
  deal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the acquisition deal, typically formatted with prefix and sequential number for tracking and reference in contracts and communications.']
  deal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acquisition deal from initial draft through negotiation, execution, and eventual expiration or termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|negotiation|legal_review|approved|executed|active|expired|terminated|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deal_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the acquisition deal, typically reflecting the content package or series being acquired.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deal structure indicating the financial arrangement: flat fee (fixed payment), revenue share (percentage of revenue), minimum guarantee (MG with upside), hybrid (combination), or barter (exchange of services/inventory).']
  deal_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the acquisition deal in the specified currency, representing the gross commitment before adjustments.']
  distribution_rights varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution platforms and methods covered by this deal (e.g., linear, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, FAST, syndication, VOD).']
  episode_count varchar [note: 'Total number of episodes included in the acquisition deal for episodic content such as television series.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the acquisition deal grants exclusive rights within the specified territory, preventing the content provider from licensing to competitors.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of exclusivity terms, including platform restrictions, genre exclusivity, or time-based exclusivity windows.']
  holdback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days during which content must be withheld from certain distribution channels or platforms as part of windowing strategy or exclusivity terms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this acquisition deal record was most recently updated, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  license_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the content license in months, representing the period during which the broadcaster has rights to exploit the acquired content.']
  marketing_materials_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deal includes delivery of marketing and promotional materials such as trailers, key art, press kits, and promotional clips.']
  metadata_requirements varchar [note: 'Specifications for content metadata to be delivered with the content package, including descriptive, technical, and rights metadata standards.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment to the content provider regardless of performance, applicable for minimum guarantee and hybrid deal structures.']
  negotiation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal negotiations for this acquisition deal commenced.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special terms, or contextual information about the acquisition deal not captured in structured fields.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Detailed schedule of payment milestones and amounts, including upfront payments, delivery payments, and ongoing royalty schedules.']
  performance_guarantees varchar [note: 'Contractual performance commitments such as minimum broadcast hours, promotional support, or audience delivery guarantees required by the content provider.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broadcaster has the option to renew or extend the acquisition deal beyond the initial term.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing deal renewal, including notice periods, pricing adjustments, and conditions for exercising renewal options.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue shared with the content provider for revenue share and hybrid deal structures, typically applied after minimum guarantee recoupment.']
  sublicensing_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broadcaster is permitted to sublicense the acquired content to third parties such as MVPDs, vMVPDs, or international distributors.']
  sublicensing_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms and restrictions governing sublicensing rights, including revenue sharing with original content provider and territorial limitations.']
  territory_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or markets covered by this acquisition deal, specified as comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional designations (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, or NORTH_AMERICA).']
  total_runtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregate runtime of all content included in the acquisition deal, measured in hours, used for inventory planning and scheduling.']
  windowing_strategy varchar [note: 'Sequential release strategy defining the order and timing of content availability across different platforms and distribution channels.']

  Note: 'Master record for content acquisition deals negotiated with studios, syndicators, and independent content providers. Captures deal structure (flat fee, revenue share, MG/minimum guarantee), content package scope, exclusivity terms, territory coverage, windowing strategy (SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, linear), and deal status through the full negotiation-to-execution lifecycle. Source of truth for content acquisition commitments feeding into the rights and finance domains.'
}

Table partner.acquisition_deal_line {
  acquisition_deal_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the acquisition deal line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent acquisition deal that contains this line item. Links this line to the master deal agreement.']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Individual titles and episodes in acquisition deal lines have specific rating requirements for the licensed territory and platform. Compliance verification at the line-item level ensures each delivere']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific content title, episode, season, or package being acquired in this line item.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.media_asset. Business justification: Deal lines license specific assets, not just titles. MAM systems must link assets to deal lines for automated rights validation, windowing enforcement, royalty calculation, and compliance reporting in']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.talent_profile. Business justification: Content acquisition deals often include attached talent as a key deal point—star power drives content valuation and marketing strategy. Essential for deal valuation models, marketing rights clearance,']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Age or content rating classification for this content line item (e.g., G, PG, PG-13, R, TV-Y, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA). Determines broadcast daypart and audience targeting.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content being acquired in this line item. Determines rights structure and windowing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: film|series|season|episode|documentary|special|sports_event|news_package|music_video|short_form — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this acquisition deal line record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this acquisition deal line record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this line item.']
  delivery_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the content provider must deliver the content asset for this line item. Critical for production scheduling and playout planning.']
  delivery_format varchar [note: 'Technical format and delivery method for the content asset associated with this line item. Determines ingest and playout requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hd|4k|sd|film|digital_file|tape|satellite|ftp|aspera|physical_media — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of content delivery for this line item. Tracks the fulfillment lifecycle from order to ingest.']
  distribution_rights varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution rights granted for this content line item. May include linear, VOD, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, theatrical, home video, digital download, streaming, broadcast, cable, satellite, OTT, FAST, syndication, etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product and junction table for complex rights matrices]']
  dubbing_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639 language codes for dubbed audio tracks included with this content line item. Supports multi-territory distribution.']
  eidr_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique EIDR identifier for the content in this line item. Global standard for audiovisual content identification.']
  episode_count varchar [note: 'Number of episodes included in this line item if the content type is series or season. Null for single-title content.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rights granted for this line item are exclusive (true) or non-exclusive (false). Impacts windowing and competitive distribution.']
  genre_primary varchar [note: 'Primary genre classification for the content in this line item. Used for programming strategy and audience analytics.']
  holdback_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any holdback periods or exclusivity restrictions that limit when or how this content can be exploited. Includes blackout periods and sequential windowing constraints.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary language of the content in this line item. ISO 639 two or three-letter language code.']
  license_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the license term expressed in months. Calculated from start and end dates for reporting and windowing analysis.']
  license_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base license fee amount for this specific content line item. Represents the core acquisition cost before adjustments.']
  license_term_end_date date [note: 'The date when the license rights for this content line item expire. Marks the end of the rights window.']
  license_term_start_date date [note: 'The date when the license rights for this content line item become effective. Marks the beginning of the rights window.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent acquisition deal. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items within the deal.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this acquisition deal line item. Tracks progression from negotiation through fulfillment and expiration.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum guarantee payment amount for this line item. Represents the floor payment regardless of performance or usage.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this acquisition deal line record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this acquisition deal line record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this acquisition deal line item. Captures special terms, exceptions, or operational instructions.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Payment structure for this line item. Defines when and how license fees and guarantees are paid to the content provider.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice or delivery milestone until payment is due for this line item. Standard commercial payment terms.']
  production_year varchar [note: 'Year the content was originally produced or released. Used for catalog management and rights valuation.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate for revenue-sharing or usage-based royalty payments on this content line item. Applied to calculate residuals beyond minimum guarantee.']
  runs_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of broadcast or exhibition runs permitted for this content line item during the license term. Null indicates unlimited runs.']
  runtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total runtime duration in minutes for the content in this line item. Used for scheduling and inventory management.']
  subtitle_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639 language codes for subtitles or closed captions included with this content line item. Supports accessibility and localization requirements.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market where rights are granted for this content line item. May be country code, region code, or multi-territory identifier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for complex territory hierarchies]']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a content acquisition deal, each representing a specific title, episode, season, or content package with its own financial terms, window schedule, territory rights, and delivery obligations. Enables granular tracking of per-title economics within a multi-title deal and feeds rights windowing and royalty calculation processes.'
}

Table partner.distribution_agreement {
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution agreement. Primary key.']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Distribution agreements for linear broadcast channels reference specific licensed facilities. Retransmission consent agreements, carriage deals, and network distribution contracts all specify call sig']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Distribution agreements specify which channels carry content (e.g., cable carriage agreements, affiliate distribution). Fundamental to carriage fee calculation, must-carry compliance, channel position']
  contracting_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Distribution agreements are executed by specific legal entities for proper revenue recognition, tax jurisdiction determination, and SOX compliance. Essential for carriage fee revenue allocation and in']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Distribution agreements specify which DMAs/markets the partner can distribute content in. Essential for territorial rights management and ensuring distribution partners operate within contracted geogr']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution partner (MVPD, vMVPD, cable operator, satellite provider, OTT aggregator) that is party to this agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the distribution agreement, used in contracts and communications with distribution partners.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution agreement indicating its operational state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|negotiation|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewal_pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution agreement based on the nature of the relationship and regulatory framework (e.g., carriage, retransmission consent, must-carry, syndication, OTT distribution, affiliate).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution agreement received final internal approval before execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive who provided final approval for this distribution agreement.']
  audit_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Media Broadcasting retains the right to audit the distributors subscriber counts, revenue reports, and compliance with agreement terms.']
  blackout_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of geographic or content-based blackout restrictions that apply to this distribution agreement (e.g., sports blackouts, regional exclusions).']
  carriage_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base carriage fee amount per the agreement structure. Interpretation depends on carriage_fee_structure (e.g., per-subscriber monthly rate, annual flat fee).']
  carriage_fee_structure varchar [note: 'Pricing model for carriage fees paid by the distributor to Media Broadcasting (per-subscriber, flat rate, tiered, revenue share, hybrid).']
  channel_positioning_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier or package level where the channels must be positioned in the distributors lineup (basic, expanded basic, digital, premium, sports tier, custom).']
  channels_included varchar [note: 'List or description of Media Broadcasting channels covered by this distribution agreement (e.g., primary network, secondary channels, HD feeds, regional variants).']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed distribution agreement contract document stored in the document management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this agreement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution agreement becomes legally binding and operational.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution agreement terminates or expires. Nullable for evergreen agreements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution agreement record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount from the distributor over the agreement term, regardless of actual subscriber counts or usage.']
  must_carry_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement includes must-carry obligations under FCC regulations requiring the distributor to carry the broadcast signal.']
  negotiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Media Broadcasting executive or team responsible for negotiating this distribution agreement.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which the distributor must remit carriage fees to Media Broadcasting.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Description of renewal terms including automatic renewal provisions, notice periods, and renegotiation triggers.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the distributor must provide subscriber counts, viewership data, and revenue reports to Media Broadcasting.']
  retransmission_consent_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Media Broadcasting has granted retransmission consent allowing the distributor to rebroadcast the signal.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response time in hours for addressing service disruptions or technical issues under the SLA.']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum uptime percentage guaranteed under the Service Level Agreement (SLA) for signal delivery and availability.']
  streaming_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes rights for the distributor to offer live streaming or simulcast of Media Broadcasting channels via OTT platforms.']
  svod_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes rights for the distributor to offer Subscription Video On Demand (SVOD) access as part of a subscription package.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Nullable for active agreements.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the agreement prior to expiration.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of the agreement, if applicable (e.g., breach of contract, mutual agreement, business closure).']
  territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market covered by this distribution agreement. May include country codes, DMA codes, or regional descriptors.']
  tvod_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes rights for the distributor to offer Transactional Video On Demand (TVOD) access where viewers pay per transaction.']
  vod_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes rights for the distributor to offer Video On Demand (VOD) access to Media Broadcasting content.']
  vod_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that content remains available in the distributors VOD library after initial broadcast, if VOD rights are included.']

  Note: 'Formal distribution agreements with MVPDs, vMVPDs, cable operators, satellite providers, and OTT aggregators governing carriage of Media Broadcasting channels and content. Tracks carriage fee structures, must-carry obligations, retransmission consent terms, blackout restrictions, channel positioning commitments, and SLA obligations. Distinct from content acquisition deals — this governs outbound distribution rather than inbound content.'
}

  syndication_fee_structure varchar [note: 'Compensation model governing how the syndication partner pays for content rights.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Conditions and procedures under which either party may terminate the agreement prior to the effective end date.']
  territory_grant varchar [note: 'Geographic territories where the syndication partner is granted broadcast rights. Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional designations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|AUS|DEU|FRA|JPN|BRA|MEX|IND|CHN — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Agreements governing the syndication of Media Broadcasting content to third-party broadcasters, regional stations, and international networks. Captures syndication fee structure, exclusivity windows, holdback periods, territory grants, episode count, run limitations, and clearance obligations. Feeds the scheduling and rights domains for cleared content availability.'
}

  affiliate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the affiliate agreement record. Primary key.']

Business justification: affiliate_agreement.affiliate_station_id represents the affiliate station partner. Renaming to affiliate_station_partner_id for clarity (ends with partner_partner_partner_id pattern). Links to partner']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Network-affiliate agreements are fundamentally tied to the affiliate station\'s FCC broadcast license. The agreement references the station\'s call sign, facility ID, licensed market (DMA), and coverage']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Affiliate agreements are channel-specific contracts between networks and local stations. Core to network-affiliate relationship management, affiliation fee calculation, must-air programming compliance']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Affiliate agreements are market-specific; each affiliate station serves a specific DMA. Essential for network-affiliate relationship management and ensuring proper market coverage. Removes denormalize']
  network_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent network or broadcast network entity that is the counterparty to this affiliate agreement.']
  network_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Affiliate agreements generate intercompany transactions requiring legal entity tracking for network compensation accounting, retransmission revenue split allocation, and consolidation elimination. Cri']
  affiliation_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly or annual fee paid by the network to the affiliate station for carrying network programming, or reverse compensation paid by affiliate to network. Negative values indicate reverse compensation.']
  affiliation_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the affiliation fee amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  affiliation_fee_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which affiliation fees are calculated and paid between network and affiliate.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the affiliate agreement, used in contracts, invoicing, and legal correspondence.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the affiliate agreement indicating its operational state and enforceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the affiliate relationship: primary affiliation (main channel), secondary affiliation, multicast channel, digital subchannel, network-owned station, or independent affiliate.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides notice of non-renewal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this affiliate agreement record was first created in the system.']
  digital_rights_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate agreement includes rights to distribute network content via digital platforms, streaming services, and Over-The-Top (OTT) channels.']

  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the affiliate agreement becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate has exclusive rights to broadcast network programming within its designated territory, preventing the network from contracting with competing stations in the same market.']
  exclusivity_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory within which the affiliate has exclusive rights to broadcast network programming, typically defined by DMA boundaries, county lists, or signal contours.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the affiliate agreement terminates unless renewed or extended. Nullable for evergreen agreements.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law that applies to the interpretation and enforcement of the affiliate agreement (e.g., State of New York, State of California).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this affiliate agreement record was most recently updated or amended.']
  local_ad_avails_minutes_per_hour decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of minutes per hour of network programming that the affiliate may use for local advertising insertion, typically 2-4 minutes per hour.']
  local_insertion_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate has the right to insert local advertising spots during designated network programming breaks (ad pods).']
  minimum_clearance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of network programming hours that the affiliate is contractually obligated to broadcast, typically 85-95% for primary affiliates. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 90.00 for 90%).']
  must_air_programming_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum number of weekly hours of network programming that the affiliate must broadcast without preemption, including prime-time, news, and sports.']
  network_compensation_model varchar [note: 'Economic model governing compensation flow between network and affiliate: fixed fee paid to affiliate, revenue share from advertising, audience-based payments, hybrid model, or reverse compensation where affiliate pays network.']
  performance_measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Audience measurement methodology used to assess affiliate performance against contractual standards (Nielsen ratings, Comscore, proprietary measurement, or multi-source approach).']
  performance_standard_grp_minimum decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Rating Points (GRP) that the affiliate must deliver for network programming to meet performance standards and avoid penalties or compensation adjustments.']
  preemption_notice_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice the affiliate must provide to the network before preempting scheduled programming, typically 24-72 hours except for breaking news.']
  preemption_rights varchar [note: 'Extent to which the affiliate may preempt scheduled network programming for local content, special events, or breaking news. Preemption typically requires advance notice and makegoods.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that either party must provide written notice of intent to terminate or renegotiate, typically 90 to 180 days.']
  retransmission_consent_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate agreement includes provisions for retransmission consent negotiations with Multichannel Video Programming Distributors (MVPDs) and virtual MVPDs (vMVPDs).']
  retransmission_revenue_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of retransmission consent fees collected from MVPDs that the affiliate must share with the network. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 30.00 for 30% to network).']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of advertising revenue or other monetization that is shared between network and affiliate under revenue-sharing compensation models. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the affiliate agreement was executed by authorized signatories from both network and affiliate parties.']
  simulcast_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate is required to simulcast network programming simultaneously across multiple platforms (linear broadcast, digital streaming, mobile apps).']
  term_length_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the affiliate agreement term expressed in months, typically ranging from 12 to 60 months for broadcast affiliations.']
  termination_for_cause_provisions varchar [note: 'Description of conditions under which either party may terminate the agreement for cause, including breach of clearance obligations, payment defaults, license revocation, or material violations of broadcast standards.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required for voluntary termination without cause, typically 90-180 days.']

  Note: 'Affiliate relationship agreements with local broadcast stations, regional affiliates, and network affiliate groups. Tracks affiliation fees, network compensation, programming obligations, local insertion rights, must-air requirements, and affiliate performance standards. Distinct from MVPD distribution agreements — affiliates are broadcast station partners, not pay-TV distributors.'
}
