  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Advertising standards clearance reviews require tracking which employee performed the review for accountability and audit trail. Reviewer contact data should reference employee record.']
  ad_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the advertising creative in seconds, used to verify compliance with time-based advertising standards.']
  alcohol_tobacco_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad creative promotes alcohol or tobacco products, subject to strict regulatory restrictions and daypart limitations.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the advertiser filed an appeal against a rejection or required-edits decision.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process if an appeal was filed against the original clearance decision.']
  children_directed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad creative is directed at children under 13, triggering stricter COPPA and CARU compliance requirements.']
  clearance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or reference number issued upon successful clearance, used for audit trail and affidavit verification.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which the standards clearance decision was finalized and communicated to the advertiser.']
  clearance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the clearance approval expires, requiring re-submission if the ad is to air beyond this date.']
  clearance_type varchar [note: 'Type of clearance review process applied to the ad creative, indicating timing and depth of review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad standards clearance record was first created in the system.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad standards clearance record was last updated, capturing any changes to review status or outcome.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the clearance review, including specific edits requested or special conditions imposed.']

  rejection_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the standards violation leading to rejection or required edits (e.g., misleading claims, inappropriate content, regulatory non-compliance, children\'s advertising violation).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the ad creative was rejected or required edits, citing specific standards violations or regulatory concerns.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision of the standards clearance review indicating whether the ad creative is approved for broadcast, requires modifications, or is rejected.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the standards review process officially began for the submitted ad creative.']
  standards_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory or self-regulatory organization conducting the clearance review (e.g., NAB, CARU, IAB, internal broadcast standards department).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the ad creative was submitted for standards clearance review.']

  substantiation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad creative makes claims requiring documented substantiation (e.g., scientific evidence, test results, endorsements).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended demographic audience for the ad creative, critical for applying age-appropriate content standards and children\'s advertising rules.']

  Note: 'Compliance clearance record for advertising content reviewed against broadcast standards, FCC rules, and industry codes (NAB Code, IAB standards, CARU guidelines for children\'s advertising). Captures ad creative identifier, advertiser, product category, clearance type (pre-clearance, post-air review), standards body, review outcome (approved, approved with edits, rejected), rejection reason, and clearance date. Ensures all aired advertising meets regulatory and self-regulatory standards.'
}

  Note: 'Certification and compliance records for broadcast technical standards including ATSC 3.0, DVB-T2, MPEG-4/HEVC encoding, HLS/MPEG-DASH streaming, SCTE-35 ad insertion markers, and loudness normalization (ATSC A/85, EBU R128). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|under_review|completed|submitted|overdue|deferred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  public_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance obligation requires public disclosure or inclusion in the public inspection file (e.g., FCC OPIF requirements).']

  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The governing body or regulatory authority that mandates this compliance deadline (e.g., FCC for broadcast licensing, GDPR for data privacy, SOX for financial reporting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCC|Ofcom|MPA|ATSC|DVB|Nielsen|IAB|SCTE|ISO|GDPR|CCPA|SOX|PCI DSS|COPPA — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reminder_trigger_date date [note: 'Date when automated reminders or alerts should be sent to the responsible owner to begin preparation. Calculated as deadline_date minus preparation_lead_time_days.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this deadline (e.g., fiscal quarter end, license term end).']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this deadline (e.g., fiscal quarter start, license term start).']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department accountable for managing and completing this compliance obligation (e.g., Legal, Compliance, Engineering, Finance).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level associated with missing this compliance deadline, considering potential penalties, reputational impact, and operational consequences.']
  submission_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number provided by the regulatory body upon successful submission of the compliance deliverable.']

  Note: 'Proactive compliance deadline and milestone tracker consolidating all regulatory filing deadlines, license renewal dates, audit schedules, certification expiries, and reporting periods into a single operational calendar. Captures deadline type, regulatory body, associated obligation, responsible owner, lead time for preparation, reminder triggers, completion status, and escalation path for at-risk deadlines. Serves as the operational heartbeat for compliance teams managing dozens of concurrent regulatory timelines across FCC, Ofcom, GDPR, SOX, and PCI obligations.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_change {
  regulatory_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory change record. Primary key.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory changes require compliance officer ownership for impact assessment and implementation tracking. Officer contact data should reference employee record for accountability.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory changes create, modify, or supersede regulatory obligations. This FK links a regulatory change to the primary obligation it affects, enabling impact tracking and compliance planning.']
  superseded_regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulatory_change (superseded_regulatory_change_id)']
  affected_business_units varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business units, departments, or divisions impacted by the regulatory change (e.g., Broadcasting Operations, Digital Platforms, Legal, Finance).']
  affected_obligations_count varchar [note: 'Number of existing regulatory obligations that are modified, superseded, or created by this change.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number assigned to this regulatory change by the compliance team or regulatory body.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory change: proposed, under review, approved, effective (in force), deferred, or withdrawn.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'The nature of the regulatory change: new rule, amendment to existing rule, repeal, guidance update, interpretation clarification, or technical standard update.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'The date by which the organization must achieve full compliance with the regulatory change.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory change record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official regulatory documentation, Federal Register notice, or issuing body\'s publication page.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory change becomes legally binding and enforceable.']
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the regulatory change, including system modifications, training, and process updates.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory change expires or sunsets, if applicable. Null for permanent changes.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the regulatory change\'s impact on the organization\'s operations, systems, and compliance posture: critical, high, medium, low, or minimal.']
  impact_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing how the regulatory change affects the organization, including operational, technical, and financial implications.']
  implementation_completion_date date [note: 'The date when implementation was completed and the organization achieved full compliance.']
  implementation_plan varchar [note: 'Summary of the plan to implement the regulatory change, including key milestones, resource requirements, and system modifications.']
  implementation_start_date date [note: 'The date when implementation activities for this regulatory change began.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the organization\'s implementation efforts: not started, planning, in progress, testing, completed, or deferred.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or governing body that issued the change (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, European Commission, MPA, ATSC).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction where the regulatory change applies (e.g., USA, GBR, EUR, California).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory change record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum financial penalty or fine that could be imposed for non-compliance with this regulatory change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the regulatory change, implementation challenges, or special considerations.']
  organization_comment_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization submitted formal comments during the public comment period (True/False).']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maximum penalty amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of potential penalties, fines, or sanctions for non-compliance with this regulatory change.']
  public_comment_period_end date [note: 'End date of the public comment period for proposed regulatory changes, if applicable.']
  public_comment_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the public comment period for proposed regulatory changes, if applicable.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory change was officially published or announced by the issuing body.']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'The name or title of the regulation being changed (e.g., FCC Part 73, GDPR Article 5, ATSC 3.0 Standard).']
  regulation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or citation of the regulation (e.g., 47 CFR 73.1201, ISO 14496-10).']
  regulatory_domain varchar [note: 'The compliance domain affected by this change: broadcast licensing, content standards, data privacy, financial reporting, accessibility, technical standards, advertising, or rights management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_licensing|content_standards|data_privacy|financial_reporting|accessibility|technical_standards|advertising|rights_management — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  related_changes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related regulatory change identifiers or reference numbers that are connected to this change.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with non-compliance or delayed implementation of this regulatory change: critical, high, medium, or low.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'The date when the compliance officer formally signed off on the implementation of this regulatory change.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Status of formal sign-off by the compliance officer: pending, approved, rejected, or conditional approval.']
  supersedes_regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the previous regulation that this change supersedes or replaces, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Tracks new, amended, or repealed regulations and their impact on the organization\'s compliance posture. Captures regulation identifier, issuing body, change type (new rule, amendment, repeal, guidance update), effective date, impact assessment, affected business units, affected existing obligations, implementation plan, implementation status, and sign-off by compliance officer. Essential for media companies navigating rapidly evolving digital privacy laws, streaming regulations, and broadcast rule modernization (e.g., FCC ATSC 3.0 mandates, EU Digital Services Act).'
}

Table compliance.facility_compliance_obligation {
  facility_compliance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-obligation compliance tracking record. Primary key.']
  accessibility_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the accessibility obligation being tracked for this facility']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the broadcast facility where this obligation applies']
  compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of content or platform features at this facility currently meeting this accessibility requirement. Moved from accessibility_obligation because compliance percentage is facility-specific.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current state of this specific facility\'s compliance with this specific obligation: compliant, non_compliant, in_progress, exempt, waived, not_applicable. Moved from accessibility_obligation because compliance status varies by facility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility-obligation compliance tracking record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this obligation became applicable to this specific facility (may differ from the obligation\'s general effective_date if facilities were added later or have phased rollout).']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this obligation does not apply to this specific facility or why a waiver was granted for this facility-obligation combination. Moved from accessibility_obligation because exemptions are granted per facility.']
  facility_responsible_person varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person at this facility responsible for ensuring compliance with this specific obligation (may differ from the corporate responsible_team).']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when this facility last conducted an internal review or audit of compliance with this specific obligation. Moved from accessibility_obligation because review schedules are managed per facility.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility-obligation compliance tracking record was most recently modified.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next internal review or audit of this facility\'s compliance with this obligation. Moved from accessibility_obligation because review schedules are facility-specific.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when a granted waiver or exemption expires for this facility and the obligation becomes enforceable. Moved from accessibility_obligation because waivers are facility-specific.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance tracking relationship between accessibility obligations and broadcast facilities. It captures facility-specific compliance status, review schedules, exemptions, and waivers for each accessibility requirement at each physical location. Each record links one accessibility obligation to one broadcast facility with compliance metrics and dates that exist only in the context of this specific facility-obligation pairing.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, each accessibility obligation (closed captioning, audio description, emergency alert accessibility, etc.) applies to multiple physical facilities (studios, NOCs, transmission sites), and each facility must comply with multiple accessibility obligations. Compliance is tracked and managed at the facility level because different facilities have different compliance statuses, review schedules, exemptions, and waiver expiration dates for the same obligation. The compliance team actively manages these facility-obligation pairings as part of regulatory reporting and audit preparation.'
}

Table compliance.facility_compliance {
  facility_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-obligation compliance record. Primary key.']
  broadcast_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the broadcast facility subject to this regulatory obligation']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation being tracked for this facility']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific facility with respect to this specific obligation. Status is facility-specific because different facilities may have different compliance states for the same obligation (e.g., Studio A compliant, Transmission Site B under review).']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this facility is exempt from this regulatory obligation, if applicable. Exemptions are facility-specific based on facility type, jurisdiction, operational scope, or regulatory waivers (e.g., transmission sites exempt from studio-specific obligations).']
  first_compliance_date date [note: 'Date when this facility first achieved compliance with this obligation. Captures the historical start of the compliance relationship for audit trail and reporting purposes.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when compliance with this obligation was last reviewed or assessed at this specific facility. Review dates vary by facility based on local audits, inspections, and facility-specific compliance activities.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or assessment of this obligation at this specific facility. Review schedules may differ by facility based on risk level, past compliance history, and facility type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about facility-specific compliance considerations, remediation actions, inspection findings, or other contextual information relevant to this facility-obligation relationship.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role responsible for ensuring compliance with this obligation at this specific facility. Responsibility is facility-specific - different facility managers or compliance officers may own the same obligation at different locations.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when a regulatory waiver or exemption expires for this facility-obligation combination. Waivers are granted per-facility and have facility-specific expiration dates requiring renewal or compliance action.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between regulatory obligations and broadcast facilities. It captures facility-specific compliance status, review schedules, exemptions, and responsible parties for each obligation at each facility. Each record links one regulatory obligation to one broadcast facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific facility-obligation compliance relationship.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, regulatory obligations apply to multiple facilities based on facility type and jurisdiction (e.g., EAS participation applies to all transmission sites, public inspection file requirements apply to all studios, tower lighting standards apply to all transmission towers), and each facility must comply with multiple obligations simultaneously (e.g., a studio must comply with EAS, public file, accessibility, content standards). Compliance officers actively manage facility-specific compliance status, review schedules, exemptions, and responsible parties for each obligation at each facility - this is an operational compliance tracking process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table compliance.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit']
  follow_up_audit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit (follow_up_audit_id)']
  accessibility_standard varchar [note: 'Accessibility standard or guideline under audit (e.g., WCAG 2.1, Section 508, CVAA closed captioning).']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the audit fieldwork or review was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the audit fieldwork or review commenced.']
  audit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the audit, including auditor fees and internal resource costs.']
  audit_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external audit firm or regulatory body conducting the audit, if applicable.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled recurrence pattern for this audit type.']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'Primary methodology or approach used to conduct the audit.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the audit, used in regulatory filings and correspondence.']
  audit_opinion varchar [note: 'Formal auditor opinion or conclusion issued for financial or SOX audits.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit scope, including systems, processes, and compliance areas covered.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit process.']
  audit_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the audit engagement.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on its regulatory or compliance domain (e.g., FCC licensing, content standards, GDPR privacy, SOX financial).']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Classification of the auditing organization (internal audit team, external audit firm, or regulatory body inspection).']
  compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall compliance assessment or rating assigned by the auditor based on findings.']
  content_rating_system varchar [note: 'Content rating or classification system under audit (e.g., MPA film ratings, TV Parental Guidelines).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first created in the system.']
  critical_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or high-severity findings that require immediate remediation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for audit cost and financial amounts.']
  data_privacy_framework varchar [note: 'Data privacy regulation or framework under audit (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, COPPA for children\'s content).']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'Total number of audit findings, observations, or non-conformances identified during the audit.']
  follow_up_audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit is required to verify remediation of findings.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or standards body that mandates or oversees this audit (e.g., Federal Communications Commission, Office of Communications).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor responsible for conducting and signing off on the audit.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Broadcast license number or regulatory authorization identifier associated with this audit, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes, observations, or additional context recorded by auditors during the audit process.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or governing body standard under which this audit is conducted.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which identified findings must be remediated or corrective actions completed.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation efforts for audit findings.']
  report_file_path varchar [note: 'Storage location or file path reference for the audit report document in the document management system.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal audit report was issued to management or regulatory body.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of records, transactions, or items sampled during the audit, if sampling methodology was used.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for the audit to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the audit to commence.']
  station_call_sign varchar [note: 'FCC-assigned call sign of the broadcast station subject to this audit, if applicable.']
  technical_standard varchar [note: 'Broadcast technical standard or specification under audit (e.g., ATSC 3.0, DVB-T, MPEG-4).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit. Referenced by audit_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key for the employee entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key used across all internal systems to reference this employee.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of this employee\'s direct manager. Used for organizational hierarchy, reporting relationships, and approval workflows. Null for CEO or top-level executives.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department where the employee is assigned. Used for organizational reporting, budget allocation, and workforce planning.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical location where the employee is based. Used for facilities management, real estate planning, and regional workforce analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s date of birth. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, retirement planning, and compliance with labor laws. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person to be contacted in case of employee emergency. Critical for workplace safety and incident response.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact. Primary method for reaching emergency contact in case of workplace incident or employee emergency.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend). Provides context for emergency communications.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique employee number used for HR operations, payroll, and external reporting. Format: two-letter prefix followed by six digits (e.g., MB123456).']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee\'s relationship with Media Broadcasting. Active indicates currently working; other statuses indicate various non-working states.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s employment arrangement. Determines benefits eligibility, work schedule expectations, and contract terms.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Employee\'s self-identified gender. Used for diversity reporting, benefits administration, and compliance with equal employment opportunity regulations.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment with Media Broadcasting. Used for seniority calculations, benefits eligibility, anniversary recognition, and tenure reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  home_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the employee\'s residential address (street number and name). Used for payroll tax calculations, benefits administration, and emergency contact.']
  home_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the employee\'s residential address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field. Used for accurate mail delivery and address verification.']
  home_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the employee\'s residential address. Used for payroll tax calculations, benefits administration, and geographic workforce analytics.']
  home_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the employee\'s residential address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for international payroll, tax compliance, and global workforce analytics.']
  home_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code of the employee\'s residential address. Used for payroll tax calculations, benefits administration, and mail delivery.']
  home_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the employee\'s residential address. Used for payroll tax withholding, benefits eligibility, and regional workforce reporting.']
  hr_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this employee in the source HR system (SAP S/4HANA HR). Used for system integration, data reconciliation, and cross-system reference.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the employee\'s position within the organization. Used for compensation benchmarking, career pathing, and organizational structure analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Entry|Associate|Mid|Senior|Lead|Manager|Director|VP|SVP|C-Level — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title assigned to the employee. Reflects the employee\'s role, level, and function within the organization. Used for organizational charts, job postings, and career progression tracking.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal first name as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax reporting, and official HR records.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal last name (surname/family name) as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax reporting, and official HR records.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal middle name or initial as it appears on government-issued identification documents. May be null if not applicable.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting, payroll, and legal compliance. Format varies by jurisdiction.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the employee\'s citizenship or nationality. Used for work authorization verification, international assignment eligibility, and diversity reporting.']
  personal_email varchar [note: 'Employee\'s personal email address. Used for emergency communications, benefits notifications, and post-employment correspondence. Must be kept current.']
  personal_phone varchar [note: 'Employee\'s personal mobile or home phone number. Used for emergency contact, urgent work communications, and HR notifications.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day work interactions. May differ from legal name. Used in email signatures, internal directories, and workplace communications.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for final payroll processing, benefits termination, and workforce analytics. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for employment termination. Used for turnover analysis, exit interview tracking, and workforce planning. Null for active employees.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  work_authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s work authorization (visa, permit) expires. Null for citizens and permanent residents. Used for compliance tracking and renewal planning. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s legal authorization to work in the country where they are employed. Critical for compliance with immigration and labor laws.']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address assigned to the employee for business communications. Primary contact method for internal and external work-related correspondence.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Corporate phone number assigned to the employee. May be desk phone, mobile, or extension. Used for business communications and internal directory.']

  Note: 'Master identity record for every internal employee at Media Broadcasting — full-time, part-time, and fixed-term staff. Captures legal name, employee number, national ID, date of birth, gender, employment type, hire date, termination date, employment status, work location, home address, personal email, work email, work phone, emergency contact, nationality, visa/work authorization status, and HR system identifier. This is the SSOT for internal workforce identity — distinct from the talent domain which manages on-screen/off-screen creative talent under contract.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit. Primary key for the org_unit product.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical location or facility where this organizational unit is based.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy. Enables traversal of the organizational tree structure from child to parent. Null for top-level units.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual operating budget allocated to this organizational unit for expense management and financial planning.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget amount, supporting multi-currency operations.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'High-level business function or operational domain that this organizational unit supports within the media broadcasting enterprise. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_operations|content_production|digital_platforms|advertising_sales|news|sports|engineering|technology|corporate_services|finance|human_resources — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code in the financial system to which this organizational unit belongs for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting this organizational unit for business inquiries, escalations, or administrative matters.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting this organizational unit for business or operational purposes.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center identifier associated with this organizational unit for expense tracking, budget allocation, and financial reporting in the general ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit ceased or will cease operations. Null for currently active units with no planned closure.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit became or will become operational and active within the enterprise structure.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Specific functional specialization or operational focus of the organizational unit, providing granular classification beyond business area.']
  head_count_actual varchar [note: 'Current actual number of employees assigned to this organizational unit, used for variance analysis against budget.']
  head_count_budget varchar [note: 'Approved budgeted number of full-time equivalent employees allocated to this organizational unit for workforce planning and cost management.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this organizational unit in the enterprise hierarchy tree, with 1 representing top-level divisions and increasing numbers for deeper nesting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representation of the organizational units position in the hierarchy, typically a delimited string of ancestor unit codes for efficient tree traversal.']
  is_broadcast_operations boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit is directly involved in broadcast playout, transmission, or on-air operations.']
  is_content_production boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit is engaged in content creation, production, or post-production activities.']
  is_revenue_generating boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit directly generates revenue through advertising sales, subscription services, or content licensing.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal entity or subsidiary under which this organizational unit operates for regulatory and compliance purposes.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the head or manager of this organizational unit, responsible for its operations and performance.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this organizational unit record, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last modified or updated.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours or shift pattern for this organizational unit, relevant for broadcast operations, production schedules, and workforce planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the organizational unit indicating whether it is actively functioning, temporarily suspended, planned for future activation, or permanently closed.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the organizational units purpose, responsibilities, and scope of operations within the media broadcasting enterprise.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center identifier for units that are managed as revenue-generating or P&L-responsible entities within the enterprise.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary operating location of this organizational unit, used for scheduling and time-sensitive operations.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of labor union or collective bargaining unit that represents employees in this organizational unit, if applicable.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit used in financial systems, reporting, and internal references. Typically alphanumeric and unique across the enterprise.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the organizational unit as used in corporate communications, org charts, and HR systems.']
  unit_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or short name of the organizational unit for display in constrained UI contexts, reports, and dashboards.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit indicating its hierarchical level and functional role within the enterprise structure.']

  Note: 'Master record for every organizational unit within Media Broadcasting — divisions, departments, sub-departments, cost centers, and functional teams. Captures unit code, unit name, unit type (division/department/team), parent unit reference for hierarchy traversal, effective date, head count budget, physical location, and operational status. Supports org chart rendering, headcount reporting, and cost allocation across broadcast operations, production, technology, and corporate functions.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile. Primary key for the job profile reference catalog.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the primary department or organizational unit where this job profile is typically assigned. Used for organizational structure reporting and headcount planning.']
  parent_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_profile (parent_job_profile_id)']
  reports_to_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the job profile of the direct supervisor or manager for this role. Establishes reporting hierarchy and organizational structure.']
  bonus_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is eligible for annual performance bonuses or incentive compensation. Used for total rewards planning and compensation design.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  eeo_job_category varchar [note: 'Standard EEO-1 job category used for federal equal employment opportunity reporting to the EEOC. Required for compliance with Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive_senior_level_officials_and_managers|first_mid_level_officials_and_managers|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|administrative_support_workers|craft_workers|operatives|laborers_and_helpers|service_workers — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile version was superseded or retired. Null for currently active profiles. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile version became effective and available for use in hiring, workforce planning, and HR operations.']
  equity_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is eligible for equity compensation such as stock options, restricted stock units (RSUs), or employee stock purchase plans (ESPP).']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the Fair Labor Standards Act determining overtime eligibility. Exempt employees are salaried and not eligible for overtime; non-exempt employees are eligible for overtime pay.']
  grade_band varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or pay band assigned to this job profile. Links to salary range tables and determines eligibility for benefits, bonuses, and equity compensation.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this job profile within the organization\'s job taxonomy. Used for HR systems integration, payroll processing, and workforce planning.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative describing the role\'s purpose, key responsibilities, deliverables, and scope of work. Used for recruitment, performance management, and role clarity.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational grouping that clusters related roles sharing similar skill sets and career progression paths. Used for talent development, succession planning, and compensation benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|production|content|advertising|distribution|technology|finance|legal|human_resources|operations — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level or seniority grade within the organization\'s career framework. Determines scope of responsibility, decision-making authority, and compensation band eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|intermediate|senior|lead|principal|manager|director|vice_president|executive — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this job profile. Active profiles are available for hiring and workforce planning; inactive or obsolete profiles are retained for historical reference only.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title as it appears on employment contracts, business cards, and organizational charts. Examples: Broadcast Engineer, News Producer, Content Scheduler, Ad Traffic Coordinator, Studio Director.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for this role. Used for candidate screening and workforce planning.']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for this role. Used for candidate qualification and talent acquisition.']
  physical_demands varchar [note: 'Description of physical requirements and working conditions for this role. Examples: ability to lift 50 lbs, prolonged standing, work in outdoor weather conditions, operate heavy equipment. Required for ADA compliance and workplace safety.']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional skills that are desirable but not mandatory. Used to identify high-potential candidates and development opportunities.']
  remote_work_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements. Used for workforce planning, real estate optimization, and talent attraction.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of professional certifications, licenses, or credentials required or preferred for this role. Examples: FCC General Radiotelephone Operator License, Certified Broadcast Networking Technician (CBNT), Society of Broadcast Engineers (SBE) Certification.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of technical, functional, and soft skills required for successful performance in this role. Examples: video editing, broadcast automation systems, Adobe Premiere, Final Cut Pro, project management, stakeholder communication.']
  salary_band_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary for this job profile in USD. Represents the ceiling for this role\'s compensation range.']
  salary_band_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint or target annual base salary for this job profile in USD. Represents the market-competitive rate for fully competent performance.']
  salary_band_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary for this job profile in USD. Used for compensation planning, budgeting, and pay equity analysis.']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary band amounts. Supports multi-currency compensation structures for global operations.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel. Used for candidate expectations, expense budgeting, and work-life balance planning.']
  union_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is eligible for union membership or collective bargaining representation. Critical for labor relations, contract negotiations, and compliance with National Labor Relations Act (NLRA).']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union or collective bargaining unit representing this role, if applicable. Examples: International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers (IBEW), National Association of Broadcast Employees and Technicians (NABET-CWA), Writers Guild of America (WGA).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this job profile. Incremented with each revision to support change management and historical tracking.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Typical work schedule pattern for this role. Critical for broadcast operations requiring 24/7 coverage and shift-based staffing.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all standardized job profiles and role definitions used across Media Broadcasting — broadcast engineer, news producer, content scheduler, ad traffic coordinator, studio director, etc. Captures job code, job title, job family, job level/grade band, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), EEO job category, minimum qualifications, required certifications, salary band reference, and whether the role is union-eligible. Serves as the authoritative job taxonomy for workforce planning, compensation benchmarking, and talent acquisition.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the approved headcount position within Media Broadcasting\'s organizational structure. Primary key.']
  assigned_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Positions like "ESPN2 Scheduler" or "Fox Sports Traffic Coordinator" are often channel-specific. Workforce planning and job descriptions in broadcasting are tied to specific channel operations for res']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that bears the budget for this position. Used for financial planning, headcount cost allocation, and budget tracking.']
  incumbent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee currently occupying this position. Null if the position is vacant. Enables tracking of position-to-person assignments and vacancy analysis.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized job profile that defines the role\'s responsibilities, competencies, and requirements. Links to the job catalog maintained by HR.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location (office, studio, broadcast facility) where this position is based. Used for facilities planning and geographic workforce distribution analysis.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (department, division, or team) to which this position is assigned. Enables hierarchical workforce reporting and cost center allocation.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisory position to which this position reports. Enables construction of organizational reporting hierarchies and span-of-control analysis.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the position was officially approved for funding by the budget committee or executive leadership. Used for budget cycle tracking and headcount authorization audit.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Budget approval status for the position: approved (funded and authorized), frozen (temporarily unfunded), pending (awaiting budget committee review), or rejected (not authorized for funding).']
  budgeted_annual_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'The annual salary amount budgeted for this position in the organization\'s financial plan. Used for headcount cost forecasting and budget variance analysis. Business-confidential.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business need and strategic rationale for creating or maintaining this position. Used for budget approval and workforce planning documentation.']
  created_date date [note: 'The date on which this position record was first created in the HR system. Used for audit trail and position history tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budgeted salary (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Enables multi-currency workforce cost reporting for global operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this position record expires or is retired. Null for open-ended positions. Used for temporal workforce planning and position lifecycle tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this position record becomes effective and the position is officially available for assignment. Used for temporal workforce planning and historical analysis.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on working hours and schedule: full-time (standard hours), part-time (reduced hours), casual (irregular hours), or on-call (as-needed).']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'The full-time equivalent allocation for this position, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.00 for full-time, 0.50 for half-time). Used for headcount budgeting and workforce capacity planning.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the position is exempt from overtime pay requirements under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). True if exempt (salaried professional/managerial), False if non-exempt (hourly/overtime-eligible).']
  is_remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements. True if remote work is permitted, False if on-site presence is required.']
  is_union_position boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this position is covered by a collective bargaining agreement. True if union-represented, False otherwise. Used for labor cost modeling and contract compliance.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category to which the position belongs (e.g., Engineering, Content Production, Sales, Finance, IT). Used for workforce segmentation and career path planning.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level or grade of the position within the organization\'s job architecture (e.g., Entry, Mid, Senior, Manager, Director, Executive). Used for compensation benchmarking and career progression.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'The date on which this position record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the position, used in HR systems, budget planning, and workforce reporting. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by HR.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the position\'s purpose, key responsibilities, and scope. Used for job postings, performance management, and organizational documentation.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position: active (approved and available), vacant (approved but unfilled), filled (occupied by employee), frozen (temporarily suspended), obsolete (retired), or pending-approval (awaiting budget authorization).']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on employment duration and nature: permanent (ongoing), temporary (fixed-term), contract (external contractor slot), seasonal (recurring short-term), project-based (tied to specific initiative), or internship.']
  preferred_qualifications varchar [note: 'Summary of the desired (but not mandatory) education, certifications, experience, and skills that would enhance candidate suitability. Used for recruitment and candidate ranking.']
  required_qualifications varchar [note: 'Summary of the minimum education, certifications, experience, and skills required for the position. Used for recruitment screening and candidate evaluation.']
  requires_security_clearance boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the position requires a security clearance or background check due to access to sensitive content, facilities, or systems. True if clearance required, False otherwise.']
  salary_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to the position, defining the salary range. Used for pay equity analysis and budget planning. Business-confidential.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title for the position as it appears in organizational charts, job postings, and HR records (e.g., Senior Broadcast Engineer, Content Producer, Ad Sales Manager).']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union or collective bargaining unit to which this position belongs, if applicable (e.g., IATSE, SAG-AFTRA, NABET-CWA). Null for non-union positions. Used for labor relations and contract compliance.']
  vacancy_posted_date date [note: 'The date on which the position was posted as a job opening for recruitment. Null if the position has never been vacant or posted. Used for time-to-fill analysis and recruitment metrics.']

  Note: 'Master record for each approved headcount position within Media Broadcasting\'s organizational structure. A position is a budgeted slot within an org unit that may be filled or vacant. Captures position code, position title, org unit, job profile reference, FTE allocation, position type (permanent/temporary/contract), budget status (approved/frozen), filled/vacant status, incumbent employee reference, and effective date range. Enables workforce planning, vacancy tracking, and headcount budget control distinct from the individual employee record.'
}

Table workforce.employment_record {
  employment_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each distinct employment engagement or assignment. Primary key for the employment record.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile or job classification that defines the role, responsibilities, and competencies for this employment engagement.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (department, division, or business unit) where the employee is assigned during this employment period.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific position held in this employment engagement. Links to the position master record.']
  previous_employment_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on employment_record (previous_employment_record_id)']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who holds this employment record. Links to the employee master record.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical work location (office, studio, broadcast facility) where the employee is based during this employment period.']
  action_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the employment action became effective in the HR system. Used for audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  action_processed_by varchar [note: 'The HR administrator or system user who processed and recorded this employment action. Used for audit trails and accountability.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The scheduled end date of the employment contract for fixed-term or project-based engagements. Nullable for permanent or at-will contracts.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'The type of employment contract governing this employment engagement. Indicates whether the contract is permanent, fixed-term, project-based, at-will, or governed by a union agreement.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which this employees compensation and benefits are charged during this employment period. Used for financial accounting and budget allocation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or application from which this employment record originated. Typically references SAP S/4HANA HR or other HR management systems.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this employment engagement or assignment officially ends. Nullable for ongoing employment. Marks the conclusion of this specific employment period.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this employment engagement or assignment officially begins. Marks the start of the employment period for this specific position and organizational assignment.']
  employee_group varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the employee type (e.g., active employees, retirees, external workers). Used for payroll processing and benefits eligibility determination.']
  employee_subgroup varchar [note: 'Detailed classification within the employee group (e.g., salaried, hourly, union, non-union). Determines specific payroll rules and time management policies.']
  employment_action_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason or justification for the employment action. Provides context for why the hire, transfer, promotion, or other action occurred.']
  employment_action_type varchar [note: 'The type of employment action that created this employment record. Indicates whether this is an initial hire, internal transfer, promotion, demotion, secondment, or rehire event.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this employment record at the time of the action. Indicates whether the employment engagement is active, inactive, suspended, terminated, or on leave.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment engagement by work arrangement. Indicates whether the employee is full-time, part-time, contract, temporary, seasonal, or intern.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The full-time equivalency percentage for this employment engagement. Represents the proportion of a full-time workload, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 100.00 for full-time, 50.00 for half-time).']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days of notice required for termination of this employment engagement, as specified in the employment contract or applicable labor law.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'The pay grade or salary band assigned to this employment engagement. Determines compensation range and benefits eligibility.']
  pay_scale_area varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational area that determines the applicable pay scale. Used for regional compensation adjustments and cost-of-living considerations.']
  pay_scale_type varchar [note: 'The type of pay scale or compensation structure applied to this employment engagement. May reference union agreements, executive compensation plans, or standard salary structures.']
  personnel_area varchar [note: 'The personnel area representing the geographic or administrative grouping for HR administration. Used for payroll processing, benefits administration, and compliance reporting.']
  personnel_subarea varchar [note: 'Subdivision of the personnel area providing finer-grained administrative grouping. Used for location-specific HR policies and time management rules.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'The date when the probationary period for this employment engagement ends. Nullable if no probation period applies or if the employee has already completed probation.']
  record_active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employment record is currently active and valid. Used for filtering current vs historical employment engagements in reporting and analytics.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this employment record was first created in the HR system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this employment record was last modified in the HR system. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  remote_work_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for remote or work-from-home arrangements during this employment period. Reflects organizational policy and role requirements.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific labor union to which the employee belongs, if applicable. References collective bargaining agreements and union-specific policies.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union during this employment period. Used for collective bargaining agreement compliance and benefits administration.']
  weekly_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The standard number of hours per week the employee is contracted to work during this employment period. Used for full-time equivalency calculations.']
  work_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the work schedule pattern assigned to the employee during this employment period. Defines working hours, shift patterns, and time allocation.']
  work_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the work schedule. Provides details about working hours, shift patterns, or flexible work arrangements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each distinct employment engagement or assignment for an employee — covering initial hire, internal transfers, promotions, demotions, secondments, and rehires. Each row represents a single employment period with a specific position, org unit, job profile, employment type, and manager. Captures effective start date, effective end date, employment action type, action reason, reporting manager, work schedule, work location, and employment status at time of action. Forms the complete employment history timeline for each employee.'
}

Table workforce.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compensation plan record. Primary key for the compensation plan entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assigned to this compensation plan. Links to the employee master record in the workforce domain.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile or position that this compensation plan is associated with. Used when the plan is defined at the job level rather than individual employee level.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or organizational unit where this compensation plan applies. Used for departmental compensation structures.']
  superseded_compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_plan (superseded_compensation_plan_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan was approved by authorized personnel.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the compensation plan: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized for use), or rejected (not approved).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR manager or executive who approved this compensation plan.']
  base_salary_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual base salary amount for salaried employees. Null for hourly or commission-only plans.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee under this plan is eligible for performance bonuses, annual bonuses, or incentive compensation.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage applied to sales or revenue for commission-based compensation plans. Null if commission not applicable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code for financial accounting and payroll expense allocation. Links compensation costs to organizational budget units.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for compensation amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this compensation plan expires or is superseded by a new plan. Null for open-ended plans currently in effect.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this compensation plan becomes effective and active for the employee or job profile.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'FLSA classification determining overtime eligibility: exempt (not eligible for overtime) or non-exempt (eligible for overtime pay).']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly wage rate for hourly employees. Null for salaried or commission-only plans.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was last updated or modified.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this compensation plan record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the compensation plan, including special terms, conditions, or historical context.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee under this plan is eligible for overtime pay under Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) or equivalent regulations. True for non-exempt employees, False for exempt.']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base hourly rate for overtime hours (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double-time). Null if overtime not applicable.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which compensation is paid to the employee: weekly (52 times/year), bi-weekly (26 times/year), semi-monthly (24 times/year), or monthly (12 times/year).']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade or band assigned to this compensation plan within the organization\'s compensation structure (e.g., \'Grade 5\', \'Band C\', \'Level 3\').']
  pay_step varchar [note: 'Step or increment within the pay grade, typically representing progression based on tenure or performance (e.g., \'Step 1\', \'Step 2\').']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the compensation plan. Externally-known unique code used in HR systems and payroll processing.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the compensation plan (e.g., \'Senior Producer Salary Plan\', \'Broadcast Engineer Hourly Plan\').']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation plan: draft (being designed), active (in effect), suspended (temporarily paused), terminated (ended), or superseded (replaced by newer plan).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of compensation structure: salary (fixed annual), hourly (paid per hour worked), commission (performance-based), hybrid (combination), or contract (fixed-term agreement).']
  shift_differential_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for shift differential pay for working non-standard hours (evening, night, weekend shifts).']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional hourly rate or percentage added for shift differential pay. Null if shift differential not applicable.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of hours per week expected under this compensation plan (e.g., 40.00 for full-time, 20.00 for part-time).']
  target_bonus_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target bonus as a percentage of base salary for bonus-eligible employees. Represents the expected bonus at 100% performance achievement.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining unit governing this compensation plan (e.g., IATSE, SAG-AFTRA, NABET-CWA). Null if non-union.']
  union_scale_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compensation plan is subject to union scale rates and collective bargaining agreement terms.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of work schedule associated with this compensation plan: full-time (standard 40 hours/week), part-time (less than full-time), seasonal (recurring periods), temporary (fixed duration), or on-call (as-needed).']

  Note: 'Master record defining the compensation structure and pay plan assigned to an employee or job profile at Media Broadcasting. Captures pay plan type (salary/hourly/commission), base salary or hourly rate, pay frequency (weekly/bi-weekly/monthly), currency, effective date, pay grade, step within grade, overtime eligibility, shift differential eligibility, and union scale applicability. Distinct from talent compensation structures in the talent domain — this covers internal staff payroll compensation only.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each payroll processing event. Primary key for the payroll record entity.']
  adjusted_payroll_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_record (adjusted_payroll_record_id)']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payroll expense allocation to cost centers is fundamental to media broadcasting financial operations. Enables departmental cost tracking for production units, broadcast operations, news divisions, and']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee receiving this payroll payment. Links to the employee master record in the workforce domain.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee bank account used for direct deposit payment. Null if payment method is not direct deposit.']
  bonus_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Discretionary or performance-based bonus payments included in this payroll cycle.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Physical check number if payment method is check. Null for direct deposit or other payment methods.']
  commission_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sales commission earnings included in this payroll payment, typically for sales or revenue-generating roles.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Typically USD for Media Broadcasting operations.']
  dental_insurance_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee portion of dental insurance premium deducted from payroll.']
  double_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of hours worked that qualify for double-time premium pay, typically for holidays or excessive overtime.']
  federal_income_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for federal income tax based on employee W-4 elections and IRS tax tables.']
  fsa_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Pre-tax contribution to Flexible Spending Account for healthcare or dependent care expenses.']
  garnishment_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Court-ordered or legally mandated wage garnishment for child support, tax levies, or other obligations.']
  gross_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions or withholdings. Sum of all pay components for the period.']
  health_insurance_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee portion of health insurance premium deducted from payroll.']
  hsa_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Pre-tax contribution to Health Savings Account for qualified medical expenses.']
  local_income_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for local or municipal income tax where applicable.']
  medicare_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee portion of Medicare tax withheld under the Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA).']
  net_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Take-home pay after all deductions and withholdings. The amount actually disbursed to the employee.']
  other_deductions decimal(15,2) [note: 'Miscellaneous voluntary or involuntary deductions not categorized elsewhere.']
  other_earnings decimal(15,2) [note: 'Miscellaneous additional earnings not categorized as regular, overtime, bonus, or commission pay.']
  overtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of hours worked beyond the standard work schedule that qualify for overtime premium pay.']
  overtime_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional earnings from overtime hours worked, typically calculated at 1.5x the regular rate.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring schedule on which the employee is paid. Determines the cadence of payroll processing for this employee.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the work period covered by this payroll payment. Defines the end of the earnings period.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the work period covered by this payroll payment. Defines the beginning of the earnings period.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the net pay is disbursed to the employee. The actual payment delivery date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which net pay is delivered to the employee.']
  payroll_run_date date [note: 'The date on which the payroll calculations were executed and this record was generated.']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this payroll record in its lifecycle from calculation through payment.']
  pto_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of paid time off hours used during this pay period, including vacation, sick leave, and personal days.']
  regular_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of standard work hours performed during the pay period at the base rate of pay.']
  regular_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Earnings from regular hours worked at the base hourly or salary rate. Excludes overtime and other premium pay.']
  retirement_401k_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee pre-tax or Roth contribution to 401(k) retirement savings plan.']
  social_security_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee portion of Social Security tax withheld under the Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA).']
  state_income_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for state income tax based on state tax regulations and employee elections.']
  total_deductions decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all tax withholdings and voluntary deductions subtracted from gross pay.']
  vision_insurance_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee portion of vision insurance premium deducted from payroll.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each payroll processing event for an employee covering a specific pay period. Captures pay period start and end dates, gross pay, federal/state/local tax withholdings, FICA contributions, voluntary deductions (401k, health insurance, FSA), net pay, payment method (direct deposit/check), bank account reference, payroll run date, and payroll status. Serves as the authoritative payroll ledger for each employee pay cycle — feeds finance domain for payroll expense posting.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_enrollment {
  benefit_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benefit enrollment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific benefit plan in which the employee is enrolling. Links to the benefit plan master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is enrolling in the benefit plan. Links to the employee master record in the workforce domain.']
  superseded_benefit_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_enrollment (superseded_benefit_enrollment_id)']
  annual_election_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For FSA, HSA, or other contribution-based plans, the total annual amount the employee elected to contribute for the plan year. Expressed in USD. Nullable for non-contribution plans.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the insurance carrier or benefits provider administering this plan (e.g., Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, Fidelity, Vanguard). Used for vendor management and claims coordination.']
  contingent_beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the contingent (secondary) beneficiary who receives benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased or unable to receive benefits. Nullable if no contingent beneficiary designated.']
  contingent_beneficiary_relationship varchar [note: 'The relationship of the contingent beneficiary to the employee. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|domestic_partner|other_relative|friend|estate|trust|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For life insurance and disability plans, the face value or benefit amount of the coverage (e.g., $100,000 life insurance, 60% salary replacement for disability). Expressed in USD or as a percentage. Nullable for non-insurance benefits.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'The level of coverage selected by the employee, determining who is covered under the benefit plan (employee only, employee plus spouse, employee plus children, full family, or employee plus domestic partner).']
